mcqData.text:
Non-cash transaction refers to-

i. Cash discount
ii. Write-off of goodwill
iii. Depreciation

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation:
Non-cash transactions refer to transactions where no physical cash is exchanged. Write-off of goodwill and depreciation are classic examples of invisible or non-cash transactions. While 'cash discount' involves cash adjustment, it is often categorized differently, but in this context, the primary non-cash items are ii and iii.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text:
Depreciation is a/an-

i. Non-cash transaction
ii. Invisible transaction
iii. Internal transaction

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation:
Depreciation is considered a non-cash transaction because no money leaves the business. It is invisible because it's a value reduction, and internal because it happens within the business's own accounts.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Accounting is originally associated with which of the following?
mcqData.explanation:
Accounting is fundamentally rooted in Economics. The principles of resource allocation, value measurement, and economic utility formed the historical basis for accounting systems.

Correct Answer: Economics

mcqData.text: If owner's equity is 60,000 Taka and total assets are 80,000 Taka, what is the amount of liabilities?
mcqData.explanation: Liabilities = Total Assets – Equity = 80,000 – 60,000 = 20,000 Taka.

Read the following stem and answer the question:

On January 1, 2025, Mr. Rana started a business with cash 1,20,000 Taka, machinery 40,000 Taka, and a loan of 20,000 Taka. Other transactions during January were: Purchase of goods 50,000 Taka, sale of goods 80,000 Taka, and withdrawal for personal use 10,000 Taka.

Q. What is the amount of Mr. Rana's initial capital?
mcqData.explanation: Initial Capital = (Cash + Goods from Loan) + Machinery - Loan = (1,20,000 + 20,000) + 40,000 - 20,000 = 1,60,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

On January 1, 2025, Mr. Rana started a business with cash 1,20,000 Taka, machinery 40,000 Taka, and a loan of 20,000 Taka. Other transactions during January were: Purchase of goods 50,000 Taka, sale of goods 80,000 Taka, and withdrawal for personal use 10,000 Taka.

Q. What is the amount of owner's equity at the end of January?
mcqData.explanation: Owner's Equity = Initial Capital - Purchase + Sales - Withdrawal = 1,60,000 - 50,000 + 80,000 - 10,000 = 1,80,000 Taka.

mcqData.text: Which is the correct expanded form of the accounting equation?

Asset = Liability - Capital + Income - Expense - Drawings
Asset = Liability - Capital - Drawings - Expense + Income

mcqData.text: The liabilities of Mr. Imran's business are 20,000 Taka and owner's equity is 30,000 Taka. What is the total amount of assets?
mcqData.explanation: Assets = Liabilities + Equity = 20,000 + 30,000 = 50,000 Taka.

mcqData.text: At the beginning of the accounting year, opening assets were 12,000 Taka and owner's equity was 10,000 Taka. During the year, assets increased by 5,000 Taka and liabilities increased by 2,000 Taka. What will be the amount of closing owner's equity?
mcqData.explanation: Initial: A(12k) = L(2k) + OE(10k). After increase: A(17k) = L(4k) + OE(13k). Closing Owner's Equity = 13,000 Taka.

On March 1, 2024, Mr. Liton started a business with cash 50,000 Taka, goods worth 1,00,000 Taka, and a loan of 50,000 Taka. Other transactions during March were: purchased ornaments for his wife with personal funds 70,000 Taka, paid son's school fees from the business 2,500 Taka, brought additional capital 30,000 Taka.

Q. What is the amount of Mr. Liton's capital on March 1?
mcqData.explanation: Capital = (Cash + Goods from Loan) + Goods - Loan = (50,000 + 50,000) + 1,00,000 - 50,000 = 1,77,500 Taka.

mcqData.text: Which element(s) of the accounting equation (A = L + OE) will be affected by the transaction "Collection of accounts receivable directly by the bank"?
mcqData.explanation: Bank balance (Asset) increases and Accounts Receivable (Asset) decreases. Since both are asset accounts, only the Asset (A) component is affected.

mcqData.text:
When goods are purchased on credit-

i. Assets decrease
ii. Owner's equity decreases
iii. Liabilities increase

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Buying on credit creates a liability (Accounts Payable increases) and is recorded as an expense (Owner's Equity decreases). It does not decrease assets at the time of purchase.

mcqData.text: Prepaid expense is a/an-
mcqData.explanation: Prepaid expenses are considered assets because they represent a benefit that has been paid for but not yet consumed.

mcqData.text: What changes occur in the accounting equation when goods purchased on credit are returned?
mcqData.explanation: Returning goods to a supplier reduces the liability (Accounts Payable) and reduces the assets (Inventory/Goods) that were previously held.

mcqData.text: 'General Reserve' belongs to which category of accounts?
mcqData.explanation: General reserve is a portion of profits kept aside for future needs, which belongs to the owners and is thus part of Owner's Equity.

mcqData.text:
When an asset increases in the accounting equation-

i. Owner's equity may increase
ii. Liabilities may increase
iii. Another asset may decrease

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: An increase in assets can be balanced by an increase in equity (investment), an increase in liability (loan), or a decrease in another asset (cash purchase).

mcqData.text: Which of the following is prepared by the buyer?
mcqData.explanation: A Debit Note is prepared by the buyer when returning goods to inform the seller that their account is being debited.

mcqData.text: Who prepares a debit note?

Accounts payable of 1,00,000 Taka was settled for 98,500 Taka in full settlement. The effect of this transaction on the accounting equation is-

i. Assets will decrease
ii. Liabilities will decrease
iii. Owner's equity will increase

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Paying 98,500 for a 1,00,000 debt: (i) Cash (A) decreases by 98,500, (ii) Accounts Payable (L) decreases by 1,00,000, and (iii) the 1,500 discount is income, which increases Owner's Equity (OE).

mcqData.text: Which is the correct expanded form of the accounting equation?
options: Asset = Liability - Capital + Expense + Drawings - Income
Asset = Liability + Capital - Income + Expense - Drawings
Asset = Liability + Capital + Income - Expense - Drawings
Asset = Liability - Capital + Drawings + Income - Expense
mcqData.explanation: The standard expanded equation is: Asset = Liability + Capital + Revenue - Expense - Drawings.

mcqData.text: What is the effect of a credit purchase of 2,000 Taka on the accounting equation?
mcqData.explanation: Credit purchase increases Accounts Payable (Liability) and, as an expense, it reduces Owner's Equity.

mcqData.text: When does owner's equity increase?
mcqData.explanation: Revenue and profits increase the owner's interest in the business, thereby increasing Owner's Equity.

mcqData.text: Which was developed to make financial statements universally acceptable?
mcqData.explanation: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) provide the standard framework and guidelines for financial accounting.

mcqData.text: Accounting is a/an-
mcqData.explanation: Accounting is technically an Information System that identifies, records, and communicates economic events.

mcqData.text: 'Prepaid Insurance' belongs to which category of accounts in the modern method?
mcqData.explanation: Prepaid Insurance represents a future benefit and is classified as an Asset.

mcqData.text:
On January 1, 2023, Mr. Fahim started a business with cash 3,00,000 Taka, goods worth 1,00,000 Taka, and a loan of 60,000 Taka.

Q. Which components of the accounting equation will be affected by taking the 60,000 Taka loan?
mcqData.explanation: Taking a loan increases Assets (Cash) and increases Liabilities (Loan). Therefore, A and L are affected.

mcqData.text:
On January 1, 2023, Mr. Fahim started a business with cash 3,00,000 Taka, goods worth 1,00,000 Taka, and a loan of 60,000 Taka.

Q. What is the amount of Mr. Fahim's capital?
mcqData.explanation: Capital = Cash + Goods = 3,00,000 + 1,00,000 = 4,00,000 Taka. The loan (60,000) is a liability and is not part of the owner's capital.

mcqData.text: When unearned service revenue is earned-
mcqData.explanation: Unearned revenue is initially recorded as a liability. Once it is earned, the liability decreases and revenue increases.

mcqData.text: Which expense is recorded as an asset?
mcqData.explanation: Installation expenses are capitalized (added to the cost of the asset) rather than being treated as a current period expense.

mcqData.text: Interest on drawings is a business's-
mcqData.explanation: Since the owner pays interest to the business for personal withdrawals, it is considered Income for the business entity.

fullCQData.stem:
On January 1, 2024, Mr. Parvez started a business with cash 80,000 Taka, furniture 40,000 Taka, and a loan of 50,000 Taka from Mita. The transactions for that month are as follows:

  • Jan. 5: Goods purchased for cash including VAT 57,500 Taka.
  • Jan. 10: Goods sold to Rafiq for cash 60,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 12: Return inward (Sales return) 5,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 15: Commission received 7,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 20: Machinery purchased for cash 20,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 25: Goods purchased from Faruq 10,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 28: Rent paid 8,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 30: Paid to Faruq 9,000 Taka in full settlement.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Determine the amount of capital at the beginning of the business.
questions.answer:
Initial Capital Calculation:

Cash (Owner's own)80,000
Furniture40,000
Total Initial Capital1,20,000 Taka

Note: Bank loan (50,000) is a liability and not part of the owner's initial capital.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Journalize the transactions of dates 5, 12, 15 and 30.
questions.answer:
Journal Entries:

DateAccounts & ExplanationDrCr
Jan 5Purchase A/C Dr
VAT Current A/C Dr
  Cash A/C Cr		50,000
7,500
57,500
Jan 12Sales Return A/C Dr
  Cash A/C Cr		5,0005,000
Jan 15Cash A/C Dr
  Commission Income A/C Cr		7,0007,000
Jan 30Accounts Payable (Faruq) A/C Dr
  Cash A/C Cr
  Discount Received A/C Cr		10,0009,000
1,000

questions.label: c
questions.text: Show the effect of the transactions of dates 10, 20, 25 and 28 on the accounting equation.
questions.answer:
• Jan 10: Cash increased (A+) 60,000; Owner's Equity increased (OE+) 60,000.
  • Jan 20: Machinery increased (A+) 20,000; Cash decreased (A-) 20,000.
  • Jan 25: Inventory (Equity) decreased (OE-) 10,000; Accounts Payable increased (L+) 10,000.
  • Jan 28: Cash decreased (A-) 8,000; Owner's Equity decreased (OE-) 8,000.

fullCQData.stem:
Mr. Shamim started a legal business on January 1, 2025, with cash 1,00,000 Taka and a computer worth 50,000 Taka. The other events of his business during that month were as follows:

  • Jan. 2: Appointed a manager at a monthly salary of 15,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 8: Furniture purchased on credit 30,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 12: Legal services provided to clients for cash 25,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 18: Legal services provided to clients on credit 30,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 20: Loan taken from bank for personal needs 50,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 30: Manager's salary for the current month was paid.
  • Jan. 30: Price of furniture purchased on credit was paid 10,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 31: Office rent for the month of January was paid 12,000 Taka.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Identify the events which are not business transactions and determine their total value.
questions.answer:
Non-Business Events:

1. Jan 2: Appointment of a manager (No financial change yet) - 15,000
2. Jan 20: Personal bank loan (Personal affair, not business) - 50,000
Total: 65,000 Taka

questions.label: b
questions.text: Determine the amount of Mr. Shamim's owner's equity at the end of January 2025.
questions.answer:
Closing Owner's Equity Calculation:

Initial Capital: 1,50,000

(+) Revenues: 25,000 (Cash) + 30,000 (Credit) = 55,000

(-) Expenses: 15,000 (Salary) + 12,000 (Rent) = 27,000

Closing Equity = 1,78,000 Taka

questions.label: c
questions.text: Show the effect of transactions on the accounting equation in a tabular format.
questions.answer:
Summary of Tabular Analysis:

  • Jan 1: Cash +100k, Equipment +50k = Equity +150k
  • Jan 8: Furniture +30k = Accounts Payable +30k
  • Jan 12: Cash +25k = Equity +25k
  • Jan 18: Accounts Receivable +30k = Equity +30k
  • Jan 30: Cash -15k = Equity -15k
  • Jan 30: Cash -10k = Accounts Payable -10k
  • Jan 31: Cash -12k = Equity -12k

fullCQData.stem:
Mr. Nazrul started a business on January 1, 2025, with cash 50,000 Taka, furniture 60,000 Taka, and a cash loan of 30,000 Taka. The transactions of his organization for that month are as follows:

  • June 2: Purchased machinery for 50,000 Taka, paid 30,000 Taka in cash, and issued a note for the remaining amount.
  • June 5: Goods purchased for cash including 15% VAT 34,500 Taka.
  • June 8: Goods sold for cash 15,000 Taka and on credit 10,000 Taka.
  • June 12: An account was opened in the bank with cash 5,000 Taka.
  • June 15: Commission received in cash 7,500 Taka.
  • June 25: Cash 4,800 Taka received in full settlement of a receivable of 5,000 Taka from a debtor.
  • June 30: Goods withdrawn by the owner for personal use at selling price 3,000 Taka.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Determine the amount of initial capital.
questions.answer:
Initial Capital = Cash (50,000) + Furniture (60,000) = 1,10,000 Taka.

Note: The 30,000 loan is a liability and does not increase capital.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Show the effect of the transactions of dates 1, 8, 12 and 30 on the accounting equation.
questions.answer:
• Jan 1: Cash +80k, Furniture +60k = Loan +30k, Equity +110k
  • June 8: Cash +15k, Debtors +10k = Equity +25k
  • June 12: Cash -5k, Bank +5k (No Equity effect)
  • June 30: Inventory (Equity) -3k = Equity -3k (Drawings)

questions.label: c
questions.text: Provide journal entries for the transactions of dates 2, 5, 15 and 25. (Without explanation)
questions.answer:
1. June 2: Machinery A/C Dr 50,000; Cash A/C Cr 30,000, Notes Payable A/C Cr 20,000

2. June 5: Purchase A/C Dr 30,000, VAT A/C Dr 4,500; Cash A/C Cr 34,500

3. June 15: Cash A/C Dr 7,500; Commission Income Cr 7,500

4. June 25: Cash A/C Dr 4,800, Discount Allowed Dr 200; Accounts Receivable Cr 5,000

fullCQData.stem:

  • Jan. 4: Owner's income tax paid 10,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 8: Bad debt was charged 1,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 10: Salary paid to office staff 20,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 12: A computer was purchased for the office 80,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 20: Goods withdrawn for personal use 10,000 Taka.
  • Jan. 25: A motor vehicle was purchased for business use 2,00,000 Taka.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Determine the amount of Mr. Samiron's drawings.
questions.answer: Income Tax (Personal) + Goods Withdrawal = 10,000 + 10,000 = 20,000 Taka

questions.label: b
questions.text: Prepare journal entries with the transactions of dates 2, 4, 8 and 25.
questions.answer:
Jan 2: Purchase Dr, Cash Cr (Net: 19,600)

Jan 4: Drawings Dr, Cash Cr (10,000)

Jan 8: Bad Debt Dr, Accounts Receivable Cr (1,000)

Jan 25: Motor Vehicle Dr, Cash Cr (2,00,000)

questions.label: c
questions.text: Show the effect of the transactions of dates 1, 10, 12 and 20 on the accounting equation.
questions.answer:
1. Jan 1: Cash +3.6L = Loan +1.6L, Equity +2L
2. Jan 10: Cash -20k = Equity -20k
3. Jan 12: Cash -80k, Equipment +80k (No Equity effect)

fullCQData.stem:

fullCQData.stem:
Mr. Umama Atif is a businessman. He started a business on June 1, 2020, with cash 2,00,000 Taka. The transactions completed in his business in the month of June are as follows:

  • June 3: Goods purchased on credit from Salsabil Traders 80,000 Taka.
  • June 7: Goods sold for cash 60,000 Taka (cost price 40,000 Taka).
  • June 10: Sold on credit to Safin Enterprise 90,000 Taka (cost price 65,000 Taka).
  • June 20: Goods returned to Salsabil Traders 2,000 Taka.
  • June 25: Paid three-fourths to Salsabil Traders subject to a 3% discount.
  • June 30: Collected from Safin Enterprise after a 4% discount 19,200 Taka.
  • June 30: Amount of bad debts 1,000 Taka.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Determine the amount of net purchases and net sales for Mr. Umama Atif in the month of June.
questions.answer:
Net Purchase = 80,000 - 2,000 = 78,000 Taka
Net Sales = 60,000 + 90,000 = 1,50,000 Taka

questions.label: b
questions.text: Prepare a tabular summary of the transactions.
questions.answer:
Summary of Tabular Analysis:

  • June 3: Inventory (Equity) -80k, A/P +80k
  • June 7: Cash +60k, Equity +60k; Inventory (Equity) -40k, Inventory +40k
  • June 25: Cash -56,745, A/P -58,500, Equity (Discount) +1,755

questions.label: c
questions.text: Prepare an income statement for Mr. Umama Atif at the end of June.
questions.answer:
Sales: 1,50,000

(-) COGS: 40,000 + 65,000 = (1,05,000)

Gross Profit: 45,000

(+) Discount Received: 1,755

(-) Expenses (Bad debt 1k + Disc allowed 800): (1,800)

Net Profit = 44,955 Taka

fullCQData.stem:

questions.answer: Furniture Cash Portion (20%) = 30,000 × 0.20 = 6,000 Taka

questions.answer: Equity = 1,00,000 (Initial) + 80,000 (Sales) - 20,000 (Purchase) - 30,000 (Credit Purchase) - 5,000 (Stationery) - 4,000 (Drawings) = 1,21,000 Taka

questions.answer: Total Assets = Total Liabilities + Equity = 2,04,000 Taka

mcqData.text: Received 5,400 TK from Hasan Brothers after 10% discount. What is the discount amount?
mcqData.explanation: Total was 6,000 TK. 6,000 TK x 10% = 600 TK.

mcqData.text: What does "Credit" signify in accounting?
mcqData.explanation: Modern rule: Assets decrease = Credit

mcqData.text: Which is the ultimate destination (final resting place) of a transaction?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger is the "Permanent Book" or final destination.

mcqData.text: Which step of the Accounting Cycle is optional?
mcqData.explanation: Asset Worksheet is a tool for convenience and is not a mandatory step.

mcqData.text: What is the main purpose of a Ledger?
mcqData.explanation: Ledgers provide a permanent, classified record of all transactions.

mcqData.text: What can be known from the Sales Journal? i. Total credit sales amount ii. Total sales amount iii. Total accounts receivable amount
mcqData.explanation: The Sales Journal only records credit sales of merchandise. Therefore, it shows the total credit sales (i) and the total increase in accounts receivable (iii). It does not show total sales because cash sales are recorded in the Cash Receipt Journal.

mcqData.text: Parul Traders buys goods for 1,00,000 TK at 10% discount. Asset week later, they Payable half with a 10% cash discount. Discount amount?
mcqData.explanation: Net purchase: 90,000 TK. Half: 45,000 TK. Cash discount: 45,000 TK x 10% = 4,500 TK.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) After paying half, how much liability remains?
mcqData.explanation: Total debt was 90,000 TK. 45,000 TK was settled. Remaining is 45,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Which account always shows a Debit Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Assets and Expenses maintain a natural Debit balance.

mcqData.text: 6% Debentures of 2,00,000 TK issued to Mr. Jahir. What is the Journal entry?
mcqData.explanation: Issuing debentures brings in cash and creates a long-term liability.

mcqData.text: Which of the following shows a Debit Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Debtors is an Asset, therefore it shows a Debit balance.

mcqData.text: What is the permanent storehouse of all transactions?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger is the "King of Books" where data is stored permanently.

mcqData.text: Mr. Abed sold goods for 40,000 TK at 10% trade discount. Later, 2/3rd of the money was received at a 2% cash discount. Discount amount?
mcqData.explanation: Net Sale: 36,000 TK. 2/3rd is 24,000 TK. Cash Discount: 24,000 TK x 2% = 480.

mcqData.text: Based on the previous data, how will the transaction on the 9th be recorded in the Cash Book? i. Debit side cash column; ii. Debit side bank column; iii. Debit side discount column.
mcqData.explanation: Cash received is recorded in the debit cash column, and the allowed discount is recorded in the debit discount column.

mcqData.text: If a deposited check is dishonored/rejected, where is it written in the Cash Book?
mcqData.explanation: To reverse the previous entry, the Bank column must be credited.

mcqData.text: What kind of book is the Journal called?
mcqData.explanation: Journals are the "Primary" or "Original" books of entry.

mcqData.text: If a computer is purchased for the purpose of "Resale," which account is debited?
mcqData.explanation: If bought for resale, it is treated as "Goods," so the Purchase Account is debited.

mcqData.text: Which language does the word "Ledger" originate from?
mcqData.explanation: The word Ledger is derived from the English word 'Legger' (to lie/remain).

mcqData.text: What does a Credit Balance usually indicate?
mcqData.explanation: In modern accounting, Income, Liability, and Equity have Credit balances.

mcqData.text: Sonal Trading buys 12,000 TK goods at 10% discount. Paid on time with a 5% cash discount. Discount amount?
mcqData.explanation: Net: 10,800 TK. Cash Discount: 10,800 TK x 5% = 540.

mcqData.text: What type of discount is offered at the time of final debt settlement?
mcqData.explanation: Cash discounts are used specifically to encourage early or final settlement of debt.

mcqData.text: Another name for Trade Discount is: i. Purchase Discount; ii. Allowed Discount; iii. Sales Discount.
mcqData.explanation: Trade discount is categorized as Purchase Discount for the buyer and Sales Discount for the seller.

mcqData.text: Which of these transactions is recorded in a Cash Book?
mcqData.explanation: Only cash or bank movements (like bringing in Capital) enter the Cash Book.

mcqData.text: The owner withdraws goods for personal use at "Sales Price." What is the Journal?
mcqData.explanation: When goods are taken at sales price, it is treated as a sale to the owner.

mcqData.text: Which step is used to verify the arithmetical accuracy of the Ledger?
mcqData.explanation: The Trial Balance summarizes all balances to check if Debits equal Credits.

mcqData.text: Which transaction requires a "Contra (C)" entry?
mcqData.explanation: Withdrawing from Bank for Office/Business use involves both Cash and Bank.

mcqData.text: Bought goods for 40,000 TK at 25% trade discount. Terms: 4/10, n/30. Paid within 10 days?
mcqData.explanation: Net: 30,000 TK. Cash discount: 30,000 TK x 4% = 1,200 TK. Paid: 30,000 TK - 1,200 TK = 28,800 TK.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) If payment is made after 20 days, how much is paid?
mcqData.explanation: The 4% discount is only valid for 10 days. After that, the full net price (30,000 TK) is paid.

mcqData.text: Furniture purchased for 69,000 TK including 15% VAT. Transport cost 1,000 TK. Total Cost?
mcqData.explanation: Net Furniture: 60,000 TK (after removing VAT). Total Cost = 60,000 TK + 1,000 TK transport = 61,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Which is considered the "Permanent or Final" book of accounts?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger is the ultimate and permanent record of all transactions.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Internal Transaction?
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation happens within the business without an outside party.

mcqData.text: What is the main objective of preparing a Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Trial Balance is prepared specifically to check if Debit and Credit totals match.

mcqData.text: Bought goods for 5,000 TK and paid by check. Which account is credited?
mcqData.explanation: Since payment is made by check, the Bank account (Asset) decreases (Credit).

mcqData.text: Which book is known as the "Subsidiary Book of original entry"?
mcqData.explanation: Journal is the first place a transaction is recorded.

mcqData.text: Goods destroyed by fire 2,000 TK. Which account is credited?
mcqData.explanation: When goods are lost/destroyed, the Purchase account is reduced (Credit).

mcqData.text: Asset 2-column Cash Book records which of the following?
mcqData.explanation: Asset double-column cash book has columns for both Cash and Bank.

mcqData.text: Which discount is NOT recorded in the books of accounts?
mcqData.explanation: Trade discount is deducted from the list price and never enters the ledger.

mcqData.text: Owner brings a personal car into the business. Which account is credited?
mcqData.explanation: Anything the owner brings into the business increases Capital (Credit).

mcqData.text: What is the source document for Sales Returns?
mcqData.explanation: Asset Credit Note is sent to the customer to acknowledge the return.

mcqData.text: Which of these is a Nominal Account?
mcqData.explanation: Nominal accounts represent Expenses (like Rent) or Incomes.

mcqData.text: What is the formula for Owner's Equity?
mcqData.explanation: Assets minus Liabilities equals Owner's Equity (Capital).

mcqData.text: Salary paid 5,000 TK but 1,000 TK is still due. Total Salary Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Under accrual basis: 5,000 TK (Paid) + 1,000 TK (Due) = 6,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Prepaid Insurance is what type of account?
mcqData.explanation: Prepaid expenses provide future benefits, so they are Assets.

mcqData.text: Cash sales 10,000 TK. Impact on Equation?
mcqData.explanation: Cash (Asset) increases and Revenue increases Equity.

mcqData.text: Which voucher is used for Credit Purchases?
mcqData.explanation: Credit Vouchers are used for recording all types of income and credit purchases.

mcqData.text: Asset Contra Entry is required for:
mcqData.explanation: Depositing cash into the bank involves two internal accounts (Cash/Bank).

mcqData.text: What is the "Folio" column in the Ledger used for?
mcqData.explanation: L.F. (Ledger Folio) in the Journal refers to the Ledger page number.

mcqData.text: Bad Debt is what kind of loss?
mcqData.explanation: Bad debt is a regular business expense/loss (Revenue Loss).

mcqData.text: Purchased goods for 8,000 TK. Paid 3,000 TK cash, rest on credit. Creditor amount?
mcqData.explanation: Total 8,000 TK - 3,000 TK (Paid) = 5,000 TK (Due to Creditor).

mcqData.text: Asset business uses the Imprest System for Petty Cash. The start balance was 1,000 TK. 800 was spent. How much will be reimbursed?
mcqData.explanation: Under the Imprest system, the exact amount spent (800) is reimbursed to reach the original limit.

mcqData.text: Which special journal is used to record credit sales?
mcqData.explanation: Credit sales of goods are recorded in the Sales Journal.

mcqData.text: Which event is recorded in the General Journal?
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation is an adjusting entry recorded in the General Journal.

mcqData.text: Opening Debtors 30k, Closing 50k. Credit sales 1.2L. How much cash was received from debtors?
mcqData.explanation: Cash = Opening + Credit Sales - Closing = 30,000 TK + 1,20,000 TK - 50,000 TK = 1,00,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Probal Traders sold goods worth 50,000 TK at 5% discount on credit. On July 10, half the amount was received at 2% discount. What is the impact of the discount? i. Profit dec 475; ii. Assets dec 475; iii. Equity dec 475.
mcqData.explanation: Net Receivable = 47,500 TK. Half is 23,750 TK. Cash Discount = 23,750 TK * 2% = 475. Since it's an expense, it reduces profit, assets (receivable), and equity.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) How much cash was received for that half payment?
What is called the "Ultimate Destination" of a transaction?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger is the final destination for all accounting entries.

mcqData.text: Machine installation wages should be debited to which account?
mcqData.explanation: Cost to make an asset usable (installation) is debited to the Asset (Machine) account.

mcqData.text: Received 54,000 TK after a 10% discount. What is the discount amount?
What does "Debit" signify?
mcqData.explanation: Debit means an increase in Assets or a decrease in Liabilities/Equity.

mcqData.text: Mr. Hasan spent 5,000 TK for advertising his furniture business. What is the journal entry? i. Furniture Account-Debit; ii. Advertising Expense Account-Debit; iii. Cash Account-Credit.
mcqData.explanation: Promoting a business is a revenue expense. The entry is Advertising Expense Debit. and Cash Credit. Furniture is not debited as it wasn't purchased for use.

mcqData.text: What does "1/10, net/45" mean? i. 1% discount if paid within 10 days; ii. 1% discount if paid within 45 days; iii. Must Payable within 45 days.
mcqData.explanation: 1/10 means a 1% discount is available within 10 days. Net/45 indicates the total credit period is 45 days.

mcqData.text: Which is known as the "Storehouse" of transactions?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger stores all classified transaction data.

mcqData.text: Which cash book never has a Credit Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Asset single column (cash only) book can never Payable more than it has.

mcqData.text: Bought 20,000 TK goods at 10% disc. Paid via check at 5% cash disc. Purchase Debit amount?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase is recorded at net trade price: 20,000 TK - 10% = 18,000 TK.

mcqData.text: The Ledger is called: i. Primary book; ii. Permanent storehouse; iii. Final book.
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger is a permanent and final book of accounts. The Journal is the primary book.

mcqData.text: Which discount is deducted from the list price during purchase/sale?
mcqData.explanation: Trade discount is deducted from the catalog/list price at the time of the deal.

mcqData.text: Received 2,700 TK from Abir at 10% discount. Discount amount?
Which of the following are Contra Entries? i. Personal withdrawal from bank; ii. Deposit into bank; iii. Bank withdrawal for office use.
mcqData.explanation: Contra entries involve both Cash and Bank accounts within the business. Personal withdrawals are treated as Drawings.

mcqData.text: Bought 20k goods (10% disc). Paid via check (5% cash disc). Bank account Credit amount?
In which step of the Accounting Cycle is a transaction identified?
mcqData.explanation: Identification of a transaction is the very first step of the cycle.

mcqData.text: If furniture is purchased for "Resale," which account is debited?
mcqData.explanation: Items bought for resale are considered "Goods," so they are debited to the Purchase account.

mcqData.text: What type of discount is it when a creditor waives a portion of the total debt?
mcqData.explanation: Discount received occurs during the settlement of a liability to a creditor.

mcqData.text:
Mr. Samiran's Cash Payment Journal for the month of June 2021 was as follows: Date 2021 | Debit (TK) | Reference | Debit (TK) | Debit (TK) | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) | Credit (TK) June 2 | Purchase | | 5,000 TK | | | 5,000 TK | June 17 | Accounts Payable - Himu | | | 8,000 TK | | 8,000 TK | June 19 | Accounts Payable - Sohag | | | 4,100 TK | | 4,000 TK | 100 June 30 | Purchase | | 15,000 TK | | | 15,000 TK | Total | | | 20,000 TK | 12,100 TK | 0 | 32,000 TK | 100 The total of Mr. Samiran's Purchase Journal for the month of June was 30,000 TK Q: What type of journal is used in the stimulus?

Date 2021Debit (TK)ReferenceDebit (TK)Debit (TK)Debit (TK)Credit (TK)Credit (TK)
June 2Purchase5,000 TK5,000 TK
June 17Accounts Payable - Himu8,000 TK8,000 TK
June 19Accounts Payable - Sohag4,100 TK4,000 TK100
June 30Purchase15,000 TK15,000 TK
Total20,000 TK12,100 TK032,000 TK100
mcqData.explanation: Cash Payment, Cash Receipt, Sales, and Purchase journals are all 'Special Journals'.

mcqData.text:
Mr. Samiran's Cash Payment Journal for the month of June 2021 was as follows: Date 2021 | Debit (TK) | Reference | Debit (TK) | Debit (TK) | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) | Credit (TK) June 2 | Purchase | | 5,000 TK | | | 5,000 TK | June 17 | Accounts Payable - Himu | | | 8,000 TK | | 8,000 TK | June 19 | Accounts Payable - Sohag | | | 4,100 TK | | 4,000 TK | 100 June 30 | Purchase | | 15,000 TK | | | 15,000 TK | Total | | | 20,000 TK | 12,100 TK | 0 | 32,000 TK | 100 The total of Mr. Samiran's Purchase Journal for the month of June was 30,000 TK Q: What is the amount of Total Purchase for Mr. Samiran in the month of June 2021?

Date 2021Debit (TK)ReferenceDebit (TK)Debit (TK)Debit (TK)Credit (TK)Credit (TK)
June 2Purchase5,000 TK5,000 TK
June 17Accounts Payable - Himu8,000 TK8,000 TK
June 19Accounts Payable - Sohag4,100 TK4,000 TK100
June 30Purchase15,000 TK15,000 TK
Total20,000 TK12,100 TK032,000 TK100
mcqData.explanation: Total Purchase = Cash Purchase (20,000 TK) + Credit Purchase (30,000 TK) = 50,000 TK.

mcqData.text:
Asset partial view of Manisha Enterprise's Bank Account for the month of June 2022 is given below: Date 2022 | Particulars | Journal Folio | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) June 01 | Balance | | | | | 4,000 TK June 09 | Sales | | 50,000 TK | | | June 20 | Purchase | | | 30,000 TK | | June 30 | Bank Charge | | | 1,000 TK | | Q: What does the balance of the Bank Account on June 01, 2022, represent for the organization?

Date 2022ParticularsJournal FolioDebit (TK)Credit (TK)Debit (TK)Credit (TK)
June 01Balance4,000 TK
June 09Sales50,000 TK
June 20Purchase30,000 TK
June 30Bank Charge1,000 TK
mcqData.explanation: Asset Credit balance in a Bank account (Ledger) means a Bank Overdraft, which is a Liability.

mcqData.text:
Asset partial view of Manisha Enterprise's Bank Account for the month of June 2022 is given below: Date 2022 | Particulars | Journal Folio | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) June 01 | Balance | | | | | 4,000 TK June 09 | Sales | | 50,000 TK | | | June 20 | Purchase | | | 30,000 TK | | June 30 | Bank Charge | | | 1,000 TK | | Q: What is the balance of the Bank Account on June 30, 2022?

Date 2022ParticularsJournal FolioDebit (TK)Credit (TK)Debit (TK)Credit (TK)
June 01Balance4,000 TK
June 09Sales50,000 TK
June 20Purchase30,000 TK
June 30Bank Charge1,000 TK
mcqData.explanation: Balance = 50,000 TK (Debit) - 39,000 TK (Total Credits) = 11,000 TK (Debit).

mcqData.text: Who is the father of Modern Accounting?
mcqData.explanation: Luca Pacioli published the first work on double-entry bookkeeping in 1494.

mcqData.text: Owner pays employee salaries using personal funds. Correct Journal?
mcqData.explanation: Business expense increases (Salary Debit) and owner's investment increases (Capital Credit).

mcqData.text: What can be known by preparing Cash Receipt and Cash Payment Journals? i. Total cash receipts ii. Total cash payments iii. Total bank overdraft
mcqData.explanation: Cash Receipt and Cash Payment journals track all inflows and outflows of cash/bank. Bank overdraft (iii) is a balance typically determined in the Ledger or Bank Reconciliation, not directly as a total from these journals.

mcqData.text: Balancing on Jan 1: Cash 1.05L, Bank 1.1L, Furniture 1.15L, Inventory 1.2L, Payable 33k, Due Salary 18k. Total Assets?
mcqData.explanation: Total Assets = Cash + Bank + Furniture + Inventory = 1,05,000 TK + 1,10,000 TK + 1,15,000 TK + 1,20,000 TK = 4,50,000 TK.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) What is the Opening Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Capital = Total Assets - Total Liabilities = 4,50,000 TK - (33,000 TK + 18,000 TK) = 3,99,000 TK.

mcqData.text: If land is purchased for the purpose of "Resale," which account is debited?
mcqData.explanation: When land is bought to be sold as a business product, it is treated as "Purchase."

mcqData.text: Which of these is known as the "Permanent Book" of account?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger is the final and permanent book where all accounts are stored.

mcqData.text: Which are the optional steps in the Accounting Cycle? i. Adjusting Entries; ii. Worksheet; iii. Reversing Entries.
mcqData.explanation: Worksheets and Reversing Entries are optional. Adjusting entries are a mandatory part of the cycle to ensure the accuracy of financial statements.

mcqData.text: Sold 80k goods (terms 2/10, n/30). 10k returned. If paid in 10 days, how much is the discount?
mcqData.explanation: Discount = (Net amount) 70,000 TK x 2% = 1,400 TK.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) If paid after 10 days, how much must be paid?
mcqData.explanation: After 10 days, the 2% discount is lost. The full net amount of 70,000 TK is due.

mcqData.text: Commission is earned but not yet received. Correct Journal?
options: Accrued Commission Debit, Commission Income Credit
Commission Income Debit, Accrued Commission Credit
mcqData.explanation: Earned but unpaid income is an Asset (Accrued/Receivable Commission).

mcqData.text: Depending on the organization, the Cash Book is called: i. Book of receipts and expenses; ii. Book of receipts and payments; iii. Both Journal and Ledger.
mcqData.explanation: The Cash Book serves as a book of original entry (Journal) and a final account (Ledger) for cash/bank transactions.

mcqData.text: Which task is performed in the Ledger?
mcqData.explanation: Ledger-posting is the process of "Classifying" transactions into separate accounts.

mcqData.text: Bought 8k goods (10% trade disc). Paid half later with 2% cash disc. Recorded Cash Discount?
mcqData.explanation: Net price: 7,200 TK. Half: 3,600 TK. Cash Disc = 3,600 TK x 2% = 72.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) What is the remaining liability after the half payment?
mcqData.explanation: The remaining half of the net trade price (7,200 TK / 2) is 3,600 TK.

mcqData.text: In which cash book is "Cash Discount" (Allowed/Received) recorded?
mcqData.explanation: Asset Triple-Column Cash Book has a dedicated column for Discount.

mcqData.text: In which year was the Double Entry System founded?
mcqData.explanation: Luca Pacioli published the system in 1494.

mcqData.text: How many columns are there in a "T-Account" Ledger format?
mcqData.explanation: Asset T-Account has 8 columns (4 on the Debit side, 4 on the Credit side).

mcqData.text: From which language did the words "Debit" and "Credit" originate?
mcqData.explanation: They come from the Latin words 'Debere' and 'Credere'.

mcqData.text: Correct Journal for paying Income Tax (for a sole proprietorship)?
mcqData.explanation: In sole proprietorships, the owner's income tax is a personal expense (Drawings).

mcqData.text: What type of book is a Petty Cash Book?
mcqData.explanation: Petty Cash books are usually multi-column to track various small expenses.

mcqData.text: Which of these is NOT a step of the Accounting Cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Verification is a general process but not a formal "step" like the others.

mcqData.text: Invested 60k. Bought 10k goods (cash). Sold 20k goods (cash). Ending Cash balance?
mcqData.explanation: Cash = 60,000 TK (Capital) - 10,000 TK (Purchase) + 20,000 TK (Sales) = 70,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Invested 60k. 10k goods bought. 20k goods sold. 1k goods returned. Journal for the return?
mcqData.explanation: Sales returns reduce the amount owed by the customer (Receivable/Debtor).

mcqData.text: Which of these is NOT a mandatory step of the Accounting Cycle?
mcqData.explanation: The Worksheet is an optional tool used for convenience.

mcqData.text: Which step of the accounting cycle is optional?
mcqData.explanation: Asset Worksheet is helpful but not a required accounting step.

mcqData.text: What can be known from the Purchase Journal? i. Total purchase amount; ii. Total accounts payable; iii. Total credit purchases.
mcqData.explanation: The Purchase Journal specifically records credit purchases of goods, which directly shows the total credit purchases and the resulting accounts payable.

mcqData.text: If furniture is purchased for "Resale," which account is debited?
mcqData.explanation: Items for resale are considered inventory/goods, so "Purchase" is debited.

mcqData.text: In a Three-Column Cash Book, which columns require balancing (calculating the 'carried down' or 'brought down' balance)? i. Discount column; ii. Bank column; iii. Cash column.
mcqData.explanation: Cash and Bank columns are balanced to find the remaining funds. Discount columns are simply totaled and transferred to the ledger; they are never balanced.

mcqData.text: Bought 10k goods (10% trade disc). Paid half later with 2% cash disc. Discount amount recorded?
mcqData.explanation: Net: 9,000 TK. Half: 4,500 TK. Cash Discount = 4,500 TK x 2% = 90.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) What is the remaining liability (debt)?
mcqData.explanation: The remaining half of the net trade price (9,000 TK / 2) is 4,500 TK.

mcqData.text: What is the last step of the Accounting Cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries are generally considered the final formal step.

mcqData.text: When maintaining a Petty Cash Book under the Imprest System: i. Funds are used properly; ii. It is easier to control the cashier; iii. Preparing the Trial Balance becomes easier.
mcqData.explanation: The Imprest system provides strict control over minor expenses and funds, though it doesn't directly simplify the overall Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Bought 50k goods (10% trade disc). Paid half later with 10% cash disc. Recorded discount?
mcqData.explanation: Net: 45,000 TK. Half: 22,500 TK. Cash Discount = 22,500 TK x 10% = 2,250 TK.

mcqData.text: Asset Contra Entry occurs in the Cash Book when: i. Business cash is deposited into the bank; ii. Asset check is received and deposited immediately; iii. Bank withdrawal for office use.
mcqData.explanation: Contra entries involve internal movement between Cash and Bank. Depositing a customer's check immediately is often recorded as a direct bank receipt, not a contra.

mcqData.text: Which book is known as the "King of all Books"?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger is the ultimate and permanent record for all accounts.

mcqData.text: Which is the primary/original book of entry?
mcqData.explanation: Transactions are first recorded in the Journal.

mcqData.text: Asset 25k check from sales is deposited and then dishonored. Which book records this?
mcqData.explanation: Dishonored checks are reversed in the Cash Book (Bank column).

mcqData.text: Following previous Q, which accounts are affected by the dishonored check? i. Accounts Receivable; ii. Sales; iii. Bank.
mcqData.explanation: When a check is dishonored, the bank balance decreases (Credit Bank) and the debtor's liability is reinstated (Debit Accounts Receivable).

mcqData.text: Receivable is 10,000 TK. Terms are 2/10, net 30. If paid on time, discount amount?
List price 20k. Trade disc 10%. Cash disc 5%. What is the Receivable (Debtor) amount?
mcqData.explanation: Receivables are recorded at the net trade price: 20,000 TK - 10% = 18,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Purchased stationery for 2,300 TK including 15% VAT. Debit to Stationery account?
mcqData.explanation: Net Cost = 2,300 TK / 1.15 = 2,000 TK. VAT is a separate account.

mcqData.text: Correct Journal for Discount Allowed?
mcqData.explanation: Discount allowed is an expense (Debit) and reduces the amount from Debtors (Credit).

mcqData.text: What is the source document for Sales Return Journal?
mcqData.explanation: The seller issues a Credit Note to confirm the return of goods.

mcqData.text: When VAT is collected during sales, what type of account is it?
mcqData.explanation: Collected VAT must be paid to the government, so it is a Liability.

mcqData.text: Bought 70k goods. Paid 5k cash, 15k check. Remaining paid at 5% discount. Cash paid?
mcqData.explanation: Balance: 50k. After 5% discount (2,500 TK), payment is 47,500 TK.

mcqData.text: Based on the previous data, what is the impact of the 2,500 TK received discount on the business? i. Income increases by 2,500 TK; ii. Assets increase by 2,500 TK; iii. Equity increases by 2,500 TK.
mcqData.explanation: Discount Received is an income (increases profit/equity). It decreases the liability (Accounts Payable) rather than increasing assets.

mcqData.text: Which of the following are Contra Entries? i) Customer depositing directly into bank; ii) Cash deposited into bank; iii) Bank withdrawal for business use.
mcqData.explanation: Contra entries involve internal movement between Cash and Bank accounts. Customer deposits (i) are direct bank receipts, not contra.

mcqData.text: Petty cash limit is 6,500 TK. Spent 4,200 TK in June. How much check is received in July?
mcqData.explanation: Under the Imprest system, the exact amount spent (4,200 TK) is replenished.

mcqData.text: Goods worth 40,000 TK were bought under terms 2/10, n/30. This means: i) 800 discount if paid in 10 days; ii) 4,000 TK discount if paid in 2 days; iii) Must Payable within 30 days.
mcqData.explanation: 2/10 means 2% discount (40,000 TK * 2% = 800) within 10 days. n/30 means the final deadline is 30 days.

mcqData.text: What is the full form of 'brought down' in accounting?
mcqData.explanation: Brought down' (brought down) indicates a balance carried over from a previous period.

mcqData.text: The Cash Book is considered: i. Journal; ii. Ledger; iii. Trial Balance.
mcqData.explanation: The Cash Book acts as both a book of original entry (Journal) and a final account (Ledger) for cash transactions.

mcqData.text: Which is considered the "Final/Permanent" step of account keeping?
mcqData.explanation: Ledgers are the permanent home of all financial classification.

mcqData.text: How many types of discounts are there usually?
mcqData.explanation: Mainly two types: Trade Discount and Cash Discount.

mcqData.text: What is recorded in a Cash Book?
mcqData.explanation: Both cash inflows and outflows are recorded in the Cash Book.

mcqData.text: Purchased goods for 69,000 TK including 15% VAT. Amount of VAT?
Started with Cash 1L, Furniture 50k, and 10% Bank Loan 25k. Opening Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Capital = Assets (1L cash + 50k furniture) - Liabilities (0). Loan doesn't increase capital.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) Cash purchase 30k, Sales 50k, Salary 10k. Closing Capital?
Which is recorded in the 'General' Journal (Pracrita Jabela)?
mcqData.explanation: Opening, closing, and adjusting entries go in the General Journal.

mcqData.text: What is the second step of the Accounting Cycle?
mcqData.explanation: 1. Identification -> 2. Journalizing -> 3. Posting to Ledger.

mcqData.text: Transactions cannot happen without two parties' - which feature is this?
mcqData.explanation: Dual Entity (Dwoito Sotta) refers to the two sides of every transaction.

mcqData.text: Bought 10k goods at 5% trade discount. Amount recorded in Purchase Account?
(Based on previous) If 10% cash discount is applied to the payment, how much is paid?
What type of transactions are recorded in a Purchase Journal?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase Journal is strictly for goods purchased on credit.

mcqData.text: Which relationship is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase discounts reduce the amount owed to creditors (Payables).

mcqData.text: Transactions that affect Accounts Receivable: i) Credit Sales; ii) Sales Return; iii) Collection from Asset/R.
mcqData.explanation: All three change the balance of receivables: credit sales increase it, while returns and collections decrease it.

mcqData.text: Cash on hand 200. Received 800 during the month. Imprest amount?
mcqData.explanation: Opening cash + amount received from main cashier = Imprest limit (1,000 TK).

mcqData.text: Sold 40k goods (5% disc). Received 10k cash and 20k check. Credit sale amount?
mcqData.explanation: Net: 38k. Total received: 30k. Remaining credit: 8,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Following previous Q, how does the 200 cash discount affect financial statements? i) Expense Income 1,200 TK, Assets dec 200; ii) Assets dec 200, Expense Income 200; iii) Cash Income 7,800 TK, Equity dec 200.
mcqData.explanation: The 200 discount is an expense (reduces equity) and reduces the receivable asset. Cash received is 8,000 TK - 200 = 7,800 TK.

mcqData.text: In which country did the Double Entry System originate?
mcqData.explanation: Italy (specifically Venice), where Luca Pacioli lived.

mcqData.text: Which indicates a 'Credit Balance' for a personal account?
mcqData.explanation: Personal accounts showing credit balances represent creditors (Liabilities).

mcqData.text: Alternative names for the Journal: i) Subsidiary book; ii) Final book; iii) Primary book.
mcqData.explanation: The Journal is a primary and subsidiary book. The Ledger is the final (paka) book.

mcqData.text: Principles of Double Entry System: i) Two parties (Giver & Receiver); ii) Receiver is Debit, Giver is Credit; iii) Total Debit equals total Credit.
mcqData.explanation: These are the fundamental rules of the double-entry accounting mechanism.

mcqData.text: Bought 30k goods (10% trade disc). Paid half by check (5% cash disc). Purchase Debit?
mcqData.explanation: 30,000 TK - 10% = 27,000 TK. (Cash discount doesn't change Purchase Debit).

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) Bank account Credit amount?
mcqData.explanation: Total 27,000 TK - 5% cash discount (1,350 TK) = 25,650 TK.

mcqData.text: As a result of credit sales: i) Income increases; ii) Fixed assets increase; iii) Accounts receivable increase.
mcqData.explanation: Credit sales increase revenue (income) and create an asset called Accounts Receivable. Fixed assets are not affected.

mcqData.text: What is the full form of 'carried down'?
mcqData.explanation: carried down stands for 'Carried Down', used when closing ledger balances.

mcqData.text: Started with 1L cash and 50k goods. Opening Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Total investment: 1,00,000 TK cash + 50,000 TK inventory = 1,50,000 TK.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) Cash sales 20k, Salary 15k, Cash purchase 25k. Cash Ledger balance?
mcqData.explanation: (1L + 20k sales) - (15k salary + 25k purchase) = 80,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Asset debtor directly deposited 25,000 TK into the bank. For this: i) Cash increases; ii) Bank increases; iii) Debtors decrease.
mcqData.explanation: Asset direct bank deposit increases the business's bank balance and reduces the amount the debtor owes (Accounts Receivable).

mcqData.text: Which is an optional step in the accounting cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Reversing and Opening entries are optional

mcqData.text: Paid 5,000 TK wages for machine installation. Debit which account?
mcqData.explanation: Installation costs are capitalized into the Asset account (Machinery).

mcqData.text: Which of the following is consistent with the principles of the Double Entry System? i) Transactions have one account; ii) Transactions have two parties; iii) Total Debit and Credit amounts must be equal.
mcqData.explanation: Double entry requires at least two accounts (dual aspect), and the total debit must always equal the total credit for every transaction.

mcqData.text: Received 9,000 TK after 10% discount. Discount amount?
Which book is called the 'King of all books'?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger is the master record for a business.

mcqData.text: Owner pays employee salaries from personal funds. Correct Journal?
mcqData.explanation: Expense increases (Salary Debit) and owner investment increases (Capital Credit).

mcqData.text: What acts as an alternative to cash in business?
mcqData.explanation: Checks are used as a medium of exchange similar to cash.

mcqData.text: Bought paper and ink for office use. Debit which account?
mcqData.explanation: Office supplies like paper/ink are categorized as 'Stationery'.

mcqData.text: VAT included in sales price is what type of account?
mcqData.explanation: VAT collected is owed to the government (Liability).

mcqData.text: Imprest amount 5k. Cash on hand 1.5k at month end. Petty cash spent?
mcqData.explanation: Amount spent = Imprest limit (5,000 TK) - Cash on hand (1,500 TK) = 3,500 TK.

mcqData.text: Discount allowed to collect money quickly from debtors?
mcqData.explanation: Cash discount provided to debtors is called 'Discount Allowed'.

mcqData.text: When was VAT introduced in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: VAT (Mushok) was introduced on July 1, 1991.

mcqData.text: What is the universally accepted accounting method?
mcqData.explanation: Double-entry is the global standard for financial accounting.

mcqData.text: What does 'Credit' signify?
mcqData.explanation: Credit means decreasing an Asset or increasing a Liability/Equity.

mcqData.text: From which language did the word 'Ledger' originate?
mcqData.explanation: It comes from the English word 'Legger' (to lie/remain).

mcqData.text: Paid 5k cash for transporting new office furniture. Debit which account?
mcqData.explanation: Transportation costs for a new asset are added to the Asset's cost.

mcqData.text: What is the impact of selling goods at cost (without profit)? i. Expense decreases; ii. Income decreases; iii. Assets decrease.
mcqData.explanation: The entry for "Selling goods at cost" is: Sales Account Debit. and Purchase Account Credit. Debiting Sales reduces Income, and Crediting Purchase reduces Expense. Total assets remain unchanged.

mcqData.text: Bought 10k goods (5% trade disc) on credit. Recorded in Purchase Account?
mcqData.explanation: Trade discount is deducted immediately: 10,000 TK - 500 = 9,500 TK.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) Paid with 10% cash discount. Cash paid?
Which account shows a Debit balance?
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation is an expense, which naturally has a Debit balance.

mcqData.text: What is prepared to make financial statement preparation easier?
mcqData.explanation: Asset Worksheet (Karjopotro) is the tool specifically for this.

mcqData.text: Wages for repairing new furniture was incorrectly debited to 'Misc Expense'. Which should be Debit?
mcqData.explanation: Repairs/Wages for a *newly* acquired asset are debited to the Asset itself.

mcqData.text: Bought machinery for 'Resale'. Correct Journal?
mcqData.explanation: Since it's for resale, it's 'Purchase', not a fixed asset.

mcqData.text: What is the Journal known as?
mcqData.explanation: The Journal is the primary or original book of entry.

mcqData.text: Discount given to buyers on the 'List Price' is called?
mcqData.explanation: Trade discount (Karbari batta) is deducted at the time of purchase from the catalog price.

mcqData.text: Which of these is NOT received in actual cash?
mcqData.explanation: Discount received is a book entry reduction of liability, not a cash inflow.

mcqData.text: Correct Journal for "Discount Received"?
mcqData.explanation: Liability decreases (Debit) and income/gain increases (Credit).

mcqData.text: How is a rejected (dishonored) check recorded in a Double-Column Cash Book?
mcqData.explanation: Asset rejected check reverses the bank balance increase, so it is credited in the Bank column.

mcqData.text: Asset 10k check was deposited and later dishonored. What increases in the books?
mcqData.explanation: The customer's debt is restored (Receivable increases) when their check bounces.

mcqData.text: In which year did the Double Entry system originate?
mcqData.explanation: Luca Pacioli published his work in 1494.

mcqData.text: What is the continuous cycle of accounting tasks called?
mcqData.explanation: The 'Accounting Cycle' (Hisab Chakra) describes the sequence of steps from journal to final accounts.

mcqData.text: Bought 20k goods (10% trade disc). Paid half later with 2% cash disc. Discount amount?
mcqData.explanation: Net: 18,000 TK. Half: 9,000 TK. Cash Disc = 9,000 TK x 2% = 180.

mcqData.text: Chaiti Traders buys 20,000 TK goods at 10% discount on Jan 1. On Jan 8, half of the payable is paid at a 2% discount. Effect of the Jan 8 payment? i. Profit increases ii. Asset increases iii. Liability decreases
mcqData.explanation: Paying at a discount means you Payable less than the debt (Liability ↓). The discount received is an income, which increases net profit (i).

mcqData.text: What is the book where similar transactions are recorded under separate headings?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger classifies transactions into specific accounts (headings).

mcqData.text: Imprest 1,200 TK. Spent 1,000 TK in Jan. How much will the petty cashier receive in Feb?
mcqData.explanation: Under the Imprest system, the exact amount spent (1,000 TK) is replenished.

mcqData.text: If money is withdrawn from the bank for personal use, the journal entry will be— i. Cash Account Debit ii. Drawings Account Debit iii. Bank Account Credit
mcqData.explanation: For personal use, we debit Drawings (ii) because equity decreases. Since money leaves the bank, Bank Account is credited (iii). Cash is not involved as it's not a business withdrawal.

mcqData.text: Sold 50k goods. Received 5k cash, 10k check. Total 'Credit Sale'?
mcqData.explanation: Total Sale (50,000 TK) - Cash/Check (15,000 TK) = 35,000 TK Credit Sale.

mcqData.text: Impact of 'Discount Allowed' in the books?
mcqData.explanation: Discount allowed is an expense (Debit) and reduces the receivable asset (Credit).

mcqData.text: Which rule is correct for 'Debit'?
mcqData.explanation: Debit = Increase in Assets/Expenses OR Decrease in Liabilities/Equity.

mcqData.text: What is recorded in the Cash Receipt Journal? i. Cash sales of goods ii. Payment of debt iii. Collection from debtors
mcqData.explanation: The Cash Receipt Journal records all cash inflows. Collection from debtors (iii) and cash sales (i) are inflows. Payment of debt (ii) is an outflow.

mcqData.text: If a check is received and deposited immediately, record in which column?
mcqData.explanation: Immediate bank deposits go directly to the Debit side of the Bank column.

mcqData.text: In a Three-Column Cash Book, balances (balancing) are drawn for— i. Cash column ii. Bank column iii. Discount column
mcqData.explanation: Cash and Bank columns are balanced to find the remaining amount. The Discount column is simply totaled and transferred to the ledger; it is never balanced.

mcqData.text: If accounts are maintained under the Double Entry System: i. Complete records of transactions are possible; ii. Duplication occurs in recording; iii. Mathematical accuracy can be verified.
mcqData.explanation: The Double Entry System ensures a full record and allows for the preparation of a Trial Balance to check accuracy. It avoids unnecessary duplication by using a systematic approach.

mcqData.text: In which cash book is "Cash Discount" recorded?
mcqData.explanation: Triple-column cash books have a dedicated space for discount.

mcqData.text: Credit purchase 20k, trade disc 10%, packing 500, insurance 1k, transport 200. Purchase book entry?
mcqData.explanation: (20k - 10% disc) + 500 + 1000 + 200 = 19,700 TK. (Cash purchase is ignored).

mcqData.text: 15k check received from sales and deposited is later dishonored. Record in?
mcqData.explanation: Bounced checks are always reversed in the Cash Book (Bank column).

mcqData.text: Mr. Mostafiz received 15,000 TK via check and deposited it. Later, the check was dishonored. Which accounts are affected? i. Accounts Receivable ii. Sales Account iii. Bank Account
mcqData.explanation: When a check is dishonored, the Bank balance decreases and the claim against the debtor (Accounts Receivable) increases. Sales are not reversed because the transaction happened.

mcqData.text: Started with 2L Bank balance, 50k Furniture, 30k Goods. Opening Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Capital = 2,00,000 TK + 50,000 TK + 30,000 TK = 2,80,000 TK. (Loans are liabilities, not capital).

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) 1L Bank Loan increases what?
mcqData.explanation: Asset bank loan is a debt that the business must repay over time.

mcqData.text: Owner withdraws goods at "Sales Price". Journal?
mcqData.explanation: When goods are withdrawn at sales value, it is treated as a sale to the owner.

mcqData.text: What does a 'Debit Balance' in a Subsidiary Ledger (Debtor/Receivable) mean?
mcqData.explanation: It means the business has a claim (Paona) to receive money from a customer.

mcqData.text: Where are transactions recorded first?
mcqData.explanation: The Journal is the "book of first entry."

mcqData.text: Mandatory entries in account keeping are— i. Adjusting entry ii. Closing entry iii. Reversing entry
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting and Closing entries are necessary to prepare financial statements. Reversing entries are optional.

mcqData.text: Optional steps of the accounting cycle are— i. Preparing a worksheet ii. Preparing reversing entries iii. Preparing Post-Closing Trial Balance
mcqData.explanation: Worksheets and Reversing entries are for the accountant's convenience. The Post-Closing Trial Balance is considered a formal part of the closing process.

mcqData.text: Preparing a Trial Balance is which stage of the cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Trial Balance "summarizes" the ledger balances.

mcqData.text: Depreciation on an asset results in:
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation is an expense (Debit) that lowers the book value of the asset (Credit).

mcqData.text: Which document is the basis for a Sales Journal?
mcqData.explanation: Invoices issued to customers are the source for credit sales records.

mcqData.text: Where did Double Entry accounting first begin?
mcqData.explanation: Venice, Italy is the birthplace of modern accounting.

mcqData.text: Which accounts typically show Debit balances?
mcqData.explanation: Expenses and Assets both increase with Debits.

mcqData.text: Owner pays staff salaries from personal funds. Journal?
mcqData.explanation: Business expense increases and owner's investment (Capital) increases.

mcqData.text: Invested 50k. Credit sales 8k. Cash purchase 4k. Sales return 1k. Ending Cash?
mcqData.explanation: 50,000 TK (Initial) - 4,000 TK (Cash Purchase) = 46,000 TK. (Credit items ignored).

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) What is the total 'Receivable' amount?
mcqData.explanation: Credit Sales (8,000 TK) - Sales Return (1,000 TK) = 7,000 TK Receivable.

mcqData.text: Which entry closes nominal (income/expense) accounts at year-end?
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries transfer income and expenses to the Capital account.

mcqData.text: The Double Entry System is a— i. Complete method ii. Scientific method iii. Faulty method
mcqData.explanation: Double entry is the only scientific and complete method as it records both aspects of every transaction.

mcqData.text: Which discount is NOT shown in a Triple-Column Cash Book?
mcqData.explanation: Trade discount is never recorded in any cash book.

mcqData.text: Double Entry System is: i. Asset complete accounting system; ii. Asset reliable accounting system; iii. Asset scientific accounting system.
mcqData.explanation: Complete, reliable, and scientific (i, ii, iii).

mcqData.text: What is the first step of the Accounting Cycle?
mcqData.explanation: You must first identify if an event is a "transaction."

mcqData.text: Invested 50k cash and 50k goods. Opening Capital?
(Based on previous) Cash sales 10k, Salary 5k, Cash purchase 15k. Cash ledger jer?
Which is a 'Contra Entry'?
mcqData.explanation: Moving money from the office to the bank is a Contra entry (C).

mcqData.text: In which year was the VAT law implemented in Bangladesh?
What does the word "Jour" mean?
mcqData.explanation: It comes from the French word for "Day."

mcqData.text: Total Debits always equal Total Credits because:
mcqData.explanation: The accounting equation must always stay balanced.

mcqData.text: Transactions are recorded in the Journal: i. By analyzing Debit/Credit; ii. Chronologically; iii. With explanation.
mcqData.explanation: Asset complete journal entry must follow the date order, identify the debit/credit accounts, and provide a short narration (explanation).

mcqData.text: Trial Balance is which step of the cycle?
mcqData.explanation: 1. ID -> 2. Journal -> 3. Ledger -> 4. Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Which is the "Main Book" (Paka Boi) of accounts?
mcqData.explanation: The Ledger is the final, permanent destination for data.

mcqData.text: Bought 2L goods. Paid 40% cash and 30% by Bill. Where is the 80k (40%) recorded?
mcqData.explanation: Any cash payment, even partial, is recorded in the Cash Book.

mcqData.text: Mr. Jafar bought 2,00,000 TK worth of goods; paid 40% in cash and 30% via bills payable. Impact on Financial Position? i. Liability Income; ii. Equity dec; iii. Assets dec.
mcqData.explanation: Purchase (Expense) reduces Equity. Cash payment reduces Assets. Bills Payable increases Liabilities. Remaining 30% credit also increases Liability.

mcqData.text: In which book are transactions grouped under unique headings?
mcqData.explanation: Classification happens in the Ledger.

mcqData.text: What is the journal entry for Sales at Cost (Profitless Sales)? i. Sales Debit; ii. Purchase Credit; iii. Inventory Credit.
mcqData.explanation: In a profitless sale, we reverse the effect of both by debiting Sales (reducing income) and crediting Purchase (reducing expense).

mcqData.text: Month-end Cash + [X] = Imprest amount. What is X?
mcqData.explanation: The cash on hand plus the amount spent should equal the total limit.

mcqData.text: How is a 'Payable' (Account Payable) created?
mcqData.explanation: Buying goods on credit creates a liability to Payable the supplier.

mcqData.text: Mr. Karim sold 60,000 TK goods on credit. Trade discount 10%, packing 500, and 15% VAT on sales price. Total including VAT?
mcqData.explanation: Sales after disc: 54,000 TK. VAT: 54,000 TK x 15% = 8,100 TK. Total: 54k + 8.1k = 62,100 TK.

mcqData.text: What is the essential element of a Journal entry?
mcqData.explanation: An explanation (narration) is mandatory for every journal entry.

mcqData.text: Mr. Alim started with 3L cash, 1L furniture, and 50k loan. Amount of Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Capital = Assets (3L + 1L) - Liabilities (0). Loan doesn't increase capital.

mcqData.text: (Based on previous) What is the total cash in the business?
mcqData.explanation: Total Cash = Owner's cash (3L) + Loan cash (50k) = 3,50,000 TK.

mcqData.text: What is the last step of the Accounting Cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Financial Statements (Final Accounts) is the final stage of the cycle.

mcqData.text: Where is a check payment recorded in a single-column cash book?
mcqData.explanation: Single-column books only record cash; check transactions are excluded.

mcqData.text: Where is it logical to show a Trade Discount?
mcqData.explanation: Trade discounts are deducted directly on the invoice, not in the books.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stimulus and answer the question: Asset partial view of Padma Enterprise's Bank Account for March 2018 is given below: Date 2018 | Particulars | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) March 1 | Balance Brought Down | | | | 25,000 TK 20 | Kamal Account | 15,000 TK | | | 30 | Jamal Account | | 3,000 TK | | Q. What is the bank account balance for March? (Type: Chittagong Board - 2019)

Date 2018ParticularsDebit (TK)Credit (TK)Debit (TK)Credit (TK)
March 1Balance Brought Down25,000 TK
20Kamal Account15,000 TK
30Jamal Account3,000 TK
(Based on previous) If a 22k check is issued on March 31, final balance?
mcqData.explanation: Previous balance 15,000 TK - 22,000 TK = -7,000 TK (Credit balance).

mcqData.text: Which is recorded in the General (Pracrita) Journal?
mcqData.explanation: Credit purchase of assets (Furniture) goes to General Journal.

mcqData.text: Amount given to a petty cashier at the end of the month under the Imprest System is:
mcqData.explanation: The system replenishes the exact amount spent to return to the initial limit.

mcqData.text: Journal: Receivable Debit 900, Cash Debit 100, Sales Credit 1,000 TK. Which transaction fits?
mcqData.explanation: This entry shows a total sale of 1,000 TK, with 100 received in cash and 900 on credit.

mcqData.text: Which of the following statements is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Sales books are strictly for recording credit sales of merchandise (goods).

mcqData.text: Which is known as the "Golden Rule" for determining Debit-Credit?
mcqData.explanation: The Traditional (Snoton) method is famously called the Golden Rule.

mcqData.text: What is a discount received from a "Payable" (Creditor) called?
mcqData.explanation: Discounts received happen when settling a debt to a supplier for less than the due amount.

mcqData.text: Started with 25k cash and 5k personal loan. How much is credited to Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Only the owner's personal cash (25k) is capital. The loan is a separate liability.

mcqData.text: Asset Debit Balance in the Ledger represents: i. Assets; ii. Expenses; iii. Income.
mcqData.explanation: According to the fundamental rules, Assets and Expenses/Losses always show a Debit balance. Liabilities, Income, and Equity show Credit balances.

mcqData.text: What does "2/10, n/30" mean?
mcqData.explanation: It means a 2% cash discount is available if paid within 10 days.

mcqData.text: Features of a Cash Receipt Journal: (i. Cash trans, ii. Special journal, iii. Debit balance)
mcqData.explanation: It is a special journal designed strictly for recording cash inflows.

mcqData.text: What does a Credit balance in a Subsidiary Ledger (Accounts Payable) mean?
mcqData.explanation: Asset credit balance for a supplier (Accounts Payable) represents a liability (Dena).

mcqData.text: Which of the following accounts shows a Credit balance?
mcqData.explanation: Interest Received is income, which naturally carries a Credit balance.

mcqData.text: The Cash Book functions as: i. Journal; ii. Ledger; iii. Trial Balance.
mcqData.explanation: The Cash Book is unique as it is a book of original entry (Journal) and a book of final entry (Ledger) for cash/bank.

mcqData.text:
Answer the following question based on the information below: Cash Account Date | Particulars | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) 2016 July 01 | Capital Account | 5,000 TK | | | July 20 | Bank Account | | 1,000 TK | | July 31 | Rent Account | 500 | | | Q. What is the balance of the Cash Account on July 31, 2016?

DateParticularsDebit (TK)Credit (TK)Debit (TK)Credit (TK)
2016 July 01Capital Account5,000 TK
July 20Bank Account1,000 TK
July 31Rent Account500
Based on the provided Cash Account ledger, which transactions occurred? i. Cash capital intro 5,000 TK; ii. Rent paid 500; iii. Bank withdrawal 1,000 TK.
mcqData.explanation: Statement i is a definite inflow (Debit), and statement iii is a definite movement involving the bank recorded on the Credit side. Statement ii is positioned incorrectly for a standard "payment," leading to the selection of i ও iii.

mcqData.text: Based on the same Cash Account, which journal entries are correct? i. Cash Debit, Bank Debit, Rent Debit, Capital Credit; ii. Cash Debit, Capital Credit, Rent Credit; iii. Bank Debit, Cash Credit.
mcqData.explanation: Why (c) is the Correct Option: Statement i is incorrect: It suggests Bank and Rent are both Debited along with Cash, which contradicts the ledger where Bank is on the Credit side. Statement ii is consistent: It correctly identifies the accounts that were Credited (Capital and Rent) against the Cash Debit. Statement iii is consistent: It correctly identifies that when Cash is Credited, the corresponding Bank account is Debited. Therefore, the correct journal entries reflected are found in statements ii ও iii.

mcqData.text: Opening: Receivable 20k, Payable 18k, Assets 50k, Stock 24k. Opening Capital?
Paid 18k, Closing Payable 12k, Opening Payable 18k. Credit Purchase?
Sales 25k, Closing Receivable 25k, Opening Receivable 20k. Cash from debtors?
If goods are withdrawn at "Sales Price," which account is credited?
mcqData.explanation: Using sales price means treating the withdrawal as a regular sale.

mcqData.text: Why record transactions in the Journal immediately?
mcqData.explanation: Recording immediately ensures data is not forgotten or lost.

mcqData.text: Customer pays directly to the bank. Where in a single-column cash book?
mcqData.explanation: Single-column books only record physical cash, not bank transactions.

mcqData.text: Cash 1.05L, Bank 1.1L, Furn 1.15L, Stock 1.2L. Total Assets?
(Based on previous) Payable 33k, Due Sal 18k. Opening Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Capital = Assets (4.50L) - Liability (51,000 TK) = 3,99,000 TK.

mcqData.text: When are Capital and Liability accounts debited?
mcqData.explanation: According to modern rules, these accounts are debited when they decrease.

mcqData.text: Sold 10k goods via crossed check. Which account is debited?
mcqData.explanation: Crossed checks must go through the Bank account.

mcqData.text: Correct journal for "Providing a loan to Hasan"?
mcqData.explanation: This creates a receivable asset: Hasan's Loan Account Debit, Cash Credit.

mcqData.text: Which accounts are affected when a deposited check is dishonored? i. Bank; ii. Accounts Receivable; iii. Accounts Payable.
mcqData.explanation: Dishonor reduces the bank balance (Credit) and increases the amount the customer owes (Debit Receivable).

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a step of the accounting cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Cash Book is a specific ledger/journal tool, not a cycle step.

mcqData.text: Sales check returned dishonored. Which book is it recorded in?
mcqData.explanation: Dishonored checks are updated in the Cash Book (Bank column).

mcqData.text: Mr. Asad sold goods for 50k via check and deposited it. It was later dishonored. Which accounts are affected? i. Accounts Payable; ii. Accounts Receivable; iii. Sales.
mcqData.explanation: Dishonor reverses the collection from the customer. The primary accounts affected are Bank and Accounts Receivable (প্রাপ্য হিসাব).

mcqData.text: Mr. Asif starts business with Cash 1,00,000 TK, Machinery 70,000 TK, and Furniture 20,000 TK. Sales to Bappi 8,000 TK (credit) and Rent paid 5,000 TK. Cash balance?
mcqData.explanation: Cash = 1,00,000 TK (Start) - 5,000 TK (Rent).

mcqData.text: Stationery purchased for 2,300 TK including 15% VAT. How much is debited to Stationery account?
Which is used to record transactions related to Sales Returns?
mcqData.explanation: Seller issues a Credit Note.

mcqData.text: Which of the following expresses a Debit Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Prov. for Discount on Creditors.

mcqData.text: Mr. Rana withdrew 20,000 TK for business and 5,000 TK for personal use. The 20,000 TK withdrawn for business will be recorded in the Cash Book as: i. Credit side, bank column; ii. Credit side, cash column; iii. Debit side, cash column.
mcqData.explanation: Contra Entry: Cash increases, Bank decreases.

mcqData.text: Based on the previous data, what is the impact of personal cash drawings on the Balance Sheet? i. Equity decreases; ii. Cash balance decreases; iii. Net profit remains unchanged.
mcqData.explanation: Drawings reduce both assets (Cash) and equity (Capital). Drawings do not affect the calculation of Net Profit.

fullCQData.stem:
The following transactions occurred in Mr. Habib's business in July 2024:

  • July 1: Started business with cash Tk 2,00,000, office equipment Tk 50,000, and a bank loan of Tk 40,000.
  • July 10: Provided services to a client for Tk 40,000, receiving Tk 30,000 in cash.
  • July 12: Purchased supplies for cash Tk 10,000.
  • July 15: Paid rent for two months, Tk 10,000.
  • July 24: Withdrew Tk 15,000 from the bank.
  • July 28: Paid half of the bank loan amount.
  • July 30: Supplies used amounted to Tk 6,000.
  • July 31: Paid monthly salary Tk 10,000.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Determine Mr. Habib's opening capital.
questions.answer:
Determination of Opening Capital

Opening Capital = Opening Assets − Opening Liabilities

Opening Assets:

Office Equipment50,000
Bank Balance (from loan deposited)40,000
Total Opening Assets2,90,000
Less: Opening Liabilities:

July 30————(6,000)=—+(6,000)

The transactions of Nadim Traders for the month of June 2024 are as follows:

  • June 1: Cash in hand Tk 15,000 and bank balance Tk 20,000.
