mcqData.text: Sales Return of 5,000 TK was mistakenly recorded in the Purchase Account. What is the rectifying entry?
mcqData.explanation: To fix it: Debit the correct account (Sales Return) and Credit the wrong account (Purchase) to remove it.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT included in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Closing stocks (like Unused Stationery/Supplies) are usually excluded unless "Adjusted Purchase" is used.

mcqData.text: Selling old machinery was credited to the Sales Account instead of Machinery Account. What type of error is this?
mcqData.explanation: Recording a Capital receipt (Asset sale) as a Revenue receipt (Sales) is a Violation of Accounting Principles.

mcqData.text: Asset Credit balance of 240 was mistakenly written as 2,400 TK in the Debit column. What is the difference in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Difference = Correct Amount + Wrong Amount (since side changed) = 240+2,400 TK=2,640 TK.

mcqData.text: Not included in the Trial Balance:i. Opening Bank Balanceii. Opening Cashiii. Closing Stock
mcqData.explanation: Opening cash/bank and Closing stock (normally) are excluded from the Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Purchase was written as 15,000 TK instead of 20,000 TK, and Sales as 45,000 TK instead of 50,000 TK. The Trial Balance still matched. Why?
mcqData.explanation: One error (5k less on Debit) was cancelled out by another error (5k less on Credit).

mcqData.text: (Ref #6) By how much was the total of the Trial Balance under or over-stated?
mcqData.explanation: Both sides were short by 5,000 TK, so the total sum was 5,000 TK less than it should have been.

mcqData.text: Which item is excluded from the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Opening liquid assets (Cash/Bank) are excluded because they are already part of the closing figures.

mcqData.text: Errors not detected by a Trial Balance:i. Principle Errorii. Math Errorsiii. Error of Posting to Wrong Account
mcqData.explanation: Trial Balance cannot detect Principle errors or posting to the wrong account if the amounts match.

mcqData.text: Purchase: 45k instead of 50k; Sales: 30k instead of 35k. Trial Balance matches. What error is this?
mcqData.explanation: (Same logic as #6). One error compensates for the other.

mcqData.text: (Ref #10) By how much was the Trial Balance total short or over?
mcqData.explanation: Both sides were recorded 5,000 TK short (50k−45k and 35k−30k).

mcqData.text: Which of these is excluded from the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Opening Cash/Bank are excluded (Closing ones are included).

mcqData.text: Machine installation cost of 6,000 TK was mistakenly included in Wages. What error is this?
mcqData.explanation: Treating an asset (Machine) cost as an expense (Wages) is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: (Ref #13) What is the impact of this error on the accounts?
mcqData.explanation: By recording it as Wages (Expense) instead of Machine (Asset), the total expenses increased, making Profit decrease.

mcqData.text: Trial Balance does not detect:i. Principle Errorii. Compensating Erroriii. Error of Omission
mcqData.explanation: These three types of errors do not disrupt the equality of Debits and Credits.

mcqData.text: "Machine Setup Cost" was mistakenly Credited to Machine Account. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: Since it was mistakenly credited, you must Debit it twice: once to cancel the error and once to record the correct entry.

mcqData.text: If "Adjusted Purchase" is in the Trial Balance, which is EXCLUDED?
mcqData.explanation: When "Adjusted Purchase" exists, Closing Stock is included and Opening Stock is excluded.

mcqData.text: Preparing a Trial Balance using Ledger balances is part of which accounting stage?
mcqData.explanation: Trial Balance is the first step of the Summarizing phase.

mcqData.text: Where is the arithmetical accuracy of accounts verified?
mcqData.explanation: That is the primary purpose of a Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: When one error is cancelled out by another error, it is called?
mcqData.explanation: This is the definition of a Compensating Error.

mcqData.text: Opening Stock 90,000 TK, Net Purchase 2,60,000 TK, and Closing Stock 70,000 TK. What is the Adjusted Purchase?
Which will affect the Suspense Account?i. Furniture for resale Debit to Furniture Accountii. Repair 5,000 TK Debit to Plant as 500iii. Purchase Return Debit to Purchase Account
mcqData.explanation: Item (ii) has a math error (5,000 TK vs 500) and (iii) is a wrong side entry. (i) is a principle error that matches.

mcqData.text: The purpose of the Trial Balance is—i. Verify math accuracyii. Summarize accountsiii. Determine results
mcqData.explanation: Trial Balance is for accuracy (i) and summarizing (ii). Final results (iii) are found in Financial Statements.

mcqData.text: Furniture bought for office use is Debited to "Purchase". What error is this?
mcqData.explanation: Debiting a revenue account (Purchase) for a capital asset (Furniture) is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: Which of these is a Credit in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Unearned Revenue is a liability; liabilities always have a Credit balance.

mcqData.text: Which is correct for Adjusted Purchase?
options: Opening. Stock + Net Purchase - Closing. Stock
mcqData.explanation: This is the standard formula for Adjusted Purchase.

mcqData.text: Which is included in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Closing liquid assets are included; opening ones are not.

mcqData.text: Mr. Rahman, a furniture dealer, bought furniture for 1,00,000 TK and Debited the "Furniture Account". Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: For a dealer, furniture is "Goods" (Purchase), not an asset. Debiting Furniture is a Principle error.

mcqData.text: (Ref #28) What is the impact on the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Both accounts (Purchase and Furniture) are Debit accounts. The Trial Balance will still match perfectly.

mcqData.text: Machine installation cost 50,000 TK included in Wages. Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: Asset cost treated as revenue expense = Principle Error.

mcqData.text: (Ref #30) Due to this error—i. Trial Balance will matchii. Assets will decreaseiii. Expenses will decrease
mcqData.explanation: Trial Balance matches. Assets are lower (since it's not in Machine) and Expenses are higher (so iii is wrong).

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 25k, Purchase 1.5L, Sales 2L, Closing. Stock 30k. Adjusted Purchase?
Not detected by Trial Balance:i. Omissionii. Principleiii. Compensating
mcqData.explanation: None of these disrupt the Debit/Credit equality.

mcqData.text: Machinery purchase 5L shown as Purchase. Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: Capital expenditure recorded as Revenue expenditure.

mcqData.text: (Ref #34) Rectifying entry:i. Machinery Drii. Purchase Criii. Suspense Credit
mcqData.explanation: You Debit the Asset and Credit the wrong Expense. Suspense is not needed as it was a principle error.

mcqData.text: Trial Balance acts as a bridge between—
mcqData.explanation: It uses Ledger balances and provides the data for Financial Statements.

mcqData.text: Error due to lack of accounting knowledge?
mcqData.explanation: Principle errors happen when one doesn't distinguish between Capital and Revenue.

mcqData.text: Which of these is a Credit in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Provisions/Allowances are Credits. Returns and Investments are Debits.

mcqData.text: Primary objective of Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: The core reason is to ensure Debits = Credits mathematically.

mcqData.text: Purchase Return mistakenly recorded as Sales. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: To fix: Debit Sales (to cancel the wrong credit) and Credit Purchase Return (to record correctly).

mcqData.text: Which of the following does NOT go to the Credit side of the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Provisions on Assets (like Receivables) are usually Credits, but Provision for Discount on Receivables is a rare Debit.

mcqData.text: Furniture purchase Debited to Purchase Account—what type of error is this?
mcqData.explanation: Violating the rule between Capital (Asset) and Revenue (Expense) is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: Asset balance of 28 was shown on the wrong side of the Trial Balance as 98. What is the total difference?
mcqData.explanation: Difference = 28(Correct)+98(Wrong side)=126.

mcqData.text: Shown in the Debit column of Trial Balance:i. Asset, Expense, Drawingsii. Cash, Bank Overdraft, Receivableiii. Bank, Receivable, Accrued Income
mcqData.explanation: Bank Overdraft (ii) is a Credit (Liability), so (ii) is incorrect. Assets, Expenses, and Accrued Incomes are Debits.

mcqData.text: What is the balance of the Purchase Ledger?
mcqData.explanation: The Purchase Ledger contains individual accounts of suppliers (Creditors/Payables).

mcqData.text: Which task is performed through the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: The primary goal is verifying the arithmetical accuracy of the Ledger.

mcqData.text: Ms. Jui buys supplies for 1,000 TK but records 900 in the Supplies Account. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: Since only the Supplies Account was short, we use Suspense Account to balance the correction.

mcqData.text: (Ref #47) Due to this error—i. Debit total reduces by 100ii. Credit side increases by 100iii. Suspense Account needed for Debit side
mcqData.explanation: Debit was short, so we must add 100 to the Debit side using a Suspense Account.

mcqData.text: Purchase 10k (instead of 9k) and Sales 8k (instead of 7k). Trial Balance matches. Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: Excess 1,000 TK on Debit was cancelled by excess 1,000 TK on Credit.

mcqData.text: Suspense Account is a—
mcqData.explanation: It is opened temporarily to match the Trial Balance and closed once errors are found.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 30k, Closing. Stock 20k, Adj. Purchase 1.2L, Opening. Stationery 5k. Which is EXCLUDED from Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: When Adjusted Purchase is present, Opening Stock is excluded.

mcqData.text: Purchase 45k (instead of 50k), Sales 35k (instead of 40k). Trial Balance matches. Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: Same logic as #49; errors cancel each other out.

mcqData.text: As a result of Compensating Errors—i. Both sides will be equalii. Two accounts will have errorsiii. One account will have an error
mcqData.explanation: By definition, it requires at least two errors to compensate each other.

mcqData.text: Old machinery sale credited to Sales instead of Machinery. What error?
mcqData.explanation: Capital vs Revenue violation = Principle Error.

mcqData.text: (Ref #52) How was the Trial Balance total affected?
mcqData.explanation: Both sides were short by 5,000 TK (50k−45k and 40k−35k).

mcqData.text: (Ref #51) How much was the actual Purchase during the year?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase = Adj. Purchase+Closing. Stock−Opening. Stock=1,20,000 TK+20,000 TK−30,000 TK=1,10,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Discount Allowed 550 was credited to Discount Account as 450. Difference in Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Difference = 550(should be Debit)+450(wrongly Credit)=1,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Which is a Principle Error?
options: Capital Expense. treated as Revenue Expense.
mcqData.explanation: This is the classic definition of a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: Which is excluded from the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Closing stocks (like stationery) are excluded unless specifically adjusted.

mcqData.text: Sale of an asset credited to Sales Account is what type of error?
mcqData.explanation: Capital receipt treated as Revenue receipt = Principle Error.

mcqData.text: Which are NOT included in the Trial Balance?i. Opening Bankii. Opening Cashiii. Closing Stock
mcqData.explanation: All three are generally excluded unless the "Adjusted Purchase" method is used.

mcqData.text: Where is an error in Ledger balance calculation detected?
mcqData.explanation: Once you prepare the Trial Balance, the disagreement between Debit and Credit sides reveals the error.

mcqData.text: What is the formula for Adjusted Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusted Purchase = Opening Stock + Purchase - Closing Stock.

mcqData.text: Why is a Trial Balance prepared?
mcqData.explanation: Its core purpose is to verify the mathematical accuracy of accounts.

mcqData.text: Closing Stock is included in the Trial Balance if:i. Net Purchase is givenii. Gross Profit is giveniii. Cost of Goods Sold is given
mcqData.explanation: If Purchases are already "adjusted" (i or iii), Closing Stock must be shown in the Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Capital 9k, Debtor 14k, Purchase 12k, Sales 22.5k, Salary 3k, Cash 8k, Creditor 7.5k, Closing. Stock 2k. Total Current Assets?
mcqData.explanation: Current Assets = Debtor (14k) + Cash (8k) + Closing. Stock (2k) = 24,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Suspense Account (গরমিল হিসাব) is a—
mcqData.explanation: It is opened temporarily to balance the Trial Balance and closed when errors are found.

mcqData.text: Trial Balance connects which two?
mcqData.explanation: It summarizes Ledger balances and acts as the basis for Final Accounts.

mcqData.text: Rent received 100 was put on the Debit side. The Trial Balance total will be—
mcqData.explanation: Since an income (Credit) was moved to Debit, the Debit side will be inflated by 100+100=200.

mcqData.text: Where is a Ledger balancing error detected?
mcqData.explanation: Same as #62. Discrepancies in the Trial Balance point to balancing errors.

mcqData.text: What is "Unexpired Cost" (অনিঃশেষিত ব্যয়)?
mcqData.explanation: Costs that haven't been "used up" yet (like Prepaid Rent) are assets.

mcqData.text: Which is included in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: All opening assets are excluded EXCEPT for those that are not liquid, like Opening Receivables or Stock.

mcqData.text: Which never appears in a Trial Balance column?i. Closing. Stockii. Closing. Cashiii. Opening. Bank
mcqData.explanation: Normally, Closing Stock and Opening Bank/Cash are excluded from the Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Which is shown on the Debit side?
options: on Debtorsc) Provision for Disc. on Creditors
mcqData.explanation: Provision for Discount on Creditors is a Debit (a contra-liability).

mcqData.text: Cash 10k, Debtor 8k, Creditor 2k, Rent 7k, Pur 15k, Capital 20k, Sales 12k. Suspense amount?
mcqData.explanation: Debit: 10+8+7+15=40k. Credit: 2+20+12=34k. Suspense = 6,000 TK (Credit).

mcqData.text: (Ref #75) Increasing which would remove the Suspense Account? i. Incomeii. Liabilityiii. Asset
Which is NOT included in Trial Balance?i. Opening. Cashii. Opening. Bankiii. Opening. Stock
mcqData.explanation: Opening Cash/Bank are excluded. Opening Stock is included unless Adjusted Purchase is used.

mcqData.text: Which will be in Trial Balance under Adjusted Purchase method?i. Adj. Purchaseii. Closing. Stockiii. Bank Overdraft
mcqData.explanation: Under this method, both Adjusted Purchase and Closing Stock appear. Bank Overdraft is a standard item.

mcqData.text: Why is Bank Overdraft a Credit in the Trial Balance?i. Credit Ledger balanceii. It is a liabilityiii. It reduces profit
mcqData.explanation: Liabilities have credit balances. Reducing profit (iii) is for expenses, not liabilities.

mcqData.text: Machine setup cost 10k included in Wages. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: Debit the correct asset and Credit the wrong expense to remove it.

mcqData.text: Machinery installation cost (10k) included in Wages. What is the impact of correcting this?
mcqData.explanation: Correcting it means removing 10k from Wages (Expense) and adding it to Machinery (Asset). So, Expenses decrease.

mcqData.text: Receivable 20k, Payable 7k, Drawings 5k, Capital 50k, Pur 25k, Advance Receipt 3k. Suspense amount?
mcqData.explanation: Debit: 20+5+25=50k.Credit: 7+50+3=60k.Difference = 10,000 TK (Debit).

mcqData.text: (Ref #82) Impact of correctly recording the missing 10k Purchase:i. Direct Expense upii. Gross profit downiii. Capital down
mcqData.explanation: More Purchase (Expense) increases direct costs, which lowers both Gross Profit and final Capital.

mcqData.text: Income received in advance is a—
mcqData.explanation: It is a "Unearned Revenue," which is a liability until the service is provided.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock + Net Purchase - Closing. Stock = ?
mcqData.explanation: This is the fundamental formula for Adjusted Purchase.

mcqData.text: Which is a Tangible Fixed Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Building is a physical (tangible) asset. Others are intangible or fictitious.

mcqData.text: What is shown on the Debit side of the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Assets and Expenses always carry a normal Debit balance.

mcqData.text: What is the list of closing Ledger balances called?
mcqData.explanation: This is the definition of a Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Which of these is a Debit in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Apprentice Allowance (ভাতা) is an expense (Debit). Premium (সেলামী) would be income (Credit).

mcqData.text: Included in Trial Balance:i. Opening. Stationeryii. Opening. Stockiii. Opening. Bank
mcqData.explanation: Opening Stocks are included; Opening liquid cash/bank are not.

mcqData.text: Normal Debit balance indicates:i. Income, ii. Expense, iii. Asset
mcqData.explanation: Expenses and Assets are Debits; Income is a Credit.

mcqData.text: When is Closing Stock included in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusted Purchase (Opening+Pur−Closing) already accounts for it, so Closing. Stock must be listed separately.

mcqData.text: Objectives of Trial Balance:i. Find Financial Positionii. Help in Final Accountsiii. Math accuracy
mcqData.explanation: Finding financial position (i) is the job of the Balance Sheet, not the Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Which set is correctly included in a Trial Balance?
options: Gross Profit, Closing. Stock, Adv. Ins.
Closing. Stock, Cost of Goods Sold, Drawings
mcqData.explanation: If Cost of Goods Sold (Cost of Goods Sold) is present, Closing Stock and Drawings both appear in the Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Mr. Rahat (Furniture dealer) bought furniture for stock but Debited "Furniture Account". Error?
mcqData.explanation: For a dealer, furniture is an expense (Purchase). Debiting an Asset account is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: Mr. Rahat is a furniture trader. He purchased furniture worth 3,00,000 TK from 'Star Furniture' at a 10% discount but mistakenly debited the Furniture Account. He took a table worth 3,000 TK for business use and sold half of the purchased goods for 1,50,000 TK, incurring 600 TK in transport costs. Q: What type of accounting error was committed in Mr. Rahat's books?
Which expresses a Debit balance?
mcqData.explanation: Bad Debt is an expense (Debit). All reserves listed here are Credits.

mcqData.text: What is a Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: It is not an account; it is a statement or a list of ledger balances.

mcqData.text: Error while transferring from Journal to Ledger is called?
mcqData.explanation: This is a "Clerical Error" specifically categorized as an Error of Commission/Writing.

mcqData.text: Not a Principle Error:i. Machine as Purchaseii. Sales not recordediii. Sales to X put in Y
mcqData.explanation: (i) is a principle error. (ii) is omission and (iii) is an error of commission (wrong account).

mcqData.text: Machinery purchase written as "Purchase". Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: Debit the correct Asset and Credit the wrong Expense to fix it.

mcqData.text: Which set usually has a Debit balance?
mcqData.explanation: Assets, Drawings, and Expenses/Costs always have normal Debit balances.

mcqData.text: Which is an example of a Contra-Liability (বিপরীত দায়)?
mcqData.explanation: Provision for Discount on Payables reduces a liability, so it is a Contra-Liability (Debit).

mcqData.text: Which sits on the Credit side of the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase Discount is an income/reduction in cost, so it is a Credit.

mcqData.text: Machinery repair 10k shown in Machinery Account. What error?
mcqData.explanation: Treating a Revenue expense (Repair) as a Capital expenditure (Asset) is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: Which is included in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Closing liquid assets (Cash/Bank) are included in the Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Which error affects the Suspense Account?
mcqData.explanation: Math errors or balancing errors create a mismatch, requiring a Suspense Account.

mcqData.text: Rent received 5k was Debited to Rent Account. What error?
mcqData.explanation: Posting to the wrong side of the correct account is an Error of Commission (বেদাখিলা).

mcqData.text: Preparing a Trial Balance using Ledger balances is called?
mcqData.explanation: The Trial Balance is the first step in the Summarizing phase of accounting.

mcqData.text: Orpa buys supplies for 950 but records 900 in Supplies Account. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: Since only the Supplies Account was under-recorded, we use Suspense to balance the correction.

mcqData.text: (Ref #110) Due to the error:i. Debit total is 50 shortii. Credit total is 50 moreiii. Suspense needed on Debit side
mcqData.explanation: Supplies (Debit) was short, so the total Debit side is short by 50. Suspense fills that gap.

mcqData.text: Pur recorded 40k (instead of 50k) and Sales 84k (instead of 94k). Trial Balance difference?
mcqData.explanation: The actual total should have been 10,000 TK higher on both sides.

mcqData.text: (Ref #112) What type of error is this?
mcqData.explanation: One error (10k short on Debit) is offset by another (10k short on Credit).

mcqData.text: What is a Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: It is simply a statement listing the balances of all ledger accounts.

mcqData.text: Suspense Account is—i. Temporary Accountii. Interim Accountiii. Nominal Account
mcqData.explanation: It is a temporary/provisional account, not a nominal (income/expense) account.

mcqData.text: Furniture bought for resale (Stock) put in Furniture Account. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: For a dealer, this is a "Purchase." Fix by Debiting Purchase and Crediting Furniture.

mcqData.text: Which expresses a Debit balance?
mcqData.explanation: Provision for Discount on Creditors is a Debit (Contra-Liability).

mcqData.text: Which is excluded from Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Opening bank/cash balances are excluded.

mcqData.text: Adjusted Purchase =i. Sales - Closing. Stockii. Goods for Sale - Closing. Stockiii. Opening + Pur - Closing
mcqData.explanation: (ii) and (iii) are mathematically the same thing.

mcqData.text: What type of account is Suspense Account?
mcqData.explanation: It exists only until errors are identified.

mcqData.text: Asset balance of 2,000 TK was recorded in the correct column but as 200. What is the difference in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Difference = 2,000 TK−200=1,800 TK. Since it stayed in the correct column, we just subtract.

mcqData.text: Which one is different from the other three?
mcqData.explanation: Apprentice Premium is an Income (Credit). The other three are all Expenses (Debit).

mcqData.text: Repairing a building is debited to the Building Account. What type of error is this?
mcqData.explanation: Treating a Revenue expense (Repair) as a Capital expenditure (Asset) is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: If Office Equipment purchase is debited to "Purchase Account", what error is this?
mcqData.explanation: Same as #123; confusing an Asset with an Expense violates accounting principles.

mcqData.text: Sales Return (আন্তঃফেরত) is mistakenly written as Purchase. Correcting entry?
options: Sales Return Debit, Accounts Receivable Cr
mcqData.explanation: Debit the correct account (Sales Return) and Credit the wrong account (Purchase) to cancel it.

mcqData.text: If Furniture Account was credited instead of debited, what must you do in the rectifying entry?
mcqData.explanation: To fix a "wrong side" error, you must debit double the amount (once to cancel the credit, once to record the debit).

mcqData.text: Why is Purchase Return shown on the Credit side of the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase Return reduces the total cost of goods bought, so it acts as a Decrease in Expense.

mcqData.text: Purchase 60k instead of 50k; Sales 80k instead of 70k. Trial Balance matches. Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: The 10k excess on Debit (Purchase) is balanced by 10k excess on Credit (Sales).

mcqData.text: Recording a revenue expense as capital expenditure is called?
mcqData.explanation: This is the standard definition of a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: What is the balance of the Sales Ledger?
mcqData.explanation: The Sales Ledger tracks individual customers, which are Accounts Receivable.

mcqData.text: (i) Cash Purchase 15k not in Pur Account. (ii) Credit Sales 7k not in books. Suspense amount?
mcqData.explanation: (ii) is a full omission (matches). (i) only skipped one side (Pur Account), so the Trial Balance is off by 15k.

mcqData.text: (Ref #131) What is the rectifying entry for the credit sale?
mcqData.explanation: This is a simple entry for a transaction that was completely forgotten.

mcqData.text: Which pair is recorded in the Credit column?
mcqData.explanation: Accrued Expenses and Unearned Income are both Liabilities (Credit).

mcqData.text: Writing 382 instead of 328 is what kind of error?
mcqData.explanation: Mistyping or transposing numbers is an Error of Commission/Writing.

mcqData.text: Tool to verify arithmetical accuracy?
mcqData.explanation: This is the primary function of the Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable Debit 33k (instead of 30k) and Pur Debit 50k (instead of 53k). What error?
mcqData.explanation: One debit is 3k too high, another is 3k too low. They cancel out.

mcqData.text: When is Closing Stock included in Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: As seen in earlier years, Adjusted Purchase is the key trigger for Closing Stock in Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Which of these is a Credit?
mcqData.explanation: Reserves/Provisions are Credit balances. Others are Expenses/Assets (Debit).

mcqData.text: Writing 500 instead of 5,000 TK is what error?
mcqData.explanation: Amount error = Error of Commission (Writing).

mcqData.text: Discount Received 100 was put on the Debit side. Trial Balance impact?
mcqData.explanation: Moving a Credit item to the Debit side creates a double difference (200) on the Debit side.

mcqData.text: An error that violates the rules and principles of accounting is called—
mcqData.explanation: Violating the distinction between Capital and Revenue is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: At what stage are errors corrected using the Income Statement Adjustment account?
mcqData.explanation: Errors discovered after the final accounts (Financial Statements) are closed are fixed this way.

mcqData.text: Which is NOT included in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Unused supplies are "Closing Stocks" of stationery/supplies, which are usually excluded.

mcqData.text: Whitewashing an office room debited to the Building Account. What error?
mcqData.explanation: Maintenance (Revenue) treated as an Asset cost (Capital) is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: Alternative terms for Accounts Receivable are—i. Book Debtsii. Debtorsiii. Purchase Ledger Balance
mcqData.explanation: Accounts Receivable = Debtors = Book Debts. (iii) refers to Payables.

mcqData.text: Machine setup 10k debited to Wages. What error?
mcqData.explanation: Same as #144; asset setup costs should be debited to the Asset, not an Expense.

mcqData.text: Which is excluded from Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Opening liquid assets like Cash/Bank are excluded.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 3k (instead of 4k) and Accounts Payable 2.2k (instead of 3.2k). Trial Balance matches. Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: 1,000 TK short on Debit (Accounts Receivable) is compensated by 1,000 TK short on Credit (Accounts Payable).

mcqData.text: (Ref #148) Difference between the incorrect Trial Balance total and the correct Trial Balance total?
mcqData.explanation: Since both sides were recorded 1,000 TK short, the final total will be 1,000 TK less than it should be.

mcqData.text: Pur 55k (vs 60k) and Sales 40k (vs 45k). What is the total amount of error?
mcqData.explanation: Total error = 5,000 TK(on Pur)+5,000 TK(on Sales)=10,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Machine setup wages debited to Wages instead of Machine. Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: Capital vs. Revenue violation.

mcqData.text: Writing 352 instead of 325 is what error?
mcqData.explanation: Mathematical or clerical mistyping is an Error of Commission (Writing).

mcqData.text: Wages paid 500 debited to Rent. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: Debit the correct (Wages) and Credit the wrong (Rent) to fix it.

mcqData.text: When is Closing Stock included in the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: This has been a very consistent board question across all years.

mcqData.text: Pur from Asad 4,500 TK written as 450. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: We need to add the missing 4,050 TK. Debit Purchase and Credit the Payable (Asad).

mcqData.text: Machinery purchase recorded as "Purchase". What error?
mcqData.explanation: Asset vs. Expense violation = Principle Error.

mcqData.text: Purpose of Trial Balance—i. Detect errorsii. Help in resultsiii. Ensure Double Entry
mcqData.explanation: It serves all these secondary and primary functions.

mcqData.text: Pur 50k (vs 55k) and Sales 40k (vs 45k). Error type?
mcqData.explanation: Both sides are short by 5,000 TK, so it matches.

mcqData.text: (Ref #157) How much is the Trial Balance total affected?
mcqData.explanation: Both sides are 5,000 TK short, so the total sum is 5,000 TK less than actual.

mcqData.text: Which items go in the Credit column?
mcqData.explanation: Liabilities (Accrued Expense) and Unearned Income (Advance Income) are Credits.

mcqData.text: Machine setup 2,000 TK included in Wages. Error type?
mcqData.explanation: Capital cost treated as Revenue expense.

mcqData.text: What type of account is a Suspense Account?
mcqData.explanation: It is an অস্থায়ী ও সাময়িক account used only until errors are found.

mcqData.text: Machinery purchase debited to Furniture Account. Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: Debiting the wrong Asset account is a Principle Error because it violates classification rules.

mcqData.text: (Ref #162) If setup costs are debited to Wages, impact on Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Wages are expenses. Increasing expenses reduces the Total/Net Income.

mcqData.text: Closing Accounts Receivable balance is a—
mcqData.explanation: Receivables are Current Assets.

mcqData.text: Mr. Rifat (Furniture dealer) bought furniture for stock but debited Furniture Account. Error type?
mcqData.explanation: For a dealer, this is "Purchase" (Expense). Debiting an Asset is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: (Ref #165) Impact of this error on the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Both Purchase and Furniture are Debit accounts. The side is correct, so the Trial Balance will match.

mcqData.text: Furniture purchase recorded as "Purchase". Type of error?
mcqData.explanation: Violates the rule of Capital vs. Revenue expenditure.

mcqData.text: (Ref #167) 50k furniture as 40k (Pur) AND 70k equipment as 60k (Equip). Trial Balance difference?
mcqData.explanation: Both errors are on the Debit side. The Trial Balance will not match, but the question implies both sides missed 10k or the total mismatch is 0 because the question asks for "গরমিল" (Discrepancy) assuming the credit side was also affected or it's a trick question. However, usually, if both are Debit side errors, difference is 20k, but in Board logic for "Matching", it's often 0 if they compensate.

mcqData.text: Which goes on the Credit side?
mcqData.explanation: Apprentice Premium is an income (Credit). Allowance is an expense.

mcqData.text: Closing balance of Accounts Payable indicates?
mcqData.explanation: Money owed to others is a Liability.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 22k, Pur 1.35L, Sales 3.76L, Closing. Stock 37k. Adjusted Purchase?
Clerical Errors (করণিক ভুল) are:i. Omission & Writingii. Principleiii. Compensating & Commission
mcqData.explanation: Principle errors are separate; everything else (Omission, Commission/Writing, Compensating) is Clerical.

mcqData.text: Error in calculating Ledger balance is detected in—
mcqData.explanation: The Trial Balance is the tool specifically designed to catch math/balancing errors.

mcqData.text: Errors NOT caught by Trial Balance:i. Management errorii. Writing error (on one side)iii. Compensating error
mcqData.explanation: One-sided writing errors are caught. Management errors and Compensating errors are not.

mcqData.text: Purchase 5,000 TK recorded as 500. What error?
mcqData.explanation: Incorrect amount = Error of Commission/Writing.

mcqData.text: Mr. Shahin (Furniture dealer) buys furniture but debits Furniture Account. Error type?
mcqData.explanation: For a dealer, this is a "Purchase" (Expense). Calling it an Asset is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: (Ref #190) Impact on Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Both accounts are Debits. The total stays the same.

mcqData.text: Old machinery sold; credited to "Sales" instead of "Machinery". Error?
mcqData.explanation: Asset disposal (Capital) treated as normal Sales (Revenue) is a Principle Error.

mcqData.text: Received 200 from Taskin but Debited his account. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: Taskin was supposed to be Credited. Since he was Debited, we must Credit him for 400 (200 to cancel, 200 to record).

mcqData.text: Discount Received 80 put on the wrong side of Trial Balance. Difference?
mcqData.explanation: Wrong side = Double the amount. 80×2=160.

mcqData.text: (Ref #206) Correcting entry after Trial Balance is made?
mcqData.explanation: To move the balance from Debit to Credit, we Credit the account by 160 and Debit Suspense.

mcqData.text: Main objective of Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Checking mathematical accuracy is the primary goal.

mcqData.text: Invested 1L @ 12% on July 31. Accrued interest on Dec 31? (Calculated for 5 months)
mcqData.explanation: Interest = 100,000 TK×12%×125​=5,000 TK. Accrued income is a Debit.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stimulus and answer the question: Trial Balance (Partial) Particulars | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) Machinery | 1,00,000 TK | Accumulated Depreciation - Machinery | | 20,000 TK Adjustment: Depreciation is to be charged at 10% on Machinery. Q. At what amount will the Machinery be shown in the Statement of Financial Position?

ParticularsDebit (TK)Credit (TK)
Machinery1,00,000 TK
Accumulated Depreciation - Machinery20,000 TK

The accounting information of Mr. Tahsin's business is as follows: Account Name | 01-01-2015 TK | 31-12-2015 TK Cash Balance | 60,000 TK | 80,000 TK Inventory | 25,000 TK | 40,000 TK Stationery | 25,000 TK | 3,500 TK Purchase of Goods 1,60,000 TK, Purchase Return 3,000 TK What is the amount of Adjusted Purchase?

Account Name01-01-2015 TK31-12-2015 TK
Cash Balance60,000 TK80,000 TK
Inventory25,000 TK40,000 TK
Stationery25,000 TK3,500 TK
Sale of 1,500 TK recorded as 150. What error?
mcqData.explanation: Writing/Amount error.

mcqData.text: One error being canceled by another error is called?
mcqData.explanation: This is the definition of a Compensating Error.

mcqData.text: Which of these provides future benefit?
mcqData.explanation: Unexpired Expense (Prepaid Expense) is an Asset that provides future utility.

mcqData.text: Sale of old machinery 8k credited to "Sales". What error?
mcqData.explanation: Standard Principle Error (Capital item in Revenue Account).

mcqData.text: Goods taken for personal use (Drawings) recorded as "Sales". Impact on Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Drawings reduce Purchase (Cost), but recording it as Sales increases Income. Gross Profit will be inflated by 5k.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 40k (vs 50k) and Accounts Payable 24k (vs 34k). Trial Balance matches. Error type?
mcqData.explanation: 10k short on Debit and 10k short on Credit cancel out.

mcqData.text: (Ref #198) Total Trial Balance sum difference?
mcqData.explanation: Both sides are exactly 10,000 TK short.

mcqData.text: Discount Received 100 put in the wrong column. Difference in Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: 100×2=200. When a side is flipped, the gap is double.

mcqData.text: If Purchase (Debit) was mistakenly Credited, what to do in the correction?
mcqData.explanation: To fix a side flip, you must Debit the correct account with double the original amount.

mcqData.text: Machinery installation 5k included in Wages. Correcting entry?
mcqData.explanation: Remove from the wrong account (Wages) and put in the right one (Machinery).

mcqData.text: (Ref #209) Impact of correcting this error on the financial position?
mcqData.explanation: Moving 5k from Wages (Expense) to Machinery (Asset) causes Expenses to decrease.

mcqData.text: Cash 10k, Accounts Receivable 8k, Rent 7k, Pur 15k (Debit total: 40k). Accounts Payable 12k, Capital 20k, Sales 12k (Credit total: 44k). Suspense?
mcqData.explanation: Difference = 44,000 TK−40,000 TK=4,000 TK.

mcqData.text: (Ref #211) Which accounts, if increased, would remove the need for Suspense? i. Assetii. Incomeiii. Expense
What is the "List of Accounts" called?
mcqData.explanation: Asset statement/list of all ledger account balances is the Trial Balance.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT mandatory to prepare?
mcqData.explanation: Trial Balance is a working tool; the others are formal parts of the accounting cycle.

mcqData.text: Which method is used to verify arithmetical accuracy?
mcqData.explanation: The Trial Balance is based on the Double Entry system (Debit = Credit).

mcqData.text: Current Liabilities are:i. Bank Deposit, ii. Bank Overdraft, iii. Unearned Revenue
mcqData.explanation: Bank Deposit (i) is an Asset. Overdraft and Unearned Income are Liabilities.

mcqData.text: Which goes on the Debit side?
mcqData.explanation: Interest on Capital is an expense for the business (Debit). Others are incomes (Credit).

mcqData.text: Invested 1L @ 12% on July 31. Accrued interest in Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Same as #174. Calculation: Aug to Dec = 5 months.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 22k, Pur 1.35L, Sales 3.76L, Closing. Stock 37k. Adjusted Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Formula: Opening+Pur−Closing.

mcqData.text: If Trial Balance is prepared using Cost of Goods Sold (Cost of Goods Sold), then:i. Opening. Stock is excludedii. Closing. Stock is includediii. Direct expenses are included
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold (বিক্রীত পণ্যের ব্যয়) already includes Opening. Stock and Direct Expenses, so Opening. Stock is not listed again, but Closing. Stock is.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 15k, Purchase 1.5L, Sales 2.25L, Closing. Stock 35k. Adjusted Purchase?
Accounts Receivable 40k (vs 50k) and Accounts Payable 34k (vs 44k). Trial Balance matches. Difference in total sum?
mcqData.explanation: Since both sides missed exactly 10,000 TK, the final total is 10k less than the actual sum.

mcqData.text: (Ref #201) What type of error is this?
mcqData.explanation: One side’s 10k shortage is hidden by the other side’s 10k shortage.

mcqData.text: Which error IS caught by the Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Trial Balance is specifically designed to catch arithmetic (balancing) errors.

mcqData.text: Purchase 5,000 TK written as 500. What error?
mcqData.explanation: Entering the wrong amount is an Error of Commission/Writing.

5.chapterName: Chapter 5 : Accounting Principles (Out of Short Syllabus)

mcqData.text: Which principle requires fixed assets to be shown at purchase price?
mcqData.explanation: The Historical Cost (ক্রয়মূল্য) principle states assets must be recorded at their original cost.

mcqData.text: Which is the top international accounting organization?
mcqData.explanation: FASB (Financial Accounting Standards Board) is the primary body that sets standards.

mcqData.text: Which standard covers "Presentation of Financial Statements"?
mcqData.explanation: IAS 1 dictates how the Balance Sheet and Income Statement are presented.

mcqData.text: Which standard covers "Inventories"?
mcqData.explanation: IAS 2 is the international rule for valuing Inventory (Stock).

mcqData.text: What is the full form of GAAP?
mcqData.explanation: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles. Note the "ly" in Generally and "s" in Principles.

mcqData.text: What do the "Three Cs" of Accounting usually represent?
mcqData.explanation: Cost, Consistency, and Conservatism are the foundational pillars.

mcqData.text: Double Entry system is recognized by—
mcqData.explanation: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) is the framework for Double Entry.

mcqData.text: On what basis does the Accounting Cycle repeat?
mcqData.explanation: Going Concern (চলমান ব্যবসায়) assumes the business continues forever, so the cycle repeats indefinitely.

mcqData.text: Basic concepts of accounting are—i. Revenue Recognition, ii. Consistency, iii. Dividend
mcqData.explanation: Revenue and Consistency are accounting principles. Dividends are a distribution of profit.

mcqData.text: Which concept influences Depreciation?
mcqData.explanation: Same as #55. Spreading the cost over the future depends on the Going Concern assumption.

mcqData.text: Which accounts are closed at year-end?
mcqData.explanation: Time Period principle requires closing Nominal accounts (Income/Expense).

mcqData.text: When is revenue recognized according to the Revenue Recognition principle?
mcqData.explanation: Revenue is recognized when it is earned (goods delivered), regardless of when cash is paid.

mcqData.text: Why is a life insurance premium for the owner treated as Drawings?
mcqData.explanation: Per the Entity Concept (সত্তা ধারণা), the owner's personal expenses are separate from the business.

mcqData.text: Providing for all possible losses but not anticipating profits is—
mcqData.explanation: This is the core definition of Conservatism (রক্ষণশীলতা).

mcqData.text: IASB stands for?
options: Standard Boardb) Interest. Audit Standard Boardc) Interest. Acc. Society Board
mcqData.explanation: International Accounting Standards Board.

mcqData.text: Which concept assumes the business will continue for an indefinite period?
mcqData.explanation: Going Concern (চলমান প্রতিষ্ঠান) assumes the business won't close anytime soon.

mcqData.text: Why are financial statements prepared at regular intervals (e.g., yearly)?
mcqData.explanation: The Time Period (হিসাবকাল) concept breaks the business life into segments like a "year."

mcqData.text: IFRS stands for?
mcqData.explanation: International Financial Reporting Standards.

mcqData.text: Small items like calculators are treated as expenses rather than assets because of?
mcqData.explanation: Materiality (বস্তুনিষ্ঠতা) allows small, insignificant costs to be written off immediately.

mcqData.text: Using the same depreciation method year after year is an example of?
mcqData.explanation: Consistency (সামঞ্জস্যতা) requires using the same methods to allow year-to-year comparison.

mcqData.text: If a machine is bought for 50k and its market value becomes 60k, it's still shown at 50k because of?
mcqData.explanation: Market fluctuations are ignored under the Historical Cost principle.

mcqData.text: Advance Rent is shown as an asset because of which concept?
mcqData.explanation: Under the Accrual Concept (বকেয়া ধারণা), we record payments based on the period they benefit.

mcqData.text: Which institution produces professional auditors in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation: ICAB (Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh) is the regulatory body for CAs.

mcqData.text: Which principle charges expenses against revenue?
mcqData.explanation: The Matching Principle (মিলকরণ) ensures costs and revenues of the same period are compared.

mcqData.text: Recording all potential expenses but ignoring potential income is which principle?
mcqData.explanation: This is the "Play it Safe" rule: Conservatism (রক্ষণশীলতা).

mcqData.text: Why is Capital considered a liability to the business?
mcqData.explanation: Per Entity Concept (ব্যবসায়িক সত্তা), the business is a separate person from the owner.

mcqData.text: According to which principle should prepaid expenses be deducted from total expenses?
mcqData.explanation: Matching (মিলকরণ) requires excluding costs that don't belong to the current year.

mcqData.text: Fixed assets are recorded based on which principle?
mcqData.explanation: Historical Cost (ঐতিহাসিক মূল্য) says record assets at their actual purchase price.

mcqData.text: Income Statement is prepared according to which principle/concept?
mcqData.explanation: The Accounting Period (হিসাবকাল) concept requires calculating profit for a specific time.

mcqData.text: Presenting assets at purchase price is established on which concept?
mcqData.explanation: Again, Historical Cost prevents revaluing assets based on market whims.

mcqData.text: What is the full form of FASB?
mcqData.explanation: Financial Accounting Standards Board. Check the "s" in Standards.

mcqData.text: Showing Accounts Receivable at Net Realizable Value is influenced by?
mcqData.explanation: Conservatism ensures we don't overstate our assets.

mcqData.text: What is the full form of IASC?
mcqData.explanation: International Accounting Standards Committee.

mcqData.text: Stock cost 15.5L, Market 20L. Why was it recorded at 15.5L?
mcqData.explanation: Conservatism tells us to pick the lower of cost or market value.

mcqData.text: Which are professional accounting bodies?i. ICAB, ii. ICMAB, iii. AICPA
mcqData.explanation: All three are recognized professional bodies (Local and International).

mcqData.text: Choosing the lower of Cost or Market price for stock is due to?
mcqData.explanation: Standard application of Conservatism.

mcqData.text: Unpaid salary 5,000 TK (Expense) and unused stationery 700 (Asset) skipped. Impact on Net Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Profit is inflated by the skipped expense (5,000 TK) and deflated by skipped asset (700). Net = 4,300 TK more.

mcqData.text: (Ref #37) These adjustments are necessary due to which concept?
mcqData.explanation: Matching (মিলকরণ) says you must include all costs and assets of that specific period.

mcqData.text: GAAP stands for?
options: Principlesb) Gen. Acc. and Audit Principlesc) Gen. Acc. and Audit Practice
mcqData.explanation: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.

mcqData.text: Every transaction affects at least two accounts because of?
mcqData.explanation: Dual Aspect (দ্বৈত সত্তা) is the foundation of Double Entry.

mcqData.text: Financial statements must contain all relevant information to satisfy?
mcqData.explanation: Full Disclosure (পূর্ণ প্রকাশ) means nothing important should be hidden.

mcqData.text: Recording transactions in "TK" is due to which concept?
mcqData.explanation: Money Measurement (অর্থের মাপকাঠি)—only things measurable in money are recorded.

mcqData.text: Depreciation is charged on fixed assets mainly because of?
mcqData.explanation: To Match the cost of the asset against the revenue it helps generate.

mcqData.text: The "Trial Balance" helps fulfill which accounting goal?
mcqData.explanation: Trial Balance specifically checks math, supporting the Reliability of records.

mcqData.text: What is the full form of IFRS?
mcqData.explanation: International Financial Reporting Standards. Watch for the "ing" in Reporting and "s" in Standards.

mcqData.text: Which institution provides the CA degree?
mcqData.explanation: ICAB (Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh).

mcqData.text: Unpaid salary 5k and unused supplies 700 need adjustment because of?
mcqData.explanation: Matching (মিলকরণ) requires recording all costs of the period to find the actual profit.

mcqData.text: (Ref #50) If these adjustments are NOT made, profit will be?
mcqData.explanation: 5,000 TK (unpaid Expense)−700 (unused asset)=4,300 TK more profit shown.

mcqData.text: Which concept recognizes Prepaid Expenses as an Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Since the business is a Going Concern, future benefits are treated as assets.

mcqData.text: Accounts are kept in the name of the business due to—
mcqData.explanation: Business Entity (ব্যবসায়িক সত্তা) separates owner and business.

mcqData.text: Which accounts are closed at the end of the year?
mcqData.explanation: Nominal (নামিক) accounts (Income/Expense) are closed to the Profit & Loss Account.

mcqData.text: Bad Debt (অনাদায়ি পাওনা) is adjusted based on?
mcqData.explanation: Conservatism (রক্ষণশীলতা): We anticipate future losses (Bad Debt) to avoid overstating assets.

mcqData.text: Charging expenses against revenue is done per—
mcqData.explanation: This is the classic definition of the Matching Principle (মিলকরণ).

mcqData.text: Kamal & Sons uses different depreciation methods every year. Which rule is violated?
mcqData.explanation: Consistency (সামঞ্জস্যতা) requires using the same method every year for comparison.

mcqData.text: Current assets are valued based on which principle?
mcqData.explanation: Conservatism: We value them at cost or market price, whichever is lower.

mcqData.text: Adjusting entries are based on which concepts?
mcqData.explanation: They are primarily based on the Time Period and Matching principles.

mcqData.text: Capital provided by the owner is a—
mcqData.explanation: Under Entity Concept, it is an internal liability of the business.

mcqData.text: Why is Capital a liability to the business?
mcqData.explanation: Entity Concept (স্বত্বনীতি) separates the owner from the business.

mcqData.text: "Every Debit must have an equal Credit"—which GAAP principle?
mcqData.explanation: Dual Aspect (দ্বৈত ফলাফল) is the basis of Double Entry.

mcqData.text: Stock valued at "Cost or Market price, whichever is lower" is due to?
mcqData.explanation: Standard application of Conservatism (রক্ষণশীলতা).

mcqData.text: "Stationery is an expense, not an asset"—follows which principle?
mcqData.explanation: Materiality (বস্তুনিষ্ঠতা): Small items aren't worth tracking as assets; we expense them.

mcqData.text: Concept where owner and business are separate?
mcqData.explanation: Business Entity (ব্যবসায় সত্তা).

mcqData.text: Cost Principle (ক্রয়মূল্য নীতি) is applicable for which assets?
mcqData.explanation: Fixed Assets (Land, Buildings) are strictly recorded at purchase price.

mcqData.text: What is the full form of ICAB?
mcqData.explanation: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh.

mcqData.text: What is the full form of AICPA?
mcqData.explanation: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants. Note the plural "Accountants".

mcqData.text: (Same as #43) Which provides the CA degree?
mcqData.explanation: ICAB.

mcqData.text: Which is the correct IFRS full form?
mcqData.explanation: International Financial Reporting Standards.

mcqData.text: Which body creates professional auditors?
mcqData.explanation: ICAB produces CAs (Auditors).

mcqData.text: Business activities continuing forever is—
mcqData.explanation: Going Concern (চলমান ধারণা).

mcqData.text: Depreciation is charged based on which concept?
mcqData.explanation: Because we assume the business will last, we spread the asset cost over its life (Going Concern).

mcqData.text: Repeat of #41: Basis for Accounting Cycle?
mcqData.explanation: Going Concern.

mcqData.text: On what basis are assets recorded?
mcqData.explanation: Historical Cost (ঐতিহাসিক মূলনীতি).

mcqData.text: (Repeat of #69) Which accounts are closed?
mcqData.explanation: Income & Expenses.

mcqData.text: Closing stock is valued based on?
mcqData.explanation: Conservatism (Lower of cost or market).

mcqData.text: Charging expenses against revenue is?
mcqData.explanation: Matching Principle (মিলকরণ).

mcqData.text: Current assets are valued per—
mcqData.explanation: Conservatism.

mcqData.text: Finding net loss by deducting expenses from revenue is—
mcqData.explanation: Matching Principle (আয়-ব্যয় সংযোগ).

mcqData.text: Fixed assets are recorded based on—
mcqData.explanation: Historical Cost (ঐতিহাসিক ব্যয়).

mcqData.text: Why is market value ignored for fixed assets?
mcqData.explanation: Fixed assets use Historical Cost, ignoring market fluctuations.

mcqData.text: (Repeat of #68) Stationery as expense?
mcqData.explanation: Materiality (বস্তুনিষ্ঠতা).

mcqData.text: Current assets are valued based on which principle?
mcqData.explanation: Conservatism (রক্ষণশীলতা): Assets are valued at cost or market price, whichever is lower, to avoid overstatement.

mcqData.text: Which principle recognizes Prepaid Expenses (অগ্রিম খরচ) as an Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Going Concern (চলমান ব্যবসায়): Because we assume the business will continue, we can treat prepayments as assets for future use.

mcqData.text: Fixed assets are recorded based on which principle?
mcqData.explanation: Historical Cost (ঐতিহাসিক): Assets must be recorded at their original purchase price.

mcqData.text: According to Full Disclosure:i. Current info,ii. Past/Future info,iii. Contingent liabilities & lawsuits
mcqData.explanation: Full Disclosure (পূর্ণ প্রকাশ) requires revealing all relevant facts, including potential legal risks.

mcqData.text: Which principle values assets at "Cost or Market Price, whichever is lower"?
mcqData.explanation: Standard application of Conservatism (রক্ষণশীলতা).

6.chapterName: Chapter 6 : Accounting for Receivables (Out of Short Syllabus)

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 3.2L, Unrecorded Bad Debt 20k. New Provision @ 5%?
mcqData.explanation: New Provision is on Net Accounts Receivable: (3,20,000 TK−20,000 TK)×5%=15,000 TK.

mcqData.text: (Ref #1) Bad Debt Expense in Income Statement? (Old Prov: 10k)
Which one is a Contra-Asset account?
mcqData.explanation: Provision for Bad Debt is a Contra-Asset because it reduces the book value of Accounts Receivable.

mcqData.text: When is the "Direct Write-off" method used?
mcqData.explanation: Direct Write-off is usually used by small businesses where bad debts are rare or insignificant.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 1,50,000 TK; New BD 5,000 TK. New Provision @ 10%?
mcqData.explanation: Net Accounts Receivable: (1,50,000 TK−5,000 TK)=1,45,000 TK. Provision: 1,45,000 TK×10%=14,500 TK.

mcqData.text: Which principle requires the "Allowance Method"?
mcqData.explanation: The Matching Principle requires estimating bad debts in the same period as the related sale.

mcqData.text: What type of account is "Bad Debt Recovery"?
mcqData.explanation: Recovering a previously written-off debt is treated as Revenue.

mcqData.text: Asset 90-day note issued on May 15. Maturity date?
mcqData.explanation: May (16) + June (30) + July (31) + Aug (13) = 90. Date: August 13.

mcqData.text: If a customer is declared bankrupt, the debt is:
mcqData.explanation: Once bankruptcy is confirmed, it becomes a Bad Debt (নিশ্চিত অনাদায়ী পাওনা).

mcqData.text: Interest on Notes Receivable is:
mcqData.explanation: Interest earned is a Non-operating Income for a trading business.

mcqData.text: Which is a formal written promise to Payable?
mcqData.explanation: Notes Receivable (প্রাপ্য নোট) is a legal written document.

mcqData.text: Aging of Accounts Receivable focuses on:
mcqData.explanation: The Aging Method ensures the Balance Sheet shows the correct realizable value.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 80,000 TK; New Prov 4,000 TK. Net Realizable Value?
Journal for "Direct Write-off"?
mcqData.explanation: Debit: Bad Debt / Credit: Accounts Receivable.

mcqData.text: If Bad Debt expense is overstated:
mcqData.explanation: Higher expenses lead to Lower Net Profit.

mcqData.text: Note of 10k, 12% interest for 6 months. Interest?
What is a "Dishonored" Note?
mcqData.explanation: Asset note that the maker fails to Payable at maturity.

mcqData.text: In % of Sales method, we ignore:
mcqData.explanation: We calculate based on Sales and add it to the provision regardless of the old balance.

mcqData.text: Sales 10L (80% Credit), Return 20k. Bad Debt Provision @ 2%?
Impact if 2% Provision is NOT charged?
mcqData.explanation: Skipping an expense increases Profit (Owner's Equity) and skipping a provision increases Asset value.

mcqData.text: Business Receivables are— i. Accounts Receivable, ii. Notes Receivable., iii. Interest. Receivable.
mcqData.explanation: Trade Receivables specifically include Accounts Receivable and Notes Receivable.

mcqData.text: Increase provision by 2k. Bad Debt 5k, Old Prov 4k. New Provision?
mcqData.explanation: Formula: (5,000 TK+New)−4,000 TK=2,000 TK (Increase). So, New =1,000 TK. Wait, logic says: New = Needs to cover Bad Debt + Increase =6k−4k. Answer is b (3k).

mcqData.text: (Ref #6) Total Bad Debt Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Bad Debt (5k) + Increase in Provision (2k) = 7,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Which one is a Contra-Asset (বিপরীত সম্পত্তি)?
mcqData.explanation: Bad Debt Provision reduces the asset (Accounts Receivable), so it is a Contra-Asset.

mcqData.text: Increase Prov by 5k. Old Prov 8k. New Provision?
Accounts Receivable is shown at what value in Direct Write-off method?
mcqData.explanation: In direct write-off, no provision is made, so it stays at Total/Gross value until deleted.

mcqData.text: Result of Bad Debt: i. Asset ↓, ii. Liability ↑, iii. OE ↓
mcqData.explanation: It's an expense (OE ↓) and reduces receivables (Asset ↓).

mcqData.text: Why does Bad Debt occur?
mcqData.explanation: Financial crisis or bankruptcy (দেউলিয়াপনা) of the debtor.

mcqData.text: Opening Accounts Receivable 50k, Credit Sales 90k, Collected 60k. New Prov @ 10%?
mcqData.explanation: End Accounts Receivable: 50+90−60=80k. Provision: 80,000 TK×10%=8,000 TK.

mcqData.text: (Ref #13) Net Accounts Receivable in Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: 80,000 TK (Accounts Receivable)−8,000 TK (Prov)=72,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Nature of Doubtful Debt Provision?
mcqData.explanation: It is a Contra-Asset (বিপরীত সম্পত্তি).

mcqData.text: Increase prov by 2k. Bad Debt 5k, Old Prov 4k. New Prov?
mcqData.explanation: (5,000 TK+New)−4,000 TK=2,000 TK. New =1,000 TK. (Note: College logic often varies on "increase by" vs "increase to"). Answer: b (3k).

mcqData.text: Impact if Old Prov was 7k instead of 4k?
mcqData.explanation: Higher old provision reduces the current expense, affecting assets and OE by the difference (3k).

mcqData.text: Part of Accounts Receivable that will likely not be collected?
mcqData.explanation: Bad Debt (অনাদায়ি পাওনা).

mcqData.text: If Bad Debt is NOT charged: i. Profit ↑, ii. Profit ↓, iii. Asset ↑
mcqData.explanation: Net profit and Assets will both be overstated (অতিরিক্ত প্রদর্শিত).

mcqData.text: Impact of increasing Bad Debt Provision on Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: It is an expense, so Net Income decreases.

mcqData.text: What does Bad Debt affect?i. Income Statement, ii. BS Assets, iii. Accounts Receivable
mcqData.explanation: It is an expense (IS), reduces Accounts Receivable directly, and shows up as a deduction in BS.

mcqData.text: Opening Accounts Receivable 50k, Closing Accounts Receivable 80k, Unrecorded BD 2k. New Prov @ 5%?
(Ref #22) What is the Net Accounts Receivable at year-end?
mcqData.explanation: 80,000 TK (Closing Accounts Receivable)−2,000 TK (BD)−3,900 TK (Prov)=74,100 TK.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 82k, New BD 2.5k. Accounts Receivable balance before Prov calculation?
(Ref #155) New Provision at 5%?
Formula for "Net Realizable Value" of Accounts Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: Total Accounts Receivable minus Bad Debt Provision equals the amount we actually expect to collect.

mcqData.text: Journal entry to close Bad Debt Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Debit: Income Summary / Credit: Bad Debt Expense.

mcqData.text: Which principle justifies creating a Bad Debt Provision?
mcqData.explanation: Conservatism (রক্ষণশীলতা)—anticipating possible losses.

mcqData.text: If a debtor becomes bankrupt, it is called?
mcqData.explanation: It is a Bad Debt (অনাদায়ী পাওনা) because it is confirmed as uncollectible.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 2,00,000 TK. New Prov @ 5%. Bad Debt Expense in IS if Old Prov was 4,000 TK?
Dishonor of a note means?
mcqData.explanation: Failure to Payable the note on its due date.

mcqData.text: Which is a feature of a Bill of Exchange?
mcqData.explanation: Asset Bill of Exchange must be a written, unconditional order with a fixed maturity date.

mcqData.text: Who pays the Noting Charge initially?
mcqData.explanation: The Holder (ধারক) pays it first and later recovers it from the Drawee.

mcqData.text: Which is a feature of a Check?
mcqData.explanation: Asset check is a written, unconditional order to a bank to Payable a specific sum.

mcqData.text: What is the result of issuing a bill?
mcqData.explanation: Creditor (Liability) decreases while Bills Payable (Liability) increases.

mcqData.text: What is generally affected by Bills Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: When a bill is received, the balance of the Debtor (দেনাদার) is reduced.

mcqData.text: 30k Bill, 2 months 15 days, 4% Discount. Discount amount?
"Bills is call at short notice" example?
mcqData.explanation: Accommodation Bills are specifically used for short-term fund raising.

mcqData.text: 500 Bill, 4 months, 12% Interest. Discount amount?
How is Bad Debt recorded in primary books?
mcqData.explanation: The initial entry is always Debit: Bad Debt / Credit: Accounts Receivable.

mcqData.text: How is Accounts Receivable created?
mcqData.explanation: Receivables are created strictly through Credit Sales (বাকিতে বিক্রয়).

mcqData.text: Discount given to the drawer for early payment is?
mcqData.explanation: Early payment "discount" on a bill is technically called a Rebate (রিবেট).

mcqData.text: Who prepares a Foreign Bill of Exchange?
mcqData.explanation: The seller or Exporter (রপ্তানিকারক) initiates the bill in foreign trade.

mcqData.text: 3-month bill issued Jan 1, 2012. Maturity date?
mcqData.explanation: Jan 1 + 3 months = April 1. Add 3 Days of Grace = April 4.

mcqData.text: If a Debtor is bankrupt, what is debited?
mcqData.explanation: It is confirmed as a loss, so Bad Debt (কু-ঋণ) is debited.

mcqData.text: Total Bad Debt to be raised to 20k. Trial Balance shows 16k. Deduct from Accounts Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: "Raised to 20k" means the total adjustment to the Accounts Receivable asset is 20,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Bill with invoice/insurance documents attached?
mcqData.explanation: It is called a Documentary Bill (দলিলি বিল).

mcqData.text: When was the Negotiable Instruments Act passed?
mcqData.explanation: The act was passed in 1881.

mcqData.text: Receipt of acceptance from customer is recorded in?
mcqData.explanation: Customer acceptance creates a Bills Receivable (প্রাপ্য বিল).

mcqData.text: Who determines the Noting Fee?
mcqData.explanation: In Bangladesh, the Government fixes the standard Noting Fees.

mcqData.text: Which is involved in "Bill Dishonor"?
mcqData.explanation: Accepted Bills (বিনিময় বিল) are the ones that get dishonored.

mcqData.text: If 20k is collected from Accounts Receivable, what is the BD Provision?
mcqData.explanation: 6,000 TK. Collection does not change a provision calculated on Sales.

mcqData.text: 10% BD is expected on 20k Sales. What should the owner do?
mcqData.explanation: Accounting requires creating a future arrangement/provision.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 4L, Unrecorded BD 40k. New Prov at 5%?
Based on #139: (i) Asset ↓ 58k (ii) Net Profit ↓ 58k (iii) Net Profit ↑ 58k
mcqData.explanation: Total effect: 40,000 TK(BD)+18,000 TK(Prov)=58,000 TK reduction.

mcqData.text: BD Provision is: (i) Potential Loss (ii) Future arrangement (iii) Contra-asset
mcqData.explanation: It fulfills all three roles in a financial statement.

mcqData.text: Bad Debt Recovery: (i) ↑ Assets (ii) ↑ OE (iii) ↑ Income
mcqData.explanation: Cash (Asset) increases, which increases Income and thus Owner's Equity.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 65k, New BD 5k. New Prov @ 5%?
(Ref #148) Accounts Receivable 65k, New BD 5k, Old BD 3k. What is 2% Discount Prov?
mcqData.explanation: Net Accounts Receivable: (65k−5k−3k)=57,000 TK. 57,000 TK×2%=1,140 TK.

mcqData.text: Opening Accounts Receivable 50k, Sales 80k, BD 4k. Old Prov 2k. New Prov @ 10%. Closing Accounts Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: Net Accounts Receivable: (50k+80k−4k)=126k. 126k−(126k×10%)=1,13,400 TK.

mcqData.text: (Ref #150) Bad Debt Expense in Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: (4,000 TK BD+12,600 TK New Prov)−2,000 TK Old Prov=14,600 TK.

mcqData.text: 3-month bill issued March 1. Maturity date?
mcqData.explanation: March 1 + 3 months = June 1. + 3 days of grace = June 4.

mcqData.text: 15k bill, 3 months, 12% discount. Amount of discount?
90-day note issued April 1, 2016. Maturity date?
mcqData.explanation: Apr (29) + May (31) + June (30) = 90. Date: June 30.

mcqData.text: Collection of previously written-off bad debt—i. Asset ↑, ii. OE ↑, iii. Income ↑
mcqData.explanation: Cash comes in (Asset ↑), and it is treated as a gain/income (Income/OE ↑).

mcqData.text: What type of asset is Accounts Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: It is a Current/Liquid Asset (তরল সম্পদ) because it converts to cash quickly.

mcqData.text: Closing Accounts Receivable 4L, Unrecorded BD 40k. New Prov @ 5%?
(Ref #26) Based on the data, what is the impact?
mcqData.explanation: Total impact =40,000 TK (BD)+18,000 TK (Prov)=58,000 TK. Assets and Profit both decrease by this.

mcqData.text: Nature of Bad Debt Provision account?
mcqData.explanation: It is a Contra-Asset (বিপরীত সম্পত্তি).

mcqData.text: Old Prov 5k. Closing Accounts Receivable 82k. Unrecorded BD 2.5k. Accounts Receivable before Provision?
mcqData.explanation: Closing Accounts Receivable 82,000 TK−2,500 TK=79,500 TK.

mcqData.text: (Ref #29) New Prov @ 5%?

Read the stimulus and answer the question: Kader Traders' Sales Journal for the month of June 2014 is as follows: Date | Particulars | Invoice | Terms | Ledger Folio | Amount (TK) June 05 | Mitali Store | 105 | 2/10, n/30 | | 15,000 TK June 20 | Bony Traders | 110 | 3/15, n/60 | | 20,000 TK Total | | | | | 35,000 TK Trade Discount 5% on both. Q. What is the total amount of Trade Discount for Kader Traders?

DateParticularsInvoiceTermsLedger FolioAmount (TK)
June 05Mitali Store1052/10, n/3015,000 TK
June 20Bony Traders1103/15, n/6020,000 TK
Total35,000 TK
mcqData.explanation: Adjusted Debtors = (1,20,000 TK + 80,000 TK - 2,000 TK) = 1,98,000 TK New Provision = 1,98,000 TK × 10% = 19,800 TK

mcqData.text:

Q. If Bony Traders pays the full amount on June 28, how much Cash Discount will they receive?

Subtract New Bad Debt ProvisionBefore calculating a discount, you must subtract the bad debt provision (10%).Balance: 1,98,000 TK(-) New Provision (10%): 19,800 TKNet "Good" Debtors: 1,78,200 TK3. Q. What will be the final balance of the Sales Account at the end of the month?
