mcqData.text: Which of these can be discounted (বাট্টা করা)?
mcqData.explanation: Promissory Notes/Bills of Exchange (প্রতিশ্রুতি পত্র) can be discounted at a bank.

mcqData.text: What is "Noting Charge"?
mcqData.explanation: It's the legal fee paid when a Receivable Note is dishonored (প্রত্যাখ্যান).

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable increases due to:i. Credit Sales, ii. Credit Services, iii. Lending money
mcqData.explanation: Lending money creates a "Loan Receivable," not "Accounts Receivable" (Trade).

mcqData.text: Note signed Mar 1 for 90 days. Maturity date?
mcqData.explanation: Mar (30 left) + Apr (30) + May (30) = 90. May 30.

mcqData.text: Methods for recording Bad Debt?
mcqData.explanation: Direct Write-off (প্রত্যক্ষ) and Allowance/Provision (সঞ্চিতি) methods.

mcqData.text: Increase Prov by 2k. BD 5k, Old Prov 4k. New Prov?
mcqData.explanation: Logic: (5,000 TK+New)−4,000 TK=2,000 TK→New=1,000 TK. Wait, if we use (5k+New)−4k=2k, then New = 1k. If "Increase to 2k" New=2k. Board Ans is b.

mcqData.text: (Ref #39) If Old Prov was 6k, change in Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: If the provision balance is higher, the net asset (Accounts Receivable - Provision) will decrease.

mcqData.text: Opening Prov 1.5k, BD 3k, Accounts Receivable 24k. New Prov 10%. Total Expense?
mcqData.explanation: (3,000 TK BD+2,100 TK New Prov)−1,500 TK Old=3,600 TK.

mcqData.text: What is the nature of the "Provision for Bad Debt" account?
mcqData.explanation: It is a Contra-Asset (বিপরীত সম্পত্তি) because it reduces the balance of Accounts Receivable.

mcqData.text: Which method of bad debt recording violates the "Matching Principle"?
mcqData.explanation: Direct Write-off (প্রত্যক্ষ অবলোপন) records the loss only when it happens, often in a different year than the sale.

mcqData.text: If a 3-month note is issued on Jan 31, what is the maturity date?
mcqData.explanation: Three months from Jan 31 (Feb, Mar, Apr). Since April has 30 days, the date is April 30.

mcqData.text: Which is a "Trade Receivable"?
mcqData.explanation: Accounts Receivable (প্রাপ্য হিসাব) arises directly from the core business of selling goods/services.

mcqData.text: What happens when a Bill of Exchange is "Discounted"?
mcqData.explanation: Discounting (বাট্টাকরণ) means selling the note to a bank for immediate cash at a lower value.

mcqData.text: Journal for creating a Bad Debt Provision?
mcqData.explanation: Debit: Bad Debt Expense / Credit: Provision for Bad Debt.

mcqData.text: Who pays the "Noting Charge" if a bill is dishonored?
mcqData.explanation: Though the drawer pays it initially, the Drawee (দেনারী) is ultimately responsible for the cost.

mcqData.text: What is the main source of Accounts Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: Credit Sales (ধারে বিক্রয়) is the primary origin of Accounts Receivable.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable is created from: (i) Credit Sales (ii) Credit Service (iii) Accrued Interest
mcqData.explanation: All three result in money being owed to the business.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable is created from: (i) Credit Sales (ii) Credit Service (iii) Accrued Interest
mcqData.explanation: Duplicate of 112 (Board repetition).

mcqData.text: Why give cash discounts to Accounts Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: To encourage customers to Payable faster (দ্রুত টাকা পরিশোধ).

mcqData.text: Opening Accounts Receivable 70k, Credit Sales 3L, Cash Coll. 2.8L. Closing Accounts Receivable?
(Ref #119) New Provision @ 5% if New BD is 1,800 TK?
Opening Accounts Receivable 80k, Credit Sales 1.2L, BD 5k. Prov is 5% of Sales. Closing Accounts Receivable?
(Ref #121) What is the New Bad Debt Provision?
mcqData.explanation: Since it is based on Credit Sales: 1,20,000 TK×5%=6,000 TK.

mcqData.text: When Bad Debt is recovered: (i) Debit Accounts Receivable (ii) Credit Allowance for Bad Debt (iii) Debit Cash
mcqData.explanation: Entries: Debit Cash / Credit BD Recovery (or Credit BD Provision in some methods).

mcqData.text: How many methods to record Bad Debt?
mcqData.explanation: The two main methods are Direct Write-off and Allowance Method.

mcqData.text: BD Provision is: (i) Potential Loss (ii) Future arrangement (iii) Contra-asset
mcqData.explanation: It meets all three criteria in accounting theory.

mcqData.text: Asset note for 60 days issued on June 10. Maturity date?
mcqData.explanation: June (20 days) + July (31 days) + Aug (9 days) = 60 days. Aug 9.

mcqData.text: "Provision for Bad Debt" is shown in which statement?
mcqData.explanation: It appears as an Expense in the Income Statement and a Deduction in the Balance Sheet.

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a feature of a Promissory Note?
mcqData.explanation: Asset Promissory Note must be Unconditional (শর্তহীন).

mcqData.text: When a written-off debt is recovered, which account is credited?
mcqData.explanation: Bad Debt Recovery (অনাদায়ী পাওনা আদায়) is treated as an Income.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 1,00,000 TK, Provision (Old) 5,000 TK. New Provision needed 7,000 TK. Amount in IS?
mcqData.explanation: New Provision (7,000 TK) - Old Provision (5,000 TK) = 2,000 TK additional expense.

mcqData.text: If Accounts Receivable is 50,000 TK and 2,000 TK is uncollectible, journal entry?
mcqData.explanation: Debit: Bad Debt (অনাদায়ী পাওনা) / Credit: Accounts Receivable.

mcqData.text: Difference between Accounts Receivable and Notes Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: Notes Receivable are formal, written, have specific dates, and usually carry interest.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable is shown in the Balance Sheet at:
mcqData.explanation: Net Realizable Value (Total Accounts Receivable - Provision).

mcqData.text: Sales 1.05L, Sales Return 3.5k, Sales Disc 1.5k. Net Credit Sales?
(Ref #127) New Provision at 10% of Net Sales?
Accounts Receivable 30k, New BD 2k. What is 10% Discount Provision?
mcqData.explanation: (30,000 TK−2,000 TK)×10%=2,800 TK. (Ignore old BD in Trial Balance).

mcqData.text: Old Prov 6.5k. Accounts Receivable 32.5k (after 2.5k BD). Reduce Prov by 5%?
(Ref #131) Amount shown in Income Statement?
Must increase Prov by 2k. Old Prov 4k. BD 5k. New Prov?
mcqData.explanation: 4,000 TK(Old)+2,000 TK(Increase)=6,000 TK.

mcqData.text: If Opening Prov was 6k instead of 4k, what changes in BS?
mcqData.explanation: Higher provision reduces net assets and capital/OE by 2k.

mcqData.text: Nature of Doubtful Debt Provision?
mcqData.explanation: It is a Contra-Asset (বিপরীত সম্পত্তি).

mcqData.text: BD Provision is: (i) Future Arrangement (ii) Reserve (iii) Estimated Expense
mcqData.explanation: All three terms describe the Provision's purpose.

mcqData.text: 90-day note issued March 1, 2014. Maturity date?
mcqData.explanation: Mar (30 days left) + Apr (30) + May (30) = 90 days. Date: May 30.

mcqData.text: Journal to close Bad Debt Recovery?
options: Debit BD Receivable / Credit Income Summ
Debit Income Summ / Credit BD Rec
Debit Accounts Receivable / Credit BD Receivable
mcqData.explanation: Debit: Bad Debt Recovery / Credit: Income Summary.

mcqData.text: Notes Receivable is a—
mcqData.explanation: It is a Current Asset (চলতি সম্পদ) if maturity is within a year.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 60,000 TK; New BD 2,000 TK; Old Prov 1,500 TK. New Prov @ 5%?
(Ref #99) Bad Debt Expense in Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: (2,000 TK New BD+2,900 TK New Prov)−1,500 TK Old Prov=3,400 TK.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 31,500 TK, New Bad Debt (BD) 1,800 TK. What is the 5% Provision balance?
12k written off in Aug is recovered in Sept. Sept Debtors will—
mcqData.explanation: Recovery of a previously written-off debt does not affect the current Accounts Receivable balance.

mcqData.text: Provision created for future potential losses is—
mcqData.explanation: Bad Debt Provision is a contra-asset created to handle future uncertainty.

mcqData.text: 40,000 TK Bill, 3 months, 5% interest. Discount amount?
If previously written-off bad debt is recovered?
mcqData.explanation: Cash (Asset) increases and Other Income (Owner's Equity) increases.

mcqData.text: What is "Provision for Bad Debt"?
mcqData.explanation: It is an Advance Arrangement for expected future losses.

mcqData.text: How many main parties are in a Bill of Exchange?
mcqData.explanation: The three parties are: Drawer, Drawee, and Payee. (Note: Primary parties are 2).

mcqData.text: How many "Days of Grace" for a Time Bill?
mcqData.explanation: Legally, 3 days of grace are added to the maturity date.

mcqData.text: 3-month bill issued June 1. Maturity date?
mcqData.explanation: June 1 + 3 months = Sept 1. Add 3 days = Sept 4.

mcqData.text: Opening Accounts Receivable 60k, Closing Accounts Receivable 45k, Collected 86k. Amount of Credit Sales?
10k Note, 4 months, 9% interest. Interest amount?
mcqData.explanation: Interest: 10,000 TK×9%×(4/12)=300. Date: July 1 + 4 months = Nov 1.

mcqData.text: Closing balance of Accounts Receivable is a—
mcqData.explanation: It is a Current Asset (চলতি সম্পদ).

mcqData.text: Opening Accounts Receivable 50k, Sales 80k, BD 4k. New Prov 10%. Closing Net Accounts Receivable?
(Ref #137) Amount of Bad Debt Expense?
Accounts Receivable 4L, New Bad Debt (BD) 40k. What is the 5% Provision?
Based on #141: (i) Asset ↓ 58k (ii) Profit ↑ 58k (iii) Profit ↓ 58k
mcqData.explanation: Total impact = 40,000 TK(BD)+18,000 TK(Prov)=58,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Which expense does NOT require cash outflow?
mcqData.explanation: Bad Debt (অনাদায়ী পাওনা) is a non-cash expense.

mcqData.text: What is Bad Debt?
mcqData.explanation: The specific portion of Accounts Receivable that will never be recovered.

mcqData.text: Bad Debt Provision is an—
mcqData.explanation: It is an Advance Arrangement (আগাম ব্যবস্থা) for future risks.

mcqData.text: Must increase Prov by 2k. Old Prov 4k. New Prov?
mcqData.explanation: 4,000 TK(Old)+2,000 TK(Increase)=6,000 TK.

mcqData.text: If Opening Prov was 6k (instead of 4k), what happens to BS?
mcqData.explanation: Higher provision reduces net assets and equity by the difference (2k).

mcqData.text: The accounting period for preparing a worksheet can be— i. One year ii. One month iii. Six months
mcqData.explanation: Asset worksheet can be prepared for any duration (monthly, quarterly, or yearly) depending on management needs.

mcqData.text: Which one is a Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Expenditures that provide long-term benefits, such as buying fixed assets, are capital expenditures.

mcqData.text: Closing entries are given to— i. Close nominal accounts ii. Close permanent accounts iii. Close drawing accounts
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries transfer balances of nominal (income/expense) and drawing accounts to zero; permanent accounts are not closed.

mcqData.text: Examples of capital expenditure are— i. Purchase of plant ii. Plant installation cost iii. Plant repair cost
mcqData.explanation: Asset purchase and installation are capital costs. General repairs are considered revenue expenditures.

mcqData.text: When are reversing entries provided?
mcqData.explanation: Reversing entries are made on the first day of the new period to simplify the recording of subsequent transactions.

mcqData.text: Which one is a Capital Income?
mcqData.explanation: Share premium is a non-recurring receipt from a long-term source, making it capital in nature.

mcqData.text: Asset worksheet can be of— i. 10 columns ii. 12 columns iii. 14 columns
mcqData.explanation: While 8, 10, and 12 columns are standard, 14-column worksheets are used when extra data is required.

mcqData.text: What type of expense is Research and Development?
mcqData.explanation: Expenses that provide benefits over several years but are revenue in nature are called deferred revenue expenditures.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Revenue Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Insurance is a recurring operating cost, whereas the others are part of the asset's cost (capital).

mcqData.text: Money received from selling old furniture is—
mcqData.explanation: The entire amount received from selling a fixed asset is a capital receipt.

mcqData.text: What is the total Service Revenue? (Based on stem)
mcqData.explanation: Service Revenue 50,000 TK + Accrued 5,000 TK + Earned portion of Unearned 9,000 TK (16,000 TK-7,000 TK) = 64,000 TK

mcqData.text: If 1/4 of Unearned Revenue is earned, what is the Net Service Revenue?
Amortization of leasehold property is—
mcqData.explanation: Annual wear and tear or expiration of a fixed asset's value is treated as a revenue expense.

mcqData.text: Which one is a Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Installation increases asset value and capacity, qualifying as capital expenditure.

mcqData.text: Which entry makes the balance of income-expense accounts zero?
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries transfer nominal account balances to the Income Summary to reset them for the next period.

mcqData.text: Through which entry are revenue accounts closed?
mcqData.explanation: Nominal (revenue/expense) accounts are always closed using closing entries at year-end.

mcqData.text: Which entry is given at the end of the period for accrued income, unearned income, etc.?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting entries ensure the matching principle is followed by recording revenues and expenses in the correct period.

mcqData.text: When unearned income is adjusted, what happens?
mcqData.explanation: As unearned revenue is earned, the liability is removed and recognized as actual income.

mcqData.text: Closing entries are provided for— i. Closing nominal accounts ii. Closing drawing accounts iii. Closing permanent accounts
mcqData.explanation: Only temporary accounts (nominal and drawings) are closed. Assets, liabilities, and equity (permanent) remain.

mcqData.text: Which one is an optional step in the accounting cycle?
mcqData.explanation: While helpful for preparing final accounts, a worksheet is not a mandatory legal requirement of the cycle.

mcqData.text: Where is "Drawings" shown in a worksheet?
options: Debit of Statement of Financial Position
Credit of Statement of Financial Position
mcqData.explanation: Drawings reduce equity and are shown on the debit side of the Balance Sheet (Financial Position) columns.

mcqData.text: Which account balance becomes zero after closing entries?
mcqData.explanation: Insurance Premium is a nominal (expense) account, which is closed at year-end. Others are permanent/real accounts.

mcqData.text: If goods are sold at cost (no profit), the effect is— i. Expense decreases ii. Income decreases iii. Assets decrease
mcqData.explanation: Selling at cost reduces purchase (expense) and results in no profit/income. Asset (stock) simply changes to cash.

mcqData.text: Examples of deferred expenses are— i. Deferred Advertisement ii. Research & Development iii. Preliminary Expenses
mcqData.explanation: All three are types of revenue expenditures whose benefits extend over multiple accounting periods.

mcqData.text: When are reversing entries provided?
mcqData.explanation: Reversing entries are traditionally recorded on the first day of the new accounting cycle.

mcqData.text: Which entry is required to close revenue accounts?
mcqData.explanation: Closing journals are specifically used to reset nominal accounts (income/expense) to zero.

mcqData.text: Trial Balance shows Office Supplies 1,000 TK. Ending Supplies in adjustment is 600. The adjusting entry is—
mcqData.explanation: The used portion (1,000 TK - 600 = 400) is the expense. Journal: Supplies Expense Debit. 400, Office Supplies Credit. 400.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is considered an asset of the business?
mcqData.explanation: Capital expenditures result in the acquisition of fixed assets, which provide long-term service.

mcqData.text: Which one is a Capital Income?
mcqData.explanation: Selling land is a non-recurring event involving a fixed asset, resulting in a capital receipt/income.

mcqData.text: What cannot be known from a worksheet?
mcqData.explanation: While a worksheet calculates Net Profit and identifies assets/liabilities, it doesn't typically isolate Gross Profit like a Trading Account.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT in an 8-column worksheet?
mcqData.explanation: An 8-column worksheet skips the Adjusted Trial Balance column to save space.

mcqData.text: If an adjusting entry for prepaid expense is omitted, what is the effect?
mcqData.explanation: Omitting the adjustment fails to recognize the expense (overstating income) and fails to reduce the asset (overstating assets).

mcqData.text: Adjusting entries ensure— i. Recognizing expense when incurred ii. Recording revenue when earned iii. Showing correct income statement
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting entries are the foundation of the accrual basis, ensuring all revenues and expenses are matched correctly.

mcqData.text: Which account does NOT require a closing entry?
mcqData.explanation: Accumulated Depreciation is a contra-asset (permanent account), so it is not closed at year-end.

mcqData.text: Which statement about the "Accrual Basis of Accounting" is correct?
options: Recorded only when cash is received
mcqData.explanation: The accrual basis recognizes revenue and expenses when they occur, regardless of when cash changes hands.

mcqData.text: Which balance becomes zero after preparing closing entries?
mcqData.explanation: Only nominal accounts like Service Revenue are zeroed out to the Income Summary.

mcqData.text: When is a reversing entry made?
mcqData.explanation: It is performed at the very beginning of a new period to reverse certain adjusting entries from the prior period.

mcqData.text: What can be known through preparing a worksheet? i. Net profit or loss ii. State of assets/Liabilities iii. Account balances
mcqData.explanation: Asset worksheet summarizes the Trial Balance, Adjustments, Income Statement, and Balance Sheet all in one place.

mcqData.text: Out of 900 commission received, if 100 is unearned, what is the revenue income?
mcqData.explanation: Revenue Income = Total Received (900) - Unearned (100) = 800 TK

mcqData.text: When are reversing entries given?
options: Start or end of any time
mcqData.explanation: They are specific to the start of a new accounting cycle/period.

mcqData.text: Acquiring a fixed asset is called—
mcqData.explanation: Costs incurred to acquire assets that provide long-term benefits are classified as capital expenditures.

mcqData.text: The necessity of closing entries is to— i. Close fixed asset accounts ii. Close nominal accounts iii. Close drawing accounts
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries reset temporary accounts (Income, Expense, Drawings) to zero; fixed assets (permanent) are never closed.

mcqData.text: If the adjusting entry for accrued income is omitted— i. Profit will be understated ii. Income will be understated iii. Equity will be understated
mcqData.explanation: Failing to record earned income results in lower reported profit, which in turn understates the owner's equity.

mcqData.text: Which entry is required to close revenue and expense accounts?
mcqData.explanation: Closing journals transfer nominal account balances to the Income Summary to prepare for the next period.

mcqData.text: Which account requires a closing entry?
mcqData.explanation: Drawings is a temporary account that must be closed against the Capital account at the end of the year.

mcqData.text: What is a worksheet in a true sense?
mcqData.explanation: Asset worksheet is an informal draft used by accountants to simplify the preparation of formal financial statements.

mcqData.text: Which account balance becomes zero after providing closing entries?
mcqData.explanation: Service Revenue is a nominal account. Nominal accounts are the only ones zeroed out via closing entries.

mcqData.text: Which one is different from the other three?
mcqData.explanation: Apprentice Premium is an "Income" for the business, while the other three are "Expenses."

mcqData.text: What is indicated by the adjustment of unearned income?
mcqData.explanation: When unearned income is earned, the liability (Unearned) decreases and the actual Revenue (Income) increases.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an example of Revenue Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Regular maintenance is a recurring expense (revenue), whereas purchase-related costs are capitalized.

mcqData.text: What type of journal is used to update accounts at the end of the period?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting entries are used to bring account balances up to date under the accrual basis of accounting.

mcqData.text: Adjusting advanced (unearned) income—
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting unearned income recognizes that a liability has now been converted into earned revenue.

mcqData.text: Which step of the accounting cycle is optional?
mcqData.explanation: The worksheet is a tool for the accountant's convenience and is not a mandatory part of the formal cycle.

mcqData.text: For which type of accounts are closing entries prepared?
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries are only for nominal accounts (Income and Expenses) and the Drawings account.

mcqData.text: Which heading is NOT required in an 8-column worksheet?
mcqData.explanation: An 8-column worksheet moves directly from adjustments to the financial statement columns, skipping the adjusted trial balance.

mcqData.text: Closing entries are provided to— i. Finish income accounts ii. Finish expense accounts iii. Finish drawing accounts
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries "finish" or zero out all temporary accounts for the current period.

mcqData.text: Adjusting entries implement which accounting principles? i. Accounting Period Concept ii. Accrual Basis iii. Revenue Recognition
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting entries ensure that revenues and expenses are matched to the correct time period (Accrual/Matching).

mcqData.text: Which is included in Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Costs necessary to get a fixed asset ready for its intended use (like installation) are capitalized.

mcqData.text: Which columns are included in a 10-column worksheet? i. Income Statement ii. Retained Earnings Statement iii. Statement of Financial Position
mcqData.explanation: Asset standard 10-column worksheet includes Trial Balance, Adjustments, Adjusted Trial Balance, Income Statement, and Balance Sheet.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Improving land increases its value and utility over the long term, making it a capital expenditure.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Revenue Income?
mcqData.explanation: Interest received is a recurring operating income, making it revenue-based. Others are capital items.

mcqData.text: Which of these is a part of a Worksheet? i. Trial Balance ii. Ledger iii. Journal
mcqData.explanation: Asset worksheet starts with a Trial Balance as its first column. Ledger and Journal are separate books.

mcqData.text: What is the reversing entry for Accrued Salaries?
mcqData.explanation: Asset reversing entry is the opposite of an adjusting entry. It clears the liability at the start of the next period.

mcqData.text: Trial Balance shows Salary 20,000 TK and Accrued Salary 5,000 TK. How much is shown in the Adjusted Trial Balance column?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusted amount = Trial Balance (20,000 TK) + Adjustment (5,000 TK) = 25,000 TK.

mcqData.text: What is the adjusting entry for Accrued Salaries?
mcqData.explanation: To record an expense that has been incurred but not yet paid, we debit the expense and credit the liability.

mcqData.text: Which account is closed with a closing entry at the end of the period?
mcqData.explanation: Revenue and Expense accounts (nominal accounts) are closed annually to reset their balances.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT determined in a worksheet?
mcqData.explanation: Worksheets focus on Net Profit calculation; Gross Profit is typically identified in the formal Trading Account.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Deferred Revenue Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Large advertisement costs that benefit the business for several years are treated as deferred revenue.

mcqData.text: Anna Traders buys a building for 2,05,000 TK, pays 1,000 TK for stamps, and 4,000 TK for other contract costs. Total Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Capital Expense = Purchase Price (2,05,000 TK) + Stamp (1,000 TK) + Contract costs (4,000 TK) = 2,10,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Which one is the scientific method of accounting?
mcqData.explanation: Accrual Basis is globally accepted as scientific because it matches revenue and expenses to their actual periods.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT mandatory?
mcqData.explanation: The worksheet is an optional working paper used to simplify the preparation of final accounts.

mcqData.text: When prepaid rent expires, what is credited in the adjusting entry?
mcqData.explanation: As the asset (Prepaid Rent) is used up, it must be reduced by crediting it.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Capital Income?
options: Profit on sale of fixed asset
mcqData.explanation: Profit made above the book value when selling a fixed asset is considered capital income.

mcqData.text: Which accounting system records revenues and expenses related to the current year?
mcqData.explanation: The accrual basis records transactions when they occur, regardless of the timing of cash flows.

mcqData.text: What is the reversing entry for Accrued Interest on Loan?
mcqData.explanation: To reverse the adjustment from the previous year, we debit the liability and credit the expense.

mcqData.text: What is the purpose of preparing closing entries?
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries reset the balances of temporary (nominal) accounts so the next period starts fresh.

mcqData.text: Asset worksheet is usually prepared—
mcqData.explanation: It acts as a bridge between the Trial Balance and the formal Financial Statements.

mcqData.text: Ad expense is 50,000 TK and free goods distributed 10,000 TK. Total to be written off over 5 years. Current year expense?
mcqData.explanation: Total (50,000 TK + 10,000 TK) / 5 years = 12,000 TK per year.

mcqData.text: What is the adjusting entry to defer the remaining advertisement?
mcqData.explanation: Note: Total 60k - 12k used = 48k to be deferred. Journal: Deferred Ad Debit. 48,000 TK; Ad Expense Credit. 48,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is prepared on loose sheets?
mcqData.explanation: Worksheets are typically prepared on large, multi-column loose paper rather than bound books.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Revenue Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Annual insurance is a recurring operating cost. Options Asset, B, and C are capital costs included in the asset's price.

mcqData.text: If the adjusting entry for accrued expense is omitted— i) Profit is overstated ii) Expense is understated iii) Profit is understated
mcqData.explanation: Failing to record an expense results in lower total expenses (understated) and higher reported profit (overstated).

mcqData.text: Why are closing entries required? i) To close expenses ii) To close income iii) To close capital expenditures
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries target temporary (nominal) accounts like income and expense. Capital expenditures involve permanent assets.

mcqData.text: How many columns are required in the most widely used worksheet?
mcqData.explanation: The 10-column worksheet is the standard format used in most academic and professional scenarios.

mcqData.text: Which heading is NOT required in an 8-column worksheet?
mcqData.explanation: To keep it to 8 columns, the Adjusted Trial Balance step is performed mentally or combined into the final columns.

mcqData.text: Asset worksheet is— i) Optional step of accounting cycle ii) Can be 8/10/12 columns iii) Prepared at the start of the year
mcqData.explanation: Worksheets are optional and multi-columned, but they are prepared at the end of the period, not the start.

mcqData.text: Which entry is required to close nominal accounts?
mcqData.explanation: Closing journals transfer nominal balances to the Income Summary to reset them for the next year.

mcqData.text: Which is known as the "Road Map" for preparing financial statements?
mcqData.explanation: The worksheet acts as a road map by organizing all adjustments and accounts before the final report.

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a permanent record of account keeping?
mcqData.explanation: The worksheet is a working paper (draft) and is not considered a formal or permanent accounting book.

mcqData.text: If accrued expense is not adjusted—
mcqData.explanation: Unrecorded expenses lead to a "fake" higher profit and an inaccurate lower expense figure.

mcqData.text: Capital expenses include— i) Machine purchase ii) Machine installation cost iii) Machine repair cost
mcqData.explanation: Asset purchase and setup are capital costs. Maintenance/repairs are recurring revenue expenditures.

mcqData.text: If "Purchase of Goods" is wrongly debited to "Repairs," what is the rectifying entry?
mcqData.explanation: To fix it, you debit the account that should have been used (Purchase) and credit the wrong one (Repairs).

mcqData.text: What is helpful to prepare before final financial statements?
mcqData.explanation: Asset worksheet ensures all adjustments are correct before committing them to the formal final statements.

mcqData.text: Stem: Sale by check 12k, Credit sale 22k, Cash purchase 8k, Credit purchase 30k, Computer 16k. Total records on Accrual Basis?
mcqData.explanation: Accrual basis records all transactions regardless of cash: 12+22+8+30+16 = 88,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on the stem above, what is the Net Income on a Cash Basis?
mcqData.explanation: Cash Basis: Only Cash Sales (12k via check) - Cash Purchase (8k) = 4,000 TK income.

mcqData.text: The basis of adjusting entries is accounting's— i) Time period concept ii) Accrual basis iii) Revenue recognition & Matching
mcqData.explanation: Adjustments exist solely to align revenues and expenses with the correct accounting period (Accrual).

mcqData.text: What is the entry to close Net Profit?
options: Income Summary Debit; Net Income Credit
Net Profit Debit; Income Summary Credit
mcqData.explanation: Net profit is transferred to Capital. Since profit increases equity, Capital is credited.

mcqData.text: 5,000 TK commission earned but not recorded—which account is debited?
mcqData.explanation: Earned but unpaid income is an asset. Accrued or Receivable Commission is debited.

mcqData.text: Which account balance remains after providing closing entries?
mcqData.explanation: Permanent accounts (Assets, Liabilities, Capital) carry their balances forward and are never closed.

mcqData.text: What is the result of adjusting unearned income (Liability method)?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting unearned income recognizes that the liability has been fulfilled, turning it into earned income.

mcqData.text: What type of expenditure is the installation cost of a newly purchased machine?
mcqData.explanation: Any cost necessary to bring an asset to its intended working condition is a capital expenditure.

mcqData.text: Closing entries are provided to— i. Close nominal accounts ii. Close fixed assets iii. Close drawing accounts
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries target temporary accounts (Income, Expense, Drawings). Fixed assets are permanent.

mcqData.text: What type of expenditure is the extension of a building?
mcqData.explanation: Extension increases the capacity or value of a fixed asset, qualifying as a capital expenditure.

mcqData.text: What type of transaction is the modernization cost of machinery?
mcqData.explanation: Modernization improves efficiency and extends the life of an asset, making it a capital expenditure.

mcqData.text: For which accounting concept are adjustments for unpaid salary and unused supplies required?
mcqData.explanation: The Matching Principle requires expenses to be recorded in the same period as the related revenue.

mcqData.text: If unpaid salary (10,000 TK) and unused supplies (1,400 TK) are NOT adjusted, how will profit change?
mcqData.explanation: Profit = (Unrecorded Expense 10,000 TK - Unrecorded Asset/Supplies 1,400 TK) = 8,600 TK Overstated.

mcqData.text: When should reversing entries be prepared?
mcqData.explanation: Reversing entries are made on the first day of the new accounting year.

mcqData.text: Through which entry does the balance of income-expense accounts become zero?
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries transfer nominal balances to the Income Summary, resetting them to zero.

mcqData.text: "10,000 TK profit does not mean 10,000 TK cash"—which logic is most related to this statement?
mcqData.explanation: Under accrual accounting, profit is based on earning/incurring, not just cash movement.

mcqData.text: Salary 25,000 TK, Accrued 5,000 TK. How much is shown in the Adjusted Trial Balance of a 10-column worksheet?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusted Balance = Paid (25,000 TK) + Accrued (5,000 TK) = 30,000 TK.

mcqData.text: Where should "Accrued Salary" be shown in a 10-column worksheet?
options: Adj Trial Balance, Income Stmt, Balance Sheet
Adj, Adj Trial Balance, Income Stmt
Adj, Adj Trial Balance, Balance Sheet
mcqData.explanation: Accrued salary is an adjustment and a liability (Balance Sheet). It is NOT an income statement item itself.

mcqData.text: What is Unearned Revenue?
mcqData.explanation: Revenue received before it is earned is a liability because the service is still owed to the customer.

mcqData.text: Transactions included in the Accrual Basis are— i. Cash ii. Advance iii. Accrued
mcqData.explanation: The accrual basis captures all economic events regardless of when cash is exchanged.

mcqData.text: Rent 22,000 TK paid for 11 TK months. What is the correct adjusting entry at year-end?
options: Expense Debit 11 TKkTK, Adv Credit 11 TKkTK
Adv Debit 2 TKkTK, Expense Credit 2 TKkTK
Expense Debit 2 TKkTK, Payable Credit 2 TKkTK
Payable Debit 2 TKkTK, Expense Credit 2 TKkTK
mcqData.explanation: 22,000 TK / 11 = 2,000 TK per month. One month is unpaid (2,000 TK). Journal: Rent Expense Debit, Rent Payable Credit.

mcqData.text: Ad is 12,000 TK for 4 TK years. What happens if this is NOT adjusted at year-end?
mcqData.explanation: Only 3 TKkTK is expense; 9 TKkTK is an asset. Omitting the adjustment keeps 9 TKkTK as an expense, lowering profit. (Note: Question likely implies profit is understated if 12 TKkTK is kept as expense). Correct logic: Profit ↓ 9 TKkTK.

mcqData.text: Which adjusting item cannot have a reversing entry?
mcqData.explanation: Estimates like bad debt provisions or depreciation are not reversed in the next period.

mcqData.text: What is the relationship between Unearned Revenue and Revenue?
mcqData.explanation: As Unearned Revenue (Liability) decreases, Revenue (Income) increases. They move in opposite directions during adjustment.

mcqData.text: Capital expenses include— i. Machine purchase ii. Installation cost iii. Repair cost
mcqData.explanation: Repairs are revenue expenditures. Purchase and installation are capital expenditures.

mcqData.text: Included in adjusting entries are— i. Unearned Income ii. Prepaid Expenses iii. Accrued Income
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting entries capture all non-cash accruals and deferrals needed to comply with the matching principle.

mcqData.text: Rent 9,000 TK paid for 6 TK months on Nov 1. What is the adjusting entry on Dec 31?
options: Adv Debit 3 TKkTK; Expense Credit 3 TKkTK
Expense Debit 3 TKkTK; Adv Credit 3 TKkTK
Adv Debit 6 TKkTK; Expense Credit 6 TKkTK
Expense Debit 6 TKkTK; Adv Credit 6 TKkTK
mcqData.explanation: Nov-Dec (2 TK months) used. Monthly = 1,500 TK. Total Expense = 3,000 TK. Journal: Rent Expense Debit, Prepaid Rent Credit.

mcqData.text: Examples of deferred revenue expenses are— i. Deferred Ad ii. Research & Dev iii. Preliminary Expenses
mcqData.explanation: All three provide long-term benefits but are technically revenue-based in nature.

mcqData.text: Which account balance becomes zero after the closing entry?
mcqData.explanation: Insurance Premium is a nominal (expense) account. Nominal accounts are closed at the end of the period.

mcqData.text: Electrical installation cost is a— i. Deferred revenue expenditure ii. Revenue expenditure iii. Capital expenditure
mcqData.explanation: Installation costs are necessary to make an asset usable, thus they are capitalized as capital expenditure.

mcqData.text: Opening Stationery 1,500 TK; Purchase 4,500 TK; Closing 500. What is the amount of Stationery Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Expense = (Opening + Purchase) - Closing = (1,500 TK + 4,500 TK) - 500 = 5,500 TK

mcqData.text: What is credited in the adjusting entry for expired prepaid insurance?
mcqData.explanation: When prepaid insurance expires, the asset is reduced by crediting the Prepaid Insurance account.

mcqData.text: Which one is a Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Buying fixed assets involves long-term benefits and large amounts, making it a capital expenditure.

mcqData.text: Which one is called a "Subsidiary Statement"?
mcqData.explanation: Asset worksheet is considered a subsidiary or auxiliary statement used to facilitate the final accounts.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is prepared on separate paper rather than in books of account?
mcqData.explanation: Asset worksheet is an informal draft prepared on loose sheets or multi-column paper.

mcqData.text: Ad Expense 15,000 TK; Free distribution 5,000 TK. 1/4 is to be written off. Amount in Adjusted Trial Balance?
mcqData.explanation: Total Ad = 15,000 TK + 5,000 TK = 20,000 TK. 1/4 write-off (expense) = 20,000 TK / 4 = 5,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on the above stem, what is the amount of Deferred Advertisement in the Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: Deferred (Asset) = Total 20,000 TK - Expensed 5,000 TK = 15,000 TK

mcqData.text: Where is "Net Loss" shown in a worksheet?
options: Income Stmt Debit & Balance Sheet Credit
Income Stmt Debit & Balance Sheet Debit
Income Stmt Credit & Balance Sheet Debit
Income Stmt Credit & Balance Sheet Credit
mcqData.explanation: Net loss is shown on the credit side of the Income Statement (to balance it) and the debit side of the Balance Sheet (reducing equity).

mcqData.text: What type of expenditure is Unexpired Advertisement?
mcqData.explanation: Advertisement costs that are not yet expensed (unexpired) are called Deferred Revenue Expenditures.

mcqData.text: Examples of capital expenditure are— i. Installation cost ii. Repair cost iii. Ship freight for purchase
mcqData.explanation: Repairs are revenue expenditures. Installation and freight for buying assets are capitalized.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Revenue Income?
mcqData.explanation: Sale of goods is the primary recurring operating income for a business.

mcqData.text: Land bought for 2,30,000 TK; Registration 25,000 TK; Mutation 2,000 TK; Annual Tax 400. Total Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Capital Expense = Purchase (2,30,000 TK) + Reg (25,000 TK) + Mutation (2,000 TK) = 2,57,000 TK. Land tax is a revenue expense.

mcqData.text: What is the effect of the land purchase on the Financial Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Buying land for cash increases one asset (Land) and decreases another (Cash) by the same amount.

mcqData.text: Debenture interest is a "Revenue Expense" because— i. Paid every year ii. Benefit is not long-term iii. Reduces net income
mcqData.explanation: Recurring payments for services or financing that reduce current year profit are revenue expenses.

mcqData.text: What is the entry used to close nominal accounts?
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries transfer nominal balances to the income summary to reset them.

mcqData.text: For which type of accounts are closing entries prepared?
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries are exclusively for Income, Expense, and Drawings accounts (Nominal).

mcqData.text: Supplies 1,350 TK in Trial Balance. Ending inventory is 800. What is the adjusting entry?
mcqData.explanation: Amount used = 1,350 TK - 800 = 550. Journal: Supplies Expense Debit, Supplies Credit.

mcqData.text: If an accountant forgets to adjust "Accrued Interest on Loan"— i. Profit overstated ii. Equity overstated iii. Liability understated
mcqData.explanation: Forgetting an expense keeps profit high and hides a debt (liability), affecting equity as well.

mcqData.text: Which entry is given to record Advances and Accruals?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting entries are used to bring the accrual-basis accounts up to date at year-end.

mcqData.text: For which of the following items is a closing entry applicable?
mcqData.explanation: Salary is an expense (nominal account), which must be closed at the end of the period. The others are permanent accounts.

mcqData.text: Which accounts must be closed at the end of the accounting period?
mcqData.explanation: Nominal accounts (Income, Expense, and Drawings) are closed to reset their balances for the next year.

mcqData.text: Unused supplies are adjusted by— i. Deducting from supplies used ii. Adding to supplies iii. Showing as a current asset
mcqData.explanation: Adjusting for unused supplies ensures that only the used portion is recorded as an expense, while the remainder stays as an asset.

mcqData.text: How many steps (main stages) does a worksheet generally have?
mcqData.explanation: The five standard steps are: Trial Balance, Adjustments, Adjusted Trial Balance, Income Statement, and Balance Sheet.

mcqData.text: Which columns are NOT included in a 10-column worksheet? i. Income Statement ii. Equity Statement iii. Retained Earnings Statement
mcqData.explanation: Asset 10-column worksheet includes Trial Balance, Adjustments, Adjusted Trial Balance, Income Stmt, and Balance Sheet.

mcqData.text: Total commission received 5,000 TK; 2,000 TK is for the next year. What is the current year's revenue?
mcqData.explanation: Revenue Income = Total (5,000 TK) - Advance (2,000 TK) = 3,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Revenue Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Interest is a recurring cost of borrowing, making it a standard revenue expenditure.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Buying fixed assets like furniture for long-term use is a capital expenditure.

mcqData.text: Mr. Anik buys machines for the purpose of "Resale." What type of expense is this?
mcqData.explanation: If assets are bought for resale (inventory), they are treated as goods purchase (Revenue Expenditure).

mcqData.text: Machine 1,00,000 TK; Installation 20,000 TK; Carriage 10,000 TK; Year-end Repair 5,000 TK. Total Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Capitalized Cost = 1,00,000 TK + 20,000 TK + 10,000 TK = 1,30,000 TK. Year-end repair is revenue expenditure.

mcqData.text: If the above machine (1.3L) is sold for 1,00,000 TK after total depreciation of 32,500 TK, what is the profit/loss?
Car repair (general) 20,000 TK; Repair to start car (initially) 2,000 TK; Monthly operating 10,000 TK. Total annual Revenue Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Revenue Expense = (10,000 TK x 12) + 20,000 TK (General Repair) = 1,40,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on the car stem above, what is the Capital Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: Capital Expense = Purchase (5,00,000 TK) + Initial repair to make it usable (2,000 TK) = 5,02,000 TK

mcqData.text: Trial Balance shows Office Supplies 1,300 TK. Ending supplies 600 TK. The adjusting entry is—
options: Sup Expense Debit 1,300 TK; Sup Credit 1,300 TK
Sup Debit 750 TK; Sup Expense Credit 750 TK
Sup Expense Debit 700 TK; Sup Credit 700 TK
Sup Expense Debit 600 TK; Sup Credit 600 TK
mcqData.explanation: Used amount = 1,300 TK - 600 = 700 TK. Journal: Supplies Expense Debit, Office Supplies Credit.

mcqData.text: By providing closing entries— i. Nominal accounts are closed ii. Drawings are transferred to capital iii. Accounts are opened for the current year
mcqData.explanation: Closing entries close the past year's revenue/expenses; they do not open new accounts (that's the opening entry's role).

mcqData.text: If an adjusting entry for accrued expense is not provided— i. Profit will be overstated ii. Expense will be understated iii. Profit will be understated
mcqData.explanation: Omitting an expense makes the profit look higher (overstated) and the total expenses look lower (understated).

mcqData.text: Stationery purchase 10,000 TK, unused at year-end 4,000 TK. Correct adjustment entry?
options: Stat Debit 10 TKkTK; Cash Credit 10 TKkTK
Stat Debit 6 TKkTK; Cash Credit 6 TKkTK
Unused Stat Debit 6 TKkTK; Stat Credit 6 TKkTK
Unused Stat Debit 4 TKkTK; Stat Credit 4 TKkTK
mcqData.explanation: To adjust for assets remaining, we debit the asset (Unused/Prepaid) and credit the expense (Stat) for the remaining amount.

mcqData.text: Opening stat 6,000 TK; Purchase 10,000 TK; Unused 4,000 TK. Amount shown in Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Stationery Expense = (Opening 6,000 TK + Purchase 10,000 TK) - Closing 4,000 TK = 12,000 TK

mcqData.text: If goods are distributed for free as advertisement, the adjusting entry is—
mcqData.explanation: Free distribution reduces inventory (Purchases) and increases the Advertisement expense.

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a mandatory (required) entry?
mcqData.explanation: Reversing entries (Option iii) are optional and provided at the discretion of the accountant for convenience.

mcqData.text: Reversing entries are applicable for— i. Receivable Service Revenue ii. Wages Payable iii. Depreciation Expense
mcqData.explanation: Reversing entries are used for accruals and deferrals, but never for non-cash estimates like Depreciation.

mcqData.text: Asset worksheet is an accounting—
mcqData.explanation: It is used internally by the accounting staff and is not legally required (optional).

mcqData.text: Salary (120%) is 1,20,000 TK. What is the amount of Salaries Payable?
Salary paid for 10 months is 40,000 TK. Amount of Salaries Payable?
mcqData.explanation: Monthly = 40,000 TK / 10 = 4,000 TK. Payable for 2 months = 4,000 TK x 2 = 8,000 TK

mcqData.text: Unearned Income is shown in— i. Trial Balance ii. Debit of Income Statement iii. Credit of Balance Sheet
mcqData.explanation: Unearned income is a liability and appears on the credit side of the Balance Sheet columns.

mcqData.text: Trial Balance shows Insurance 18,000 TK. 3 months are prepaid. Amount of Prepaid Insurance?
mcqData.explanation: Prepaid = (18,000 TK / 12 months) x 3 months = 4,500 TK

mcqData.text: What is "Unexpired Cost" considered?
mcqData.explanation: Unexpired costs (like prepaid insurance or unused supplies) are assets because they offer future benefit.

mcqData.text: What type of expenditure is Unexpired Advertisement?
mcqData.explanation: Advertisement costs that carry benefits to future periods are called Deferred Revenue Expenditures.

mcqData.text: "Revenue Expenditure" refers to— i. Current period revenue cost ii. Reserved cost for next periods iii. Current period write-offs
mcqData.explanation: It includes current operational costs and the portion of deferred costs expensed (written off) this year.

mcqData.text: What is the cost of advertising a brand new product in the market?
mcqData.explanation: Large initial costs for a new product are typically deferred and written off over several years.

mcqData.text: When prepaid expenses are adjusted (expiring)—
mcqData.explanation: As the prepayment expires, the expense is recorded (Increases) and the asset (Prepaid) is used up (Decreases).

mcqData.text: Closed at the end of the accounting period— i. Expenses ii. Revenues iii. Drawings
mcqData.explanation: All temporary accounts (Revenue, Expense, Drawings) are reset to zero via closing entries.

8.chapterName: Chapter 8 : Accounting for Tangible and Intangible Assets

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Lamisha Ltd. charges depreciation at a rate of 10% on machinery. The book value of a machine is 25,000 Taka and its accumulated depreciation is 15,000 Taka.

Q. What is the purchase price of the machine?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase Price = Book Value + Accumulated Depreciation = 25,000 + 15,000 = 40,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Lamisha Ltd. charges depreciation at a rate of 10% on machinery. The book value of a machine is 25,000 Taka and its accumulated depreciation is 15,000 Taka.

Q. If the current selling price of the machine is 30,000 Taka, what will be the profit or loss?
mcqData.explanation: Profit = Selling Price - Book Value = 30,000 - 25,000 = 5,000 Taka (Profit).

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Pushpo imported a machine for 20,00,000 Taka. He paid 2,00,000 Taka for customs duty and dock charges, and spent 50,000 Taka for installation. After an estimated useful life of 5 years, the salvage value of the machine is estimated at 1,50,000 Taka.

Q. At what value will the machine be recorded in the books of accounts?
mcqData.explanation: Recorded Value (Cost) = Purchase Price + Import Duty + Installation = 20,00,000 + 2,00,000 + 50,000 = 22,50,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Pushpo imported a machine for 20,00,000 Taka. He paid 2,00,000 Taka for customs duty and dock charges, and spent 50,000 Taka for installation. After an estimated useful life of 5 years, the salvage value of the machine is estimated at 1,50,000 Taka.

Q. What type of expenditure are the dock charges and customs duty described in the stem?
mcqData.explanation: Costs like dock charges and customs duty are Capital Expenditures because they are necessary to bring the fixed asset to its working condition.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Miss Tahsin purchased a motor car for 50,000 Taka. Currently, its accumulated depreciation balance is 20,000 Taka. She sold the car for 36,000 Taka.

Q. What is the profit or loss on the sale?
mcqData.explanation: Profit/Loss = Selling Price - (Purchase Price - Accumulated Depreciation) = 36,000 - (50,000 - 20,000) = 36,000 - 30,000 = 6,000 Taka (Profit).

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Miss Tahsin purchased a motor car for 50,000 Taka. Currently, its accumulated depreciation balance is 20,000 Taka. She sold the car for 36,000 Taka.

Q. In the journal entry for the sale of the car, which will be credited?

i. Motor car account
ii. Accumulated depreciation account
iii. Gain on sale of motor car account

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: The credit entries are Motor Car (to remove the cost) and Gain on Sale. Accumulated Depreciation is debited to remove its existing balance.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Korimon Ltd. charges depreciation at a rate of 10% on machinery. The book value of a machine is 30,000 Taka and its accumulated depreciation is 20,000 Taka.

Q. What is the purchase price of the machine?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase Price = Book Value + Accumulated Depreciation = 30,000 + 20,000 = 50,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Korimon Ltd. charges depreciation at a rate of 10% on machinery. The book value of a machine is 30,000 Taka and its accumulated depreciation is 20,000 Taka.

Q. If the current selling price of the machine is 35,000 Taka, what will be the profit or loss?
mcqData.explanation: Profit = Selling Price - Book Value = 35,000 - 30,000 = 5,000 Taka (Profit).

mcqData.text: What type of account is Accumulated Depreciation?
mcqData.explanation: Accumulated Depreciation is a Contra Asset account. It reduces the gross value of the asset on the balance sheet.

mcqData.text:
What type of transaction is depreciation?

i. Non-cash
ii. Invisible
iii. On credit

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation is Non-cash because no cash is paid out, and Invisible because no physical exchange of value is seen.

mcqData.text: Mr. Bashar purchased furniture for 20,000 Taka for use in business. If the annual depreciation is 1,000 Taka, what is the rate of depreciation?
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation Rate = (Annual Depreciation / Purchase Price) × 100 = (1,000 / 20,000) × 100 = 5%.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a non-cash transaction?
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation is a non-cash transaction as it is an allocation of cost over time, requiring no immediate cash payment.

mcqData.text: From which word did the word 'Depreciation' originate?
mcqData.explanation: The word originates from the Latin 'Depretium' (De = down, Pretium = price/value).

mcqData.text:
Reasons for charging depreciation-

i. Consistency principle
ii. Tax savings
iii. Replacement of asset

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Charging depreciation ensures consistency, provides tax savings by reducing taxable income, and creates a fund for asset replacement.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a non-cash transaction?
mcqData.explanation: Amortization (for intangible assets) and depreciation (for tangible assets) are both non-cash internal allocations.

mcqData.text:
When charging depreciation under the fixed installment (straight-line) method, what is considered?

i. Purchase price of the asset
ii. Book value of the asset
iii. Estimated useful life

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Straight-line uses Cost, Salvage Value, and Useful Life. Book value is primarily used in reducing balance methods.

mcqData.text:
Depreciation is charged on-

i. Fixed assets
ii. Current assets
iii. Tangible assets

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation is exclusively charged on Fixed Assets that are Tangible in nature.

mcqData.text:
As a result of charging depreciation-

i. Asset and owner's equity decrease
ii. Expense increases and profit decreases
iii. Asset decreases and owner's equity increases

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation expense decreases Owner's Equity, while the credit to Accumulated Depreciation decreases the Net Asset value.

mcqData.text: Which of the following values of a fixed asset is distributed over its useful life?
mcqData.explanation: The Depreciable Value (Cost - Salvage) is the total amount that is systematically allocated over the asset's life.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Latif Traders purchased a machine on January 1, 2015, for 1,90,000 Taka. The installation cost of the machine was 10,000 Taka. Depreciation is charged at a rate of 10% annually using the reducing balance method.

Q. What is the amount of depreciation for the year 2015?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Latif Traders purchased a machine on January 1, 2015, for 1,90,000 Taka. The installation cost of the machine was 10,000 Taka. Depreciation is charged at a rate of 10% annually using the reducing balance method.

Q. What will be the book value of the machine after three years?

What factors must be considered when calculating depreciation under the straight-line method?

i. Purchase price of the asset
ii. Salvage value of the asset
iii. Estimated useful life

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Calculating straight-line depreciation requires the Original Cost, the Salvage Value, and the Useful Life.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Rony Limited purchased a machine on January 1, 2014, for 3,00,000 Taka. The salvage value is 20,000 Taka and the estimated life is 10 years. Depreciation is calculated using the reducing balance method.

Q. What is the depreciable value of the machine for the year 2014?
mcqData.explanation: In the reducing balance method, salvage value is not deducted from the cost to determine the base for depreciation. Thus, the depreciable value is the full cost of 3,00,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Rony Limited purchased a machine on January 1, 2014, for 3,00,000 Taka. The salvage value is 20,000 Taka and the estimated life is 10 years. Depreciation is calculated using the reducing balance method (Double Declining assumed).

Q. Determine the amount of depreciation for the year 2015.
mcqData.explanation:
Rate = 20% (assuming double-declining 2/10)

2014 Dep = 3,00,000 × 20% = 60,000

2015 Dep = (3,00,000 - 60,000) × 20% = 48,000 Taka

mcqData.text: In which method of determining depreciation is the salvage value not subtracted?
mcqData.explanation: In the Reducing Balance method, the depreciation rate is applied to the full book value (Cost - Accum Dep) without deducting salvage first.

mcqData.text: Which method is most commonly used for charging depreciation?
mcqData.explanation: The Straight-line method is the most widely used due to its simplicity and equal distribution of cost.

mcqData.text: If an asset has no salvage value, which method of depreciation will not result in the asset value becoming zero at the end of its useful life?
mcqData.explanation: Because it calculates a percentage of a declining balance, the Reducing Balance method mathematically never reaches exactly zero.

mcqData.text: Which is the modern journal entry for recording depreciation?
options: Depreciation A/C Dr; Asset A/C Cr
Accumulated Depreciation A/C Dr; Depreciation A/C Cr
Asset A/C Dr; Depreciation Expense A/C Cr
Depreciation Expense A/C Dr; Accumulated Depreciation A/C Cr
mcqData.explanation: Modern accounting uses the Indirect Method, where the expense is debited and the provision (Accumulated Depreciation) is credited.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Sohel purchased a machine on January 1, 2014, for 80,000 Taka. The installation cost of the machine was 20,000 Taka. The depreciation rate is 10%.

Q. If Mr. Sohel records the machine purchase and installation cost in cash-

i. Machine account Dr
ii. Installation expense account Dr
iii. Cash account Cr

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Capitalization rule: All costs to make an asset usable (like installation) are debited to the Asset Account (Machine), not an expense.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Sohel purchased a machine on January 1, 2014, for 80,000 Taka. The installation cost of the machine was 20,000 Taka. The depreciation rate is 10%.

Q. What is the amount of depreciation for the machine in 2015?

In the fixed installment method, which of the following are considered when calculating depreciation?

i. Purchase price of the asset
ii. Book value of the asset
iii. Estimated useful life

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: The Straight-line method focuses on the Original Cost and Life; Book value is the base for the reducing balance method.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

The value of machinery in Shoikat Traders' Trial Balance is 1,00,000 Taka. The installation cost of the machine was 2,000 Taka and the repair cost was 1,000 Taka. He charges depreciation at a rate of 10%.

Q. What will be the annual depreciation on December 31?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Depreciation = (Machinery + Installation Cost) × 10% = (1,00,000 + 2,000) × 10% = 1,02,000 × 10% = 10,200 Taka. (Repair cost is revenue expenditure and not capitalized).

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

The value of machinery in Shoikat Traders' Trial Balance is 1,00,000 Taka. The installation cost of the machine was 2,000 Taka and the repair cost was 1,000 Taka. He charges depreciation at a rate of 10%.

Q. If Mr. Shoikat decides to charge depreciation at a rate of 20% instead of 10%-

i. Net profit will decrease
ii. There will be a change in asset valuation
iii. Inconsistency will appear in the Balance Sheet

Which of the following is correct?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

The purchase price of a machine is 1,90,000 Taka, installation cost is 10,000 Taka, salvage value is 20,000 Taka, and useful life is 10 years. Depreciation is charged at a rate of 20% using the reducing balance method.

Q. What is the book value of the machine at the end of the third year?
mcqData.explanation:
Cost = 2,00,000

Year 1 Dep: 40,000 | BV: 1,60,000

Year 2 Dep: 32,000 | BV: 1,28,000

Year 3 Dep: 25,600 | BV: 1,02,400 Taka

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

The purchase price of a machine is 1,90,000 Taka, installation cost is 10,000 Taka, salvage value is 20,000 Taka, and useful life is 10 years. Depreciation is charged at a rate of 20% using the reducing balance method.

Q. If straight-line depreciation is used instead of the reducing balance method, what will be the effect on the financial statements in the initial years?
mcqData.explanation: Straight-line depreciation (18k) would be lower than the first-year reducing balance depreciation (40k), leading to higher reported Net Profit.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

On April 1, 2012, Rita Enterprise purchased a machine for 47,000 Taka and installed it for 7,000 Taka. The life of the machine is estimated at 5 years with a salvage value of 4,000 Taka. The firm closes its accounts on December 31 every year.

Q. What is the depreciation for 2012 under the straight-line method?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Dep = (47k + 7k - 4k) / 5 = 10,000. For 9 months (Apr-Dec): 10,000 × 9/12 = 7,500 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

On April 1, 2012, Rita Enterprise purchased a machine for 47,000 Taka and installed it for 7,000 Taka. The life of the machine is estimated at 5 years with a salvage value of 4,000 Taka.

Q. If the firm uses the double-declining balance method, what will be the depreciation rate?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Sachin Machinery purchase details: Jan 1, 2012: 1,00,000 Taka; June 30, 2012: 70,000 Taka. The machine purchased in June was sold on July 1, 2013, for 60,000 Taka. 10% Straight-line depreciation is charged.

Q. What is the total depreciation on December 31, 2012?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Sachin Machinery purchase details: June 30, 2012: 70,000 Taka. This machine was sold on July 1, 2013, for 60,000 Taka. 10% Straight-line depreciation is charged.

Q. What is the profit or loss on the sale of the machine on July 1, 2013?
Which method of depreciation uses the formula to determine the rate based on salvage value and cost ($1 - \sqrt[n]{S/C}$)?
mcqData.explanation: This is the standard mathematical formula used to calculate the percentage rate for the Reducing Balance method.

mcqData.text:
In the straight-line method, which factors are considered for depreciation?

i. Purchase price of the asset
ii. Estimated salvage value
iii. Estimated useful life

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: All three factors (Cost, Salvage, and Life) are mandatory components of the Straight-line depreciation formula.

mcqData.text: A machine was purchased for 20,000 Taka on January 1, 2010. 10% reducing balance depreciation is charged. What will be the accumulated depreciation balance after 6 years?
mcqData.explanation: BV after 6 years = 20,000 × (0.9)^6 ≈ 10,629. Accum Dep = 20,000 - 10,629 = 9,371 Taka.

mcqData.text: If an asset's useful life is 8 years, what will be the depreciation rate under the straight-line method?
mcqData.explanation: Rate = 100 / 8 = 12.5%.

mcqData.text: Which is the correct formula for the depreciation rate under the straight-line method?
mcqData.explanation: The formula is Rate = (100 / Life) for the basic straight-line percentage.

mcqData.text:
If the useful life of a machine is 8 years instead of 10 years-

i. The depreciation rate will increase
ii. Net profit will decrease
iii. Tax liability will increase

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: A shorter life increases the annual depreciation expense, which **reduces net profit** and consequently **reduces tax liability**.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

On December 31, 2017, Jason Ltd. purchased a machine for 50,000 Taka. The installation cost was 7,000 Taka and carriage cost was 3,000 Taka. Salvage value is 5,000 Taka and useful life is 5 years.

Q. What is the annual depreciation for the machine under the straight-line method?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

On December 31, 2017, Jason Ltd. purchased a machine for 50,000 Taka. The installation cost was 7,000 Taka and carriage cost was 3,000 Taka. Useful life is 5 years.

Q. If the company uses the double-declining balance method, what will be the depreciation rate?
mcqData.explanation: Rate = (100% / 5) × 2 = 40%.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

The useful life of a machine is 10 years and the salvage value is 5,000 Taka. The purchase price of the machine is 55,000 Taka.

Q. What will be the annual depreciation rate under the reducing balance method?
mcqData.explanation: Reducing Balance Rate = (100% / Life) × 2 = (100% / 10) × 2 = 20%.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

The useful life of a machine is 10 years and the salvage value is 5,000 Taka. The purchase price of the machine is 55,000 Taka. The depreciation rate is 20%.

Q. What will be the depreciation for the second year under the reducing balance method?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Rahim purchased a machine on January 1, 2017, for 2,00,000 Taka. The installation cost was 10,000 Taka and the carriage cost was 15,000 Taka. Useful life is 10 years and salvage value is 20,000 Taka.

Q. What is the total cost of the machine?
mcqData.explanation: Total Cost = Purchase Price + Installation + Carriage = 2,00,000 + 10,000 + 15,000 = 2,25,000 Taka.

mcqData.text: On January 1, 2012, furniture was purchased for 40,000 Taka. If depreciation is charged at 10% using the reducing balance method, how much will be in the accumulated depreciation provision after 4 years?
mcqData.explanation:
Yr 1: 4,000 | Yr 2: 3,600 | Yr 3: 3,240 | Yr 4: 2,916

Total Accumulated = 4,000 + 3,600 + 3,240 + 2,916 = 13,756 Taka

mcqData.text: The purchase price of an asset is 1,00,000 Taka and its salvage value is 20,000 Taka. If depreciation is charged at 20% under the reducing balance method, what is the first-year depreciation?
mcqData.explanation: In the reducing balance method, salvage value is not subtracted from the cost for the calculation. Dep = 1,00,000 × 20% = 20,000 Taka.

mcqData.text: If an asset was purchased for 25,000 Taka and depreciation for 2012 was 2,500 Taka (10%), what will be the depreciation for 2013 under the reducing balance method?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Sabila Builders purchased a machine on July 1, 2017, for 9,75,000 Taka. The carriage cost was 25,000 Taka. Useful life is 20 years and salvage value is 40,000 Taka.

Q. If the carriage cost is debited to the Purchase (মেশিন ক্রয়) account instead of the Machine account, what type of error is it?
mcqData.explanation: Debiting a capital expenditure (Machine) to a revenue account (Purchase) is a Principle Error as it violates accounting rules.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Sabila Builders purchased a machine on July 1, 2017, for 9,75,000 Taka. The carriage cost was 25,000 Taka. Useful life is 20 years. Depreciation rate is 10%.

Q. What is the depreciation for 2017 under the reducing balance method?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

On January 1, 2017, Shrobona Ltd. had a furniture balance of 1,20,000 Taka and an accumulated depreciation balance of 50,000 Taka. The salvage value after 5 years is estimated at 20,000 Taka.

Q. What is the annual depreciation under the straight-line method?
mcqData.explanation: Straight-line Dep = (Cost - Salvage) / Life = (1,20,000 - 20,000) / 5 = 1,00,000 / 5 = 20,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

On January 1, 2017, Shrobona Ltd. had a furniture balance of 1,20,000 Taka and an accumulated depreciation balance of 50,000 Taka. Useful life is 5 years.

Q. If the depreciation rate is 10%, what is the depreciation for 2017 under the reducing balance method?
mcqData.explanation: Dep (2017) = (Balance - Accumulated Dep) × Rate = (1,20,000 - 50,000) × 10% = 70,000 × 10% = 7,000 Taka.

mcqData.text: On which of the following assets is depreciation charged?
mcqData.explanation: Land is never depreciated as its life is considered infinite. Trademarks and Patents are amortized. Buildings are tangible fixed assets that lose value over time, hence they are depreciated.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Arafat Ali purchased a machine on January 1, 2024, for 6,00,000 Taka. The installation cost was 40,000 Taka and carriage cost was 10,000 Taka. Useful life is 10 years. Reducing balance method is used.

Q. What is the acquisition cost of the machine?
mcqData.explanation: Acquisition Cost = Purchase Price + Installation + Carriage = 6,00,000 + 40,000 + 10,000 = 6,50,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Arafat Ali purchased a machine on January 1, 2024, for 6,00,000 Taka. The installation cost was 40,000 Taka and carriage cost was 10,000 Taka. Useful life is 10 years. Reducing balance method is used.

Q. What is the book value of the machine at the end of the first year?
mcqData.explanation:
Cost = 6,50,000. Rate = (100 / 10) × 2 = 20%.

Depreciation = 6,50,000 × 20% = 1,30,000.

Book Value = 6,50,000 - 1,30,000 = 5,20,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Factors to be considered in determining depreciation are-

i. Purchase price
ii. Effective useful life
iii. Salvage value

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: All three factors are essential for any depreciation calculation to accurately allocate the asset's cost over time.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

A machine was purchased on January 1, 2022, for 1,00,000 Taka. Estimated useful life is 10 years and salvage value is 10,000 Taka.

Q. What is the depreciation for the year 2024 under the straight-line method?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Dep = (1,00,000 - 10,000) / 10 = 90,000 / 10 = 9,000 Taka. In the straight-line method, the amount remains constant every year.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

A machine was purchased on January 1, 2022, for 1,00,000 Taka. Estimated useful life is 10 years and salvage value is 10,000 Taka.

Q. What is the depreciation for the year 2025 under the reducing balance method?
What type of value of an asset is referred to as depreciation?
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation is the systematic allocation of the Used-up portion of the asset's cost over its life.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is the most suitable method for calculating depreciation on fixed assets like buildings, furniture, and machinery?
mcqData.explanation: The Reducing Balance method is ideal for machinery as it charges higher depreciation in earlier years when the asset is most productive.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Sun Traders spent 6,00,000 Taka and 50,000 Taka for machine purchase and installation respectively on January 1, 2024. Life is 10 years and salvage value is 40,000 Taka. Straight-line method is used.

Q. What is the annual depreciation of the machine?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Sun Traders spent 6,50,000 Taka for a machine. Life is 10 years and salvage value is 40,000 Taka. Straight-line annual depreciation is 61,000 Taka.

Q. If reducing balance is used, by how much more will the asset value decrease in the Balance Sheet in the 1st year compared to straight-line?
mcqData.explanation:
Red. Bal Rate = 20%. Dep = 6,50,000 × 20% = 1,30,000.

Difference = 1,30,000 - 61,000 = 69,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Mamun purchased a machine for 1,80,000 Taka on Jan 1, 2023, with 20,000 Taka installation cost. Life is 10 years and salvage value is 30,000 Taka.

Q. What is the depreciation for 2024 under the reducing balance method?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Mamun spent 20,000 Taka for machine installation. What if this is incorrectly recorded as a revenue expenditure?

i. Machine value understated by 20,000
ii. Net profit understated by 20,000
iii. Total assets and liabilities understated by 20,000

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Recording capital expenditure as revenue understated assets (Machine) and understated profit (which in turn understates Equity/Liabilities side).

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Machine cost 3,00,000 Taka, installation 40,000, carriage 10,000. Life is 10 years. Reducing balance method is used.

Q. What will be the book value of the machine at the end of the first year?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Machine cost 3,50,000. If straight-line is used instead of reducing balance in the first year-

i. Depreciation amount will decrease
ii. No impact on profit
iii. Net profit will increase

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Straight-line dep (35,000) is lower than reducing balance (70,000) in the first year, hence profit will increase.

mcqData.text:
Factors considered in determining depreciation are-

i. Purchase price
ii. Effective useful life
iii. Salvage value

Which of the following is correct?
In which method is the salvage value of the asset considered in the final year of its useful life?
mcqData.explanation: In the Reducing Balance method, salvage value is not subtracted from the cost base initially but serves as a floor below which the asset cannot be depreciated.

mcqData.text:
Essential factors for charging depreciation on assets are-

i. Total cost of the asset
ii. Estimated useful life
iii. Salvage value

Which of the following is correct?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Selim Traders purchased a machine on July 1, 2023, for 90,000 Taka and spent 10,000 for installation. Life is 10 years and salvage value is 20,000 Taka. Accounts close on Dec 31.

Q. What is the depreciation for 2024 under the straight-line method?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Dep = (1,00,000 - 20,000) / 10 = 8,000 Taka. Since 2024 is a full year, the amount is 8,000.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Selim Traders purchased a machine on July 1, 2023, for 1,00,000 Taka. Rate is 20%.

Q. What is the depreciation for 2023 under the reducing balance method?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Dep = 1,00,000 × 20% = 20,000. For 6 months (July-Dec): 20,000 × 6/12 = 10,000 Taka.

mcqData.text: In which method does the amount of depreciation remain equal in all financial years?
mcqData.explanation: The Straight-line method distributes the cost of the asset equally over its useful life.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mita Enterprise purchased a machine on April 1, 2024, for 94,000 Taka and installed it for 14,000 Taka. Life is 5 years and salvage value is 8,000 Taka. Accounts close on Dec 31.

Q. What is the depreciation for 2024 under the straight-line method?
mcqData.explanation:
Annual Dep = (1,08,000 - 8,000) / 5 = 20,000.

For 9 months (Apr-Dec): 20,000 × 9/12 = 15,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Useful life is 5 years.

Q. If the firm uses the reducing balance method, what is the depreciation rate?
mcqData.explanation: Rate = (100% / 5) × 2 = 40%.

mcqData.text: The useful life of a machine in Jubayer Traders is 10 years. Annual depreciation is 20,000 Taka and salvage value is 25,000 Taka. What is the purchase price of the machine?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Dep = (Cost - Salvage) / Life -> 20,000 = (Cost - 25,000) / 10 -> 2,00,000 = Cost - 25,000 -> Cost = 2,25,000 Taka.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a contra-asset?
mcqData.explanation: Accumulated Depreciation is a contra-asset account as it reduces the value of the fixed asset on the balance sheet.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an external cause of depreciation?
mcqData.explanation: Obsolescence (becoming outdated due to new technology) is an external factor. Time, depletion, and usage are internal or inherent to the asset.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Mr. Akib purchased a machine for 1,00,000 Taka at the beginning of 2021. Useful life is 5 years and the straight-line method is used. At the end of 2023, the machine was sold for 50,000 Taka.

Q. What is the amount of profit or loss on the sale?
mcqData.explanation:
Annual Dep = 1,00,000 / 5 = 20,000.

Accum Dep (3 years) = 60,000. Book Value = 40,000.

Profit = 50,000 - 40,000 = 10,000 Taka (Profit).

mcqData.text: On which of the following fixed assets is depreciation NOT charged?
mcqData.explanation: Land is an asset with an infinite life and is not subject to physical wear and tear in an accounting sense, so it is not depreciated.

mcqData.text: The purchase price of a machine is 4,00,000 Taka. If the useful life is 10 years and annual straight-line depreciation is 35,000 Taka, what is the salvage value?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Dep = (Cost - Salvage) / Life -> 35,000 = (4,00,000 - Salvage) / 10 -> 3,50,000 = 4,00,000 - Salvage -> Salvage = 50,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

On January 1, 2020, Tonmoy Co. purchased equipment for 42,000 Taka. Carriage and installation costs were 3,000 Taka. Life is 6 years and salvage value is 6,000 Taka. On January 1, 2023, the equipment was sold for 20,000 Taka. Straight-line method is used.

Q. What is the amount of profit or loss on the sale?
mcqData.explanation:
Cost = 45,000. Annual Dep = (45,000 - 6,000) / 6 = 6,500.

Accum Dep (3 yrs) = 19,500. Book Value = 25,500.

Loss = 25,500 - 20,000 = 5,500 Taka (Loss).

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

On January 1, 2020, Tonmoy Co. purchased equipment for 42,000 Taka. Carriage and installation costs were 3,000 Taka. Life is 6 years and salvage value is 6,000 Taka. Straight-line method is used.

Q. What is the amount of annual depreciation?

Factors that influence the determination of depreciation are-

i. Purchase price of the asset
ii. Estimated useful life
iii. Estimated salvage value

Which of the following is correct?
Which of the following is NOT an internal cause of depreciation?
mcqData.explanation: Obsolescence (becoming outdated due to technology) is an external factor. Time, wear and tear, and depletion are internal factors inherent to the asset's use.

mcqData.text:
Depreciation is-

i. An invisible transaction
ii. A non-cash transaction
