Which of the following is correct?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Soma Traders purchased a machine on January 1, 2021, for 5,75,000 Taka (including 15% VAT). Installation cost was 25,000 Taka. Life is 10 years and salvage value is 50,000 Taka.

Q. What is the annual depreciation under the straight-line method?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Soma Traders machine cost was 6,00,000. Life is 10 years and salvage value is 50,000 Taka. Reducing balance method is used.

Q. What is the depreciation for the second year under the reducing balance method?
mcqData.explanation:
Rate = 20%.

Yr 1 Dep = 1,20,000. BV = 4,80,000.

Yr 2 Dep = 4,80,000 × 20% = 96,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Oronyo Ltd. purchased a machine on January 1, 2020, for 2,00,000 Taka. Reducing balance depreciation is charged at 20% annually.

Q. What amount will be shown for the machine in the Balance Sheet on Dec 31, 2021?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Oronyo Ltd. machine BV was 1,28,000 on Dec 31, 2021. It was sold for 1,00,000 on Dec 31, 2022. Rate is 20%.

Q. What is the amount of profit or loss on the sale?
mcqData.explanation:
2022 Dep = 1,28,000 × 20% = 25,600.

BV at sale = 1,02,400.

Loss = 1,02,400 - 1,00,000 = 2,400 Taka (Loss).

mcqData.text:
If depreciation is over-charged-

i. It will decrease net income
ii. It will decrease owner's equity
iii. It will increase liabilities

Which of the following is correct?
mcqData.explanation: Over-charging depreciation increases expenses, which reduces net profit and owner's equity. It does not affect liabilities.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Salam Enterprise purchased a machine for 60,000 Taka on Jan 1, 2023. Life is 10 years and salvage value is 10,000 Taka.

Q. What is the depreciation for 2023 under the straight-line method?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Salam Enterprise machine cost 60,000. Life is 10 years. Reducing balance rate is 20%.

Q. What is the depreciation for 2024 under the reducing balance method?
mcqData.explanation:
2023 Dep = 12,000. BV = 48,000.

2024 Dep = 48,000 × 20% = 9,600 Taka.

mcqData.text: Which equation is correct for determining the book value of a fixed asset?
mcqData.explanation: The fundamental definition of Book Value is the original cost minus all accumulated depreciation.

mcqData.text:
Depreciation is a-

i. Non-cash expense
ii. Cost allocation process
iii. Invisible transaction

Which of the following is correct?

Read the following stem and answer the question:

Anik Ltd. Trial Balance (Dec 31, 2023): Machine 5,00,000 Taka; Accumulated Depreciation 95,000 Taka. The machine was purchased on Jan 1, 2021. Life is 10 years, salvage value is 20,000 Taka. Rate is 10% reducing balance.

Q. What is the amount of depreciation for the first year?
mcqData.explanation: Dep (Year 1) = 5,00,000 × 10% = 50,000 Taka.

mcqData.text:
Read the following stem and answer the question:

Anik Ltd. machine cost 5,00,000. Accumulated Dep (up to 2022) is 95,000. Rate is 10% reducing balance. The machine is sold for 5,50,000 on Dec 31, 2023.

Q. What is the profit on the sale of the machine?
What is the cost allocation process for intangible assets called?
mcqData.explanation: Amortization is the term used for intangible assets, while Depreciation is for tangible assets and Depletion is for natural resources.

mcqData.text:
Factors considered in straight-line depreciation calculation are-

i. Purchase price
ii. Salvage value

mcqData.text: In the above stem, what is the depreciation rate if the Reducing Balance method is used?
mcqData.explanation: Rate = (1 - 6√(4000/70000)) × 100 = 37.94%.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Kawsar purchased a machine on Jan 1, 2019, for 9,00,000 Taka. Installation cost was 90,000 and carriage was 40,000. Salvage value is estimated at 30,000. The machine's production units are: 2019 – 10,000 units; 2020 – 15,000 units; 2021 – 10,000 units; 2022 – 8,000 units; and 2023 - 7,000 units.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Determine the depreciable value of the machine.
questions.answer:
Cost = 9,00,000 + 90,000 + 40,000 = 10,30,000

Depreciable Value = Cost - Salvage

= 10,30,000 - 30,000 = 10,00,000 Taka

questions.label: b
questions.text: Calculate the depreciation rate per unit and show journals for the first two years (Production Method).
questions.answer:
Total Units = 10,000+15,000+10,000+8,000+7,000 = 50,000 units.

Rate per unit = 10,00,000 / 50,000 = 20 Taka/unit.

2019 Dep = 10,000 × 20 = 2,00,000.

2020 Dep = 15,000 × 20 = 3,00,000.

fullCQData.stem: Reza & Co. bought a machine for 1,50,000 on Jan 1, 2023. A 2nd machine was bought for 1,00,000 on July 1, 2023, and a 3rd for 60,000 on Jan 1, 2024. The 1st machine was sold for 90,000 on July 1, 2024. 20% Straight-line method is used.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Calculate depreciation for 2023 and 2024.
questions.answer:
2023:
M1 (Full yr): 1,50,000 × 20% = 30,000
M2 (6 mo): 1,00,000 × 20% × 6/12 = 10,000
Total = 40,000

2024:
M1 (Sold Jul 1): 1,50,000 × 20% × 6/12 = 15,000
M2 (Full yr): 1,00,000 × 20% = 20,000
M3 (Full yr): 60,000 × 20% = 12,000
Total = 47,000

questions.label: b
questions.text: Calculate profit/loss on sale of M1 and give journals.
questions.answer:
Cost = 1,50,000
Accum Dep (Yr 2023+2024) = 30,000 + 15,000 = 45,000
Book Value = 1,05,000
Sale Price = 90,000
Loss = 15,000

Journal:

Loss on Sale Dr 15,000
Accum. Dep Dr 45,000
Machine Cr 1,50,000

questions.answer:
Total Cost = 30+3+1+2 = 36,00,000.
Depreciable Value = 36,00,000 - 6,00,000 = 30,00,000 Taka (Note: While base is cost for RB, question asks specifically for depreciable value).

questions.label: b
questions.text: Show journals for 2023 and 2024.

Total Cost = 30,00,000 + 3,00,000 + 1,00,000 + 2,00,000 = 36,00,000.

2023 Dep (6 mo) = 36,00,000 × 20% × 6/12 = 3,60,000.

2024 Dep = (36,00,000 - 3,60,000) × 20% = 6,48,000.

Cost = 9,00,000 + 90,000 + 40,000 = 10,30,000.

Depreciable Value = 10,30,000 - 30,000 = 10,00,000 Taka.

Rate = 10,00,000 / 50,000 = 20 Taka/unit.

2021 Dep = 10,000 × 20 = 2,00,000. 2022 Dep = 15,000 × 20 = 3,00,000.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT shown in the Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: Secret Reserves are hidden and not disclosed on the face of financial statements.

mcqData.text: If Profit on Sales is 20 TK\%TK, what is the profit percentage on Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: If Sales = 100 TK and Profit = 20 TK, Cost = 80 TK. Profit on Cost = (20 TK/80) \times 100 = 25\%TK.

mcqData.text: To determine Net Purchase, what should be deducted from Purchase? i. Purchase Discount ii. Carriage Inward iii. Purchase Return
mcqData.explanation: Purchase returns and discounts reduce the total purchase amount. Carriage inward is an addition, not a deduction.

mcqData.text: Closing stock cost 40,000 TK, market value 30,000 TK. Includes 5,000 TK fire-damaged goods. Final Closing Stock value?
mcqData.explanation: Follow "Lower of Cost or Market": 30,000 TK is base. Subtract damaged goods: 30,000 TK - 5,000 TK = 25,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on the stem above (Q8), what is the amount of Current Assets? (Insurance covers 40 TK\%TK of 5,000 TK loss)
mcqData.explanation: Current Assets = Closing Stock (25,000 TK) + Insurance Claim Receivable (5,000 TK \times 40\% = 2,000 TK) = 27,000 TK

mcqData.text: If Bad Debt is not charged— i. Net Profit overstated ii. Net Profit understated iii. Asset value overstated
mcqData.explanation: Omitting an expense makes profit look higher (overstated) and prevents accounts receivable from decreasing (asset overstated).

mcqData.text: Trial Balance: 15 TK\%TK Investment (31 TK-03 TK-2024 TK) 50,000 TK. Interest received 1,500 TK. Total interest for the year?
mcqData.explanation: Interest for 9 TK months: (50,000 TK \times 15\% \times 9/12 TK) = 5,625 TK

mcqData.text: Non-operating expenses include— i. Interest on Investment ii. Interest on Loan iii. Miscellaneous Expenses
mcqData.explanation: Interest on loan is a financing (non-operating) cost. Interest on investment is income, not expense.

mcqData.text: Elements of financial position are— i. Assets ii. Liabilities iii. Owner's Equity
mcqData.explanation: These three components make up the basic accounting equation: Assets = Liabilities + Owner's Equity.

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold is 5,00,000 TK. Profit is 20 TK\%TK on Sales. What is the total Sales amount?
mcqData.explanation: If Sales is 100 TK\%TK and Profit 20 TK\%TK, Cost of Goods Sold is 80 TK\%TK. Sales = (5,00,000 TK / 80) \times 100 = 6,25,000 TK

mcqData.text: To increase Net Profit rate while Gross Profit rate is fixed— i. Increase sales ii. Decrease operating expenses iii. Increase non-operating income
mcqData.explanation: Net profit depends on what happens after Gross Profit. Cutting costs (ii) or adding other income (iii) works.

mcqData.text: Opening Stock + Net Purchase + Direct Expenses - Closing Stock = ?
mcqData.explanation: This is the standard formula for Cost of Goods Sold (বিক্রীত পণ্যের ব্যয়).

mcqData.text: Operating expenses are— i. Office Rent ii. Advertisement iii. Interest on Loan
mcqData.explanation: Office rent and ads are part of daily operations. Loan interest is a non-operating financing cost.

mcqData.text: Opening 22 TKkTK, Purchase 1.35 TKLTK, Wages 10 TKkTK, Sales 3.75 TKLTK, Closing 36 TKkTK. Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 22 TKk+1.35L+10k-36k = 1,31,000 TK. Gross Profit = Sales (3.75 TKLTK) - Cost of Goods Sold (1.31 TKLTK) = 2,44,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on the stem above (Q18), what is the Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = Opening (22,000 TK) + Purchase (1,35,000 TK) + Wages (10,000 TK) - Closing (36,000 TK) = 1,31,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is Non-operating Income? i. Interest Received ii. Discount Received iii. Profit on Sale of Asset
mcqData.explanation: Interest and profit on asset sales are non-operational. Discount received is an operating item related to purchases.

mcqData.text: Gross Profit - Operating Expenses = ?
mcqData.explanation: Operating Profit is derived by subtracting operating expenses (admin & selling) from Gross Profit.

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold 2,00,000 TK; Gross Profit 1,00,000 TK. What is the amount of Sales?
mcqData.explanation: Sales = Cost of Goods Sold + Gross Profit = 2,00,000 TK + 1,00,000 TK = 3,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: Gross Profit 1,50,000 TK; Operating Expense 1,00,000 TK; Interest on Loan 20,000 TK; Discount Receivable. 30,000 TK. Net Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Net Profit = (Gross Profit 1,50,000 TK - Opening Expense 1,00,000 TK) + Non-Opening Income 30,000 TK - Non-Opening Expense 20,000 TK = 60,000 TK

mcqData.text: Who has the claim over Net Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Net Profit belongs to the owner(s) and is added to the Owner's Equity in the Balance Sheet.

mcqData.text: Accounts Payable Opening 5,000 TK; Closing 8,000 TK; Purchase Return 2,000 TK; Paid to Creditors 20,000 TK. Credit Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Credit Purchase = (Closing 8k + Paid 20k + Return 2k) - Opening 5k = 25,000 TK

mcqData.text: Stock Cost 1,00,000 TK; Market 8,000 TK less than cost. Includes Unused Stationery 2,000 TK. Final Stock Value?
mcqData.explanation: Lower of Cost/Market = 92,000 TK. Subtract non-stock items: 92,000 TK - 2,000 TK = 90,000 TK

mcqData.text: If Market Value is 5,000 TK HIGHER than cost, in the Balance Sheet— i. Assets ↑ ii. Net Profit ↑ iii. Equity ↑
mcqData.explanation: Following the prudence concept, we usually use cost if it's lower. However, if market value is correctly used/rises, it inflates assets and profits.

mcqData.text: Net Sales 3,90,000 TK; Gross Profit rate 40%; Net Profit is 30% of Gross Profit. Indirect Expenses?
What type of asset is "Preliminary Expenses"?
mcqData.explanation: Preliminary expenses are fictitious assets (অলীেক সম্পদ) as they have no real resale value and are written off over time.

mcqData.text: If VAT Received > VAT Paid, the difference is called—
mcqData.explanation: Excess VAT collected from customers over VAT paid to suppliers is a liability (VAT Payable) to the government.

mcqData.text: Sales 50,000 TK; Opening Expense 3,200 TK; Cost of Goods Sold 30,000 TK; Discount Receivable. 2,000 TK. Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = Sales (50,000 TK) - Cost of Goods Sold (30,000 TK) = 20,000 TK (Other items are for Net Profit).

mcqData.text: Sales - Cost of Goods Sold - Operating Expenses = ?
mcqData.explanation: Subtracting both Cost of Goods Sold and operating expenses from sales yields the Operating Profit.

mcqData.text: Stat in Trial Balance 1,000 TK. Opening Stock includes 150 stat; Closing includes 350 stat. Stationery used?
mcqData.explanation: Used = (Opening 150 + Trial Balance 1,000 TK) - Closing 350 = 800 TK

mcqData.text: If 150 Unused Stationery is recorded— i. Closing stock overstated ii. Net profit decreases iii. Stationery Expense decreases
mcqData.explanation: Identifying an unused asset reduces the year's expense (iii) and keeps assets correct. It does not inflate stock if categorized separately.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Intangible Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Goodwill (সুনাম) is an intangible asset as it has value but no physical substance.

mcqData.text: Rent paid 12,000 TK (2/3 portion). How much is Rent Payable?
mcqData.explanation: If 2/3 = 12,000 TK, then 1/3 (payable) = 6,000 TK. Total rent is 18,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which is a Non-operating Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Interest on loan is a financial expense, not related to the core daily operations of the business.

mcqData.text: How many elements are in the Balance Sheet (Accounting Equation)?
mcqData.explanation: The three elements are Assets, Liabilities, and Owner's Equity (TKA = Liability + OETK).

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold 4,50,000 TK. Profit 25% on Sales. Total Sales?
mcqData.explanation: If Sales = 100%, Profit = 25%, then Cost of Goods Sold = 75%. Sales = (4,50,000 TK / 75) * 100 = 6,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: To increase Net Profit rate while Gross Profit rate is fixed— i. Increase sales ii. Decrease operating expenses iii. Increase non-operating income
mcqData.explanation: Net Profit is improved by managing costs (ii) or increasing other income (iii) after the Gross Profit is calculated.

mcqData.text: Ad paid 60,000 TK, Free goods distribution 15,000 TK. Total Ad is to be written off over 5 TK years. Amount of Deferred Ad?
mcqData.explanation: Total Ad = 60 TKk + 15k = 75kTK. Current year write-off (1 TK/5 TK) = 15,000 TK. Deferred Ad (4 TK/5 TK) = 60,000 TK

Read the following stimulus and answer the question: Trial Balance (Partial) Particulars | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) Service Revenue | | 1,40,000 TK Unearned Revenue | | 60,000 TK Adjustment: Service Revenue of 20,000 TK has not been recorded. Unearned Revenue60,000 TK

Read the following stimulus and answer the question: Trial Balance (Partial) Particulars | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) Service Revenue | | 1,40,000 TK Unearned Revenue | | 60,000 TK Adjustment: Service Revenue of 20,000 TK has not been recorded. Q. If 3/4 portion of Unearned Service Revenue is earned, how much will be shown as Unearned Service Revenue in the Statement of Financial Position?

ParticularsDebit (TK)Credit (TK)
Service Revenue1,40,000 TK
Unearned Revenue60,000 TK
mcqData.explanation: Total Unearned = 60,000 TK. Earned = 60,000 TK \times 3/4 = 45,000 TK. Remaining Unearned = 60,000 TK - 45,000 TK = 15,000 TK

Based on the stimulus below, answer the following question: Pinky Traders' Partial Trial Balance and Adjustments are as follows: Partial Trial Balance Particulars | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) Accounts Receivable | 61,000 TK | Bad Debt | 2,000 TK | Allowance for Bad Debt | | 3,500 TK Adjustment: 1,000 TK of Accounts Receivable is not collectible and Allowance for Bad Debt must be increased to 5,000 TK Q. How much Bad Debt Expense will be charged in the Comprehensive Income Statement?

Bad Debt2,000 TK
Allowance for Bad Debt3,500 TK
mcqData.explanation: Expense = Old Bad Debt (2 TKkTK) + New Bad Debt (1 TKkTK) + New Allowance (5 TKkTK) - Old Allowance (3.5 TKkTK) = 4,500 TK

mcqData.text:
Based on the stimulus below, answer the following question: Pinky Traders' Partial Trial Balance and Adjustments are as follows: Partial Trial Balance Particulars | Debit (TK) | Credit (TK) Accounts Receivable | 61,000 TK | Bad Debt | 2,000 TK | Allowance for Bad Debt | | 3,500 TK Adjustment: 1,000 TK of Accounts Receivable is not collectible and Allowance for Bad Debt must be increased to 5,000 TK Q. mcqData.text: Sales including 15 TK\%TK VAT 1,18,000 TK. Sales Return 3,000 TK. Amount of Output VAT?
VAT collected/received by a seller is a/an—
mcqData.explanation: VAT collected from customers is a liability because the business must Payable it to the government.

mcqData.text: Formula for Cost of Goods Sold (Cost of Goods Sold) is— i. Cost of Goods Available for Sale - Closing Stock ii. Sales - Gross Profit iii. Adjusted Purchase + Direct Expenses
mcqData.explanation: All three are valid mathematical ways to calculate Cost of Goods Sold depending on the available information.

Based on the stimulus below, answer the following question: Some accounting information of Padma Enterprise is given below: Particulars | 2023 | 2024 Gross Profit | 2,10,000 TK | 2,60,000 TK Net Profit | 31,500 TK | ? In 2024, Office and Administrative Expenses were 1,77,000 TK (including Depreciation of 77,000 TK), Selling and Distribution Expenses were 80,000 TK, and Miscellaneous Income was 20,000 TK Q. What is the amount of Net Profit for Padma Enterprise in 2024?

mcqData.text: Profit on Sales is 20 TK\%TK. What is the profit percentage on Cost of Goods Sold?
Net Profit increases what?
mcqData.explanation: Net profit is added to the Capital, thus increasing Owner's Equity (স্বত্ত্বাধিকার).

mcqData.text: Opening Stock 10 TKkTK, Purchase 100 TKkTK, Return 7.5 TKkTK, Discount 2.5 TKkTK, Wages 3 TKkTK, Excise Duty 12 TKkTK, Closing 25 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 10 TKk + (100k-7.5k-2.5k) + 3k + 12k - 25k = 90,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on Q53, if profit is 20 TK\%TK on Sales, what is the Gross Profit amount?
mcqData.explanation: If Sales is 100 TK\%TK, Cost of Goods Sold is 80 TK\%TK. 90,000 TK is 80 TK\%TK. Total Sales = 1,12,500 TK. Gross Profit = 1,12,500 TK \times 20\% = 22,500 TK

mcqData.text: Interest received 5,000 TK, accrued 500 TK. Rate 10 TK\%TK. Total Investment amount?
mcqData.explanation: Total Interest = 5,000 TK + 500 = 5,500 TK. Investment = (5,500 TK / 10\%TK) = 55,000 TK

mcqData.text: Salary paid 30,000 TK, including 3 TK months advance. Current year salary expense?
mcqData.explanation: 30,000 TK is for 15 TK months (12 TK+3 TK). Monthly = 2,000 TK. Current year (12 TK months) = 24,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Fictitious Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Underwriter's commission is a deferred expense (fictitious asset). The others are intangible/fixed assets.

mcqData.text: Purchase incl. VAT 71,000 TK; Return 2,000 TK. Input VAT amount?
mcqData.explanation: Net Purchase = 71 TKk - 2k = 69,000 TK. VAT = 69,000 TK \times 15/115 = 9,000 TK

mcqData.text: If Profit on Cost is 25 TK\%TK, what is the profit percentage on Sales?
mcqData.explanation: If Cost = 100 TK and Profit = 25 TK, Sales = 125 TK. Profit on Sales = (25 TK/125) \times 100 = 20\%TK.

mcqData.text: If Net Profit increases from TK 60,000 TK to TK 80,000 TK, what is the effect on Total Assets?
mcqData.explanation: Net profit is an addition to Equity, and according to TKA=Liability+ETK, an increase in profit (retained) reflects an equal increase in assets.

mcqData.text: Purchase TK 4,00,000 TK; Opening Stock TK 30,000 TK; Cost of Goods Sold TK 3,00,000 TK; Gross Profit TK 2,00,000 TK. Sales?
mcqData.explanation: Sales = Cost of Goods Sold + Gross Profit = 3,00,000 TK + 2,00,000 TK = 5,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on Q63, what is the value of Closing Stock?
mcqData.explanation: Closing Stock = (Opening. Stock + Purchase) - Cost of Goods Sold = (30 TKk + 4,00kTK) - 3,00 TKk = 1,30,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is part of Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Carriage Inward (আন্তঃপরিবহন) is a direct expense related to bringing goods to the warehouse.

mcqData.text: Manager gets 10 TK\%TK commission on Net Profit. Profit after commission is TK 44,000 TK. Commission?
mcqData.explanation: If 44,000 TK is the profit after commission, it represents the 100 TK\%TK base. Commission = 44,000 TK \times 10\% = 4,400 TK

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 60 TKkTK; Cost of Goods Pur. 3.8 TKLTK; Closing. Stock 50 TKkTK; Opening. Expense 15 TKkTK; Sales 4.5 TKLTK. Gross Profit & NP?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 60 TKk+3.8L-50k = 3.9LTK. Gross Profit = 4.5 TKL-3.9L=60kTK. NP = 60 TKk-15k=45,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on Q67, what is the Cost of Goods Sold (Cost of Goods Sold)?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = Opening Stock + Cost of Goods Purchased - Closing Stock = 60,000 TK + 3,80,000 TK - 50,000 TK = 3,90,000 TK

mcqData.text: Headers written while preparing a Balance Sheet include— i. Name of Entity ii. Name of Statement iii. Date of preparation
mcqData.explanation: Asset complete heading requires the business name, the statement name, and the specific date (e.g., "As of Dec 31").

mcqData.text: Asset company forgot to adjust TK 35,000 TK for outstanding salary. Impact?
mcqData.explanation: Omitting an expense makes the profit look higher (overstated) than it actually is.

mcqData.text: Closing Capital 30 TKkTK which is 25 TK\%TK less than Opening Capital. Interest on Capital @ 5 TK\%TK?
mcqData.explanation: 30 TKkTK is 75 TK\%TK of Opening. Capital. Opening. Capital = 30 TKk / 0.75 = 40,000 TK. Interest = 40,000 TK \times 5\% = 2,000 TK

mcqData.text: Assets TK 5 TKLTK; Opening. Capital TK 2 TKLTK; NP TK 50 TKkTK; Drawings TK 40 TKkTK. If Assets become 4.8 TKLTK, effect on Internal Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Internal Liability (Equity) is determined by Capital, Profit, and Drawings. Total assets changing doesn't change Equity unless the Profit/Capital figures change.

mcqData.text: Based on Q72, what is the amount of External Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Equity = 2 TKL + 50k - 40k = 2.1LTK. Ext. Liability = Assets (5 TKLTK) - Equity (2.1 TKLTK) = 2.9 TKLTK (2,90,000 TK).

mcqData.text: If 10 TK\%TK Bad Debt Allowance is charged on Accounts Receivable, impact on financial position?
mcqData.explanation: Allowance reduces Net Accounts Receivable (Asset ↓) and the corresponding expense reduces Net Profit (Equity ↓).

mcqData.text: What is found by adding Gross Profit to the Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: The basic relationship is: Sales = Cost of Goods Sold + Gross Profit.

mcqData.text: Opening. Capital 1.5 TKLTK; Drawings 10 TKkTK; Cost of Goods Sold 1.5 TKLTK; Sales 2.5 TKLTK; Net Profit 60 TKkTK. Closing Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Closing Capital = Opening. Capital + Net Profit - Drawings = 1,50,000 TK + 60,000 TK - 10,000 TK = 2,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on Q76, if Cost of Goods Sold increases by 10 TK\%TK, effect on Financial Statements? i. NP ↑ ii. NP ↓ iii. Equity ↓
mcqData.explanation: Increased Cost of Goods Sold reduces Gross Profit, which in turn reduces Net Profit (ii) and Equity (iii).

mcqData.text: Gross Profit is 10 TK\%TK of Sales. If Gross Profit is TK 20,000 TK, then Sales is—
mcqData.explanation: Sales = Gross Profit / Rate = 20,000 TK / 0.10 = 2,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: VAT is included in Sales. What type of account is VAT?
mcqData.explanation: Collected VAT is a liability (VAT Payable) that must be remitted to the government.

mcqData.text: Stock TK 65 TKkTK; incl. 7 TKkTK fire loss (80 TK\%TK insured) and Unused Supplies 4 TKkTK. Effect of fire on Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Net Loss = Total Loss (7,000 TK) - Ins. Claim (7,000 TK \times 80\% = 5,600 TK) = 1,400 TK This is an expense.

mcqData.text: Closing Stock valued at TK 65,000 TK. Includes TK 7,000 TK fire loss and TK 4,000 TK unused supplies. Actual Closing Stock?
mcqData.explanation: Actual Stock = 65,000 TK - 7,000 TK (Fire loss) - 4,000 TK (Supplies) = 54,000 TK

mcqData.text: Sales TK 2,00,000 TK; Closing Stock TK 30,000 TK; Cost of Goods Sold TK 1,40,000 TK. What is the Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = Sales - Cost of Goods Sold = 2,00,000 TK - 1,40,000 TK = 60,000 TK

mcqData.text: Sales 2 TKLTK; Cost of Goods Sold 1.4 TKLTK; Opening. Expense is 50 TK\%TK of Cost of Goods Sold. Effect of Net Profit/Loss on Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit=60 TKkTK. Opening. Expense = 1.4 TKL \times 50\% = 70kTK. Net Loss = 10 TKkTK. Loss reduces Capital (i) and Internal Liability (iii).

mcqData.text: Who can claim the Net Income?
mcqData.explanation: Net Income belongs to the owner of the business and is added to their equity.

mcqData.text: On which concept are financial statements prepared?
mcqData.explanation: Periodicity (সময়কাল ধারণা) dictates that business life is divided into specific periods to report performance.

mcqData.text: What is Capital for a business organization?
mcqData.explanation: Capital is the obligation of the business towards its owner, hence an Internal Liability (অন্তর্দায়).

mcqData.text: Prepaid Rent TK 20,000 TK expired. Effect on Financial Statements? i. Prepaid Rent ↓ 20 TKkTK ii. Current Assets ↓ 20 TKkTK iii. Rent Expense ↓ 20 TKkTK
mcqData.explanation: Expiry reduces the asset (Prepaid Rent) and increases the expense. It does not decrease the expense.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock TK 60 TKkTK. Net Purchase TK 1.5 TKLTK. Closing Stock Cost TK 30 TKkTK. Adjusted Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusted Purchase = Opening. Stock + Net Purchase - Closing Stock = 60 TKk + 1.5L - 30k = 1,80,000 TK

mcqData.text: Sales 80.7 TKkTK; Cost of Goods Sold 50.5 TKkTK; Opening. Expense 10 TKkTK. Gross Profit and Net Profit?
Rent paid is 125 TK\%TK equal to TK 50,000 TK. What is the amount of Prepaid Rent?
Ad paid TK 10,000 TK for 5 TK years. Amount to be written off at year-end?
mcqData.explanation: Annual write-off = Total / Years = 10,000 TK / 5 = 2,000 TK

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 60 TKkTK; Net Pur. 1.5 TKLTK; Closing Stock Cost 30 TKkTK. If Market Value of Closing. Stock is 20 TKkTK, effect on Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Using 20 TKkTK instead of 30 TKkTK increases Cost of Goods Sold by 10 TKkTK, which reduces Gross Profit by 10,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Non-operating expense?
mcqData.explanation: Interest on loan is a financing cost, separate from the main business operations.

mcqData.text: Through which statement are financial results (profit/loss) known?
mcqData.explanation: The Comprehensive Income Statement (বিশদ আয় বিবরণী) shows the profit or loss for the period.

mcqData.text: Prepaid Insurance 1.2 TKLTK in Trial Balance (paid on 01 TK-04 TK-23 TK for 2 TK yrs). Year ends 30 TK-06 TK-23 TK. Insurance Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Monthly = 1,20,000 TK / 24 = 5,000 TK. Expense for 3 TK months (April-June) = 5,000 TK \times 3 = 15,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on Q95, omitting insurance expense violates which principle?
mcqData.explanation: The Matching Principle (মিলকরণ নীতি) requires matching revenues with the expenses incurred to earn them in the same period.

mcqData.text: Invested 1.2 TKLTK @ 10 TK\%TK. On Dec 31, accrued interest 5,000 TK was adjusted. When was the investment made?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Interest = 12 TKkTK. Monthly = 1 TKkTK. 5,000 TK Interest means 5 TK months (Aug, Sept, Oct, Nov, Dec). So, August 1.

mcqData.text: Operating expenses are— i. Purchase of goods ii. Advertisement iii. Interest on Loan
mcqData.explanation: Only advertisement is an operating expense. Purchase is part of Cost of Goods Sold, and loan interest is non-operating.

mcqData.text: What type of account is VAT for a seller?
mcqData.explanation: VAT collected from customers is a debt owed to the government.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 50 TKkTK. Net Purchase 1.25 TKLTK. Closing Stock Cost 15 TKkTK. Adjusted Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusted Purchase = Opening Stock + Net Purchase - Closing Stock = 50,000 TK + 1,25,000 TK - 15,000 TK = 1,60,000 TK

mcqData.text: Closing. Stock cost TK 15,000 TK. If Market Value is TK 12,000 TK, effect on Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: We use the lower value (12 TKkTK instead of 15 TKkTK). Since Closing. Stock is subtracted from cost, a lower value means higher Cost of Goods Sold, which decreases Gross Profit by 3,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Revenue Income?
mcqData.explanation: Sales is the primary revenue income (মুনাফা জাতীয় আয়) for a trading business.

mcqData.text: What type of asset is "Cash at Bank"?
mcqData.explanation: Cash at bank is a liquid asset that can be used within a year, making it a Current Asset.

mcqData.text: To adjust Additional Capital in Balance Sheet— i. Add to Investment ii. Add to Cash iii. Add to Capital
mcqData.explanation: Additional capital increases both the asset (Cash) and the liability toward the owner (Capital).

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Indirect Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Export duty (রপ্তানি শুল্ক) is a selling expense (indirect), while others are direct expenses related to purchase.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Fictitious Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Preliminary expenses (প্রাথমিক খরচ) are capitalized costs with no resale value, classified as fictitious.

mcqData.text: Financial Statements include— i. Income Statement ii. Equity Statement iii. Balance Sheet
mcqData.explanation: These are the three core components of a complete set of financial statements at this level.

mcqData.text: Which is a Revenue Income?
mcqData.explanation: Sub-let rent (উপভাড়া) is a regular operating/non-operating income for the business.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 20 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 40 TKkTK, Pur. 90 TKkTK, Carriage Inward 10 TKkTK, Sales 1.2 TKLTK. Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 20 TKk+90k+10k-40k = 80kTK. Gross Profit = 1,20,000 TK - 80,000 TK = 40,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on Q109, what is the Cost of Goods Sold (Cost of Goods Sold)?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = Opening Stock + Purchase + Carriage Inward - Closing Stock = 20 TKk + 90k + 10k - 40k = 80,000 TK

mcqData.text: Under which heading is "Mortgage Loan" placed?
mcqData.explanation: Mortgage loans are typically repaid over several years, classifying them as Long-term Liabilities.

mcqData.text: Purposes of Income Statement are— i. Determine Gross Profit/Loss ii. Determine NP/Loss iii. Determine Tax Liability
mcqData.explanation: It measures operational performance and the resulting profit which is the base for tax calculation.

mcqData.text: Ad Expense 50 TKkTK, Free goods 10 TKkTK. Total Ad written off over 4 TK years. Deferred Ad?
mcqData.explanation: Total = 60 TKkTK. Current Expense (1 TK/4 TK) = 15 TKkTK. Deferred (3 TK/4 TK) = 45,000 TK

mcqData.text: Sales incl. VAT 59,000 TK. Return 1,500 TK. Sales VAT amount?
mcqData.explanation: Net Sales = 59 TKk - 1.5k = 57,500 TK. VAT = 57,500 TK \times 15/115 = 7,500 TK

mcqData.text: Which group represents Fictitious Assets?
options:
on Share

b) Patent/Copyright

c) Trademark/Def. Ad
mcqData.explanation: Preliminary expenses and Underwriter's commission are classic examples of fictitious assets.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 40 TKkTK, Net Pur. 1.5 TKLTK, Export Duty 15 TKkTK, Closing. Stock Cost 30 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 40 TKk + 1.5L - 30k = 1,60,000 TK (Export duty is an indirect selling expense, so not in Cost of Goods Sold).

mcqData.text: From Q116: If Closing. Stock Market Value is 20 TKkTK and Gross Profit rate is 20 TK\%TK on Sales, Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: New Cost of Goods Sold = 40 TKk+1.5L-20k = 1.7LTK. If Gross Profit is 20 TK\%TK on sales, it's 25 TK\%TK on cost. Gross Profit = 1.7 TKL \times 25\% = 42,500 TK

mcqData.text: Loan 1 TKLTK, Accrued Interest 12 TKkTK, Accounts Payable 40 TKkTK, Unearned Rent 40 TKkTK, Fixed Asset 3 TKLTK. Current Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Current Liability = Accrued Interest (12 TKkTK) + Accounts Payable (40 TKkTK) + Unearned Rent (40 TKkTK) = 92,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on Q118, what is the Total Liability of the firm?
mcqData.explanation: Total Liability = Loan (1 TKLTK) + Current Liability (92 TKkTK) = 1,92,000 TK (Capital is owner's equity, not external Liability).

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Intangible Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Patent (পেটেন্ট) is an intangible asset; the rest are tangible fixed assets.

mcqData.text: According to IAS-01, how many components are there in a complete set of Financial Statements?
mcqData.explanation: IAS-01 specifies 5 parts: Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Equity Statement, Cash Flow, and Notes.

mcqData.text: What is the primary objective of an Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: The ultimate goal of the Income Statement is to determine the final Net Profit or Loss for the period.

mcqData.text: Adjustments must be made in the Income Statement for— i. Depreciation ii. Bad Debt iii. Loan taken
mcqData.explanation: Depreciation and Bad Debt are expenses. Taking a loan is a balance sheet (liability) item and not an adjustment for revenue/expense.

mcqData.text: Purchase TK 1,00,000 TK; Opening. Stock TK 10,000 TK; Cost of Goods Sold TK 90,000 TK; Gross Profit TK 30,000 TK. Sales?
mcqData.explanation: Sales = Cost of Goods Sold + Gross Profit = 90,000 TK + 30,000 TK = 1,20,000 TK

mcqData.text: Based on Q124, what is the amount of Closing Stock?
mcqData.explanation: Closing. Stock = (Opening. Stock + Purchase) - Cost of Goods Sold = (10 TKk + 100kTK) - 90 TKk = 20,000 TK

mcqData.text: Ad Expense 30 TKkTK; Free goods 20 TKkTK. If 3 TK/4 TK is to be deferred, amount of Deferred Ad?
mcqData.explanation: Total Ad = 30 TKk + 20k = 50kTK. Deferred = 50,000 TK \times 3/4 = 37,500 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Direct Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Wages (মজুরি) are directly related to production or acquisition of goods, hence a direct expense.

mcqData.text: Sales 20 TKkTK; Opening. Stock 5.5 TKkTK; Closing. Stock 2.5 TKkTK; Pur. 15 TKkTK; Carriage Inward 1 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 5.5 TKk + 15k + 1k - 2.5k = 19,000 TK

mcqData.text: Effect of not recording a Revenue Expense at year-end— i. Gross Profit increases ii. NP increases iii. Net Assets increase
mcqData.explanation: Omitting an indirect expense (like rent) doesn't affect Gross Profit, but it inflates NP and Equity (Net Assets).

mcqData.text: Which of the following is shown on the Liability side of the Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: Unearned income (অনুপার্জিত আয়) is a liability because the service/product is yet to be delivered.

mcqData.text: What is "Allowance for Doubtful Accounts"?
mcqData.explanation: It is a provision against a probable loss (সম্ভাব্য ক্ষতিপূরণ/সঞ্চিতি) from non-payment by debtors.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Intangible Asset?
options:
on Share

b) Deferred Ad

c) Goodwill
mcqData.explanation: Goodwill (সুনাম) is an intangible asset. Others in the list are fictitious assets (অলীক সম্পদ).

mcqData.text: Net Sales 6 TKLTK. Profit is 20 TK\%TK on Cost of Goods Sold. What is the Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: If Cost of Goods Sold is 100 TK\%TK, Sales is 120 TK\%TK. 6,00,000 TK / 1.2 = 5,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q133: If Gross Profit is 1 TKLTK and Opening. Expense is 60 TK\%TK of Gross Profit, what is the Net Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = 6 TKL - 5L = 1LTK. Opening. Expense = 1 TKL \times 60\% = 60kTK. NP = 1 TKL - 60k = 40,000 TK

mcqData.text: Advanced Apprentice Premium is a/an— i. Current Asset ii. Current Liability iii. Unearned Income
mcqData.explanation: Apprentice premium is an income; receiving it in advance is a liability/unearned income.

mcqData.text: Ad Expense 50,000 TK. If 2 TK/5 TK is written off, what is the Deferred Ad expense?
mcqData.explanation: Written off = 2 TK/5 TK. Remaining Deferred = 3 TK/5 TK. 50,000 TK \times 3/5 = 30,000 TK

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 10 TKkTK, Pur. 50 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 20 TKkTK, Carriage In 5 TKkTK, Wages 5 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 10 TKk + 50k + 5k + 5k - 20k = 50,000 TK (Indirect expenses like rent/salary are excluded).

mcqData.text: From Q137: Carriage Out 2 TKkTK, Salary 4 TKkTK (-500 TK adv), Rent 1 TKkTK (+1 TKkTK due). Total Indirect Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Ind. Expense = 2 TKkTK (Carr. Out) + 3.5 TKkTK (Net Salary) + 2 TKkTK (Net Rent) = 7,500 TK

mcqData.text: Ad 1.8 TKLTK, Free goods 20 TKkTK. Write-off over 4 TK years. Ad expense for 2021 TK in Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Total = 2,00,000 TK. Annual write-off (1 TK/4 TK) = 50,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q139: What will be the Deferred Ad in Balance Sheet for the year 2022 TK?
mcqData.explanation: After 2021 TK & 2022 TK (2 TK years), 2 TK/4 TK is written off. Remaining 2 TK/4 = 1,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: If 10 TK\%TK depreciation for 6 TK months is TK 2,000 TK, what is the total value of the asset?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Depreciation. = 2,000 TK \times 2 = 4,000 TK. Since rate is 10 TK\%TK, Asset Value = 4,000 TK / 0.10 = 40,000 TK

mcqData.text: Non-operating expenses are— i. Interest. on Bank Overdraft ii. Apprentice Allowance iii. Interest. on Notes Payable
mcqData.explanation: All three are financing costs or non-core business activities, hence non-operating expenses.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a purchase-related expense?
mcqData.explanation: Carriage inward (ক্রয় পরিবহন) is directly added to the cost of purchasing goods.

mcqData.text: Profit is 33 TK \frac{1}{3}\%TK on Sales. What is the profit rate on Cost?
mcqData.explanation: 33 TK \frac{1}{3}\%TK on Sales is 1 TK/3 TKrd. Thus, on Cost it is 1 TK/2 TK or 50 TK\%TK.

mcqData.text: Unearned Revenue TK 40,000 TK. If 3 TK/5 TK is earned, what is the Current Liability at year-end?
mcqData.explanation: Remaining Liability = 2 TK/5 TK of 40,000 TK = 16,000 TK

mcqData.text: What does "Unearned Income" represent?
mcqData.explanation: Unearned income is a liability because the service/good is still owed to the customer.

mcqData.text: Opening. Capital 1.7 TKLTK, Drawing 20 TKkTK, Cost of Goods Sold 1.6 TKLTK, Sales 2.7 TKLTK, Net Profit 50 TKkTK. Owner's Equity?
mcqData.explanation: Equity = Opening. Capital + NP - Drawing = 1,70,000 TK + 50,000 TK - 20,000 TK = 2,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q147: If Cost of Goods Sold was 10 TK\%TK less, what would be the impact? i. NP increases ii. Sales increase iii. Equity increases
mcqData.explanation: Lower Cost of Goods Sold increases profit (i) and thus increases equity (iii). It has no effect on Sales (ii).

mcqData.text: What is the purpose of preparing the Statement of Owner's Equity?
options:
Capital

b) Find Opening. Capital

c) Find Closing Capital

d) Find Retained Earnings
mcqData.explanation: It tracks the change from opening capital to closing capital (সমাপনী মূলধন) for the period.

mcqData.text: Started business with: Cash 50 TKkTK, Credit Purchase 25 TKkTK, 10 TK\%TK Loan 50 TKkTK. Opening Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Capital = Assets (Cash 50 TKkTK) - Liability (Accounts Payable 25 TKkTK + Loan 50 TKkTK) = ... Wait, logic: Capital = Assets 50 TKkTK - Liability 25 TKkTK (Loan is usually cash). Result: 25,000 TK.

mcqData.text: From Q150: If Loan interest is not recorded, effect on Financial Statements? i. Expense ↓ 5 TKkTK ii. Liability ↓ 5 TKkTK iii. Equity ↓ 5 TKkTK
mcqData.explanation: Omitting interest means expenses are understated (i) and liabilities (interest payable) are understated (ii).

mcqData.text: Sales 2 TKLTK, Gross Profit 75 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 15 TKkTK, Net Purchase 1.3 TKLTK. Opening Stock?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 2 TKL - 75k = 1.25LTK. Opening. Stock = Cost of Goods Sold + Closing. Stock - Purchase = 1.25 TKL + 15k - 1.3L = 10,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q152: If Closing Stock value is increased by 5,000 TK then— i. Opening. Stock increases ii. Current Asset increases iii. Equity increases
mcqData.explanation: Increased Closing. Stock increases Current Assets (ii) and decreases Cost of Goods Sold, which increases Profit/Equity (iii).

mcqData.text: Closing. Stock Cost 1.5 TKLTK, Market 1.7 TKLTK. Includes 6 TKkTK fire loss (insured) and 4 TKkTK unused stationery. Closing. Stock in BS?
mcqData.explanation: Closing. Stock = Cost (1.5 TKLTK) - Fire Loss (6 TKkTK) - Stationery (4 TKkTK) = 1,40,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q154: Fire loss 6 TKkTK, Insurance covers 50 TK\%TK. Net fire loss in Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Net Fire Loss = Total Loss (6 TKkTK) - Insurance Claim (3 TKkTK) = 3,000 TK

mcqData.text: Fixed Assets 60 TKkTK, Total Liability 50 TKkTK, Equity 90 TKkTK. Current Assets?
mcqData.explanation: Total Assets = Liability + Equity = 50 TKk + 90k = 1.4LTK. Current Assets = 1.4 TKL - 60k = 80,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT shown in Financial Statements?
mcqData.explanation: Secret reserves (গোপন সঞ্চিতি) are intentionally hidden and not disclosed in financial reports.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock + Net Purchase + Carriage Inward - Closing. Stock = ?
mcqData.explanation: This is the standard formula for Cost of Goods Sold (বিক্রীত পণ্যের ব্যয়).

mcqData.text: Closing. Stock cost 40 TKkTK, Market value 50 TKkTK. Amount to be in Financial Statements?
mcqData.explanation: According to the Prudence/Conservatism principle, the lower of cost or market value is taken.

mcqData.text: Based on Q159, which principle was followed in valuing closing stock?
mcqData.explanation: Conservatism Principle (রক্ষণশীলতার নীতি) ensures assets are not overstated and liabilities not understated.

mcqData.text: Net Profit of a business organization indicates—
mcqData.explanation: Net profit belongs to the owner, increasing their equity. Thus, it is an Internal Liability (অভ্যন্তরীণ দায়).

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 10 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 15 TKkTK, Carriage In 1 TKkTK, Carriage Out 500 TK, Purchase 50 TKkTK, Sales 1 TKLTK, Rent 1.5 TKkTK. Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 10 TKk+50k+1k-15k = 46kTK. Gross Profit = 1,00,000 TK - 46,000 TK = 54,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q162: What is the total Indirect Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Indirect Expense = Carriage Out (500 TK) + Rent (1.5 TKkTK) = 2,000 TK

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold 6 TKLTK, Opening. Expense 55 TKkTK, Interest. on Capital 20 TKkTK, Profit rate 20 TK\%TK on Sales. Sales price?
mcqData.explanation: If Gross Profit is 20 TK\%TK on Sales, it's 25 TK\%TK on Cost. Sales = 6,00,000 TK \times 1.25 = 7,50,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q164: What is the Net Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = 7.5 TKL - 6L = 1.5LTK. NP = 1.5 TKL - 55kTK (Opening Expense) - 20 TKkTK (Non-Opening Expense) = 75,000 TK

mcqData.text: Closing Capital 30 TKkTK, which is 20 TK\%TK less than Opening. Capital. If Interest on Capital is 5 TK\%TK, what is the interest amount?
mcqData.explanation: Opening. Capital = 30 TKk / 0.80 = 37,500 TK. Interest = 37,500 TK \times 5\% = 1,875 TK

mcqData.text: Why does the Balance Sheet match (Asset=Liability+E)?
mcqData.explanation: The fundamental principle of double-entry bookkeeping ensures that total assets always equal total liabilities and equity.

mcqData.text: Trial Balance: Insurance 2,400 TK paid annually on March 31. Insurance expense in Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Paid for Apr-Mar (12 TK months). Year ends Dec. Months used = 9 TK. Expense = 2,400 TK \times 9/12 = 1,800 TK

mcqData.text: Financial Statements provide info on— i) Financial Results ii) Financial Position iii) Cash Flows
mcqData.explanation: These are the core outputs of a complete set of financial reports.

mcqData.text: Capital 5 TKLTK, 12 TK\%TK Loan 1 TKLTK, Accrued Interest 12 TKkTK, Accounts Payable 40 TKkTK, Unearned Rent 4 TKkTK. Current Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Current Liability = Accrued Interest (12 TKkTK) + Accounts Payable (40 TKkTK) + Unearned Rent (4 TKkTK) = 56,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q170: What is the Total Asset of the firm?
mcqData.explanation: Total Assets = Capital (5 TKLTK) + Loan (1 TKLTK) + Current Liability (56 TKkTK) = 6,56,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Current Asset?
options:
Depreciation.

b) Cash Account

c) 10 TK\%TK Investment
mcqData.explanation: Cash (নগদান) is the most liquid current asset.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is shown in the Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Actual expenses incurred during the period (Paid +/- Adjustments) are shown in the Income Statement.

mcqData.text: Total Loan 70 TKkTK. 20 TKkTK to be paid next year. Long-term Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Long-term Liability = Total Loan - Current portion = 70 TKk - 20k = 50,000 TK

mcqData.text: Ad Expense 20,000 TK. If 3 TK/5 TK is written off, what is the Deferred Ad expense?
mcqData.explanation: Written off = 3 TK/5 TK. Deferred = 2 TK/5 TK of 20,000 TK = 8,000 TK

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold + Gross Profit = ?
mcqData.explanation: This is the basic income statement formula for a trading concern.

mcqData.text: Paid 2 TK/3 TK of Factory Expense = TK 4,000 TK. Amount of Accrued Factory Expense?
mcqData.explanation: 2 TK/3 = 4kTK, so 1 TK/3 = 2kTK. Total = 6 TKkTK. Accrued (remaining 1 TK/3 TK) = 2,000 TK

mcqData.text: Profitless Sales is deducted from— i) Purchase ii) Closing. Stock iii) Sales
mcqData.explanation: Profitless sales (মুনাফাবিহীন বিক্রয়) must be deducted from both Net Sales and Net Purchase.

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a non-operating income?
mcqData.explanation: Sales revenue is the primary Operating Income of a business.

mcqData.text: Result will be Net Income when—
mcqData.explanation: Net Income occurs when total revenues exceed total expenses for the period.

mcqData.text: If installation cost is debited to the wages account, what will be the effect on the Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Installation cost is a capital Expense. If debited to Wages (Direct Expense), Cost of Goods Sold increases, causing Gross Profit (Gross Profit) to decrease.

mcqData.text: What is the effect of charging a 5 TK\%TK Bad Debt Provision on Accounts Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: Provision is an expense (Equity ↓) and it's deducted from Accounts Receivable (Assets ↓).

mcqData.text: Ad Expense 40 TKkTK, Free goods 5 TKkTK. Write off 1 TK/3 TK of total. Deferred Advertisement amount?
mcqData.explanation: Total Ad = 45 TKkTK. Written off = 15 TKkTK (1 TK/3 TK). Deferred = 30,000 TK (2 TK/3 TK).

mcqData.text: Result of free goods distribution— i) Gross Profit decreases ii) Gross Profit increases iii) NP decreases
mcqData.explanation: It reduces purchase (Gross Profit ↓) and increases ad expense (NP ↓).

mcqData.text: Profit is 25 TK\%TK on Sales. Cost of Goods Sold is TK 6,00,000 TK. Sales amount?
mcqData.explanation: If Profit is 25 TK\%TK on Sales, Cost of Goods Sold is 75 TK\%TK. Sales = 6,00,000 TK / 0.75 = 8,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q185: What is the amount of Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = Sales - Cost of Goods Sold = 8,00,000 TK - 6,00,000 TK = 2,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following does NOT affect the total sum of the Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: Collection from Accounts Receivable increases Cash and decreases Accounts Receivable by the same amount; total assets remain unchanged.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Current Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Unearned income (অগ্রিম আয়) is a liability until the service is provided.

mcqData.text: Salary paid 30 TKkTK, Accrued salary 20 TKkTK. Total Revenue Expense?
mcqData.explanation: According to the matching principle: Paid (30 TKkTK) + Accrued (20 TKkTK) = 50,000 TK

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 50 TKkTK, Purchase 1.5 TKLTK, Sales 2 TKLTK, Closing. Stock 80 TKkTK. Adjusted Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Adj. Purchase = Opening. Stock + Purchase - Closing. Stock = 50 TKk + 1.5L - 80k = 1,20,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q190: If Net Profit is 50 TKkTK, what is the Operating Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = Sales - Adj Pur = 2 TKL - 1.2L = 80kTK. Opening Expense = Gross Profit - NP = 80 TKk - 50k = 30,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Non-operating Income?
mcqData.explanation: Apprentice Premium (শিক্ষানবিশ সেলামি) is a non-core revenue item for most businesses.

mcqData.text: Capital 5 TKLTK, Loan 1 TKLTK, Accrued Interest 10 TKkTK, Accounts Payable 50 TKkTK, Unearned Rent 20 TKkTK. Current Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Current Liability = Accrued Interest (10 TKkTK) + Accounts Payable (50 TKkTK) + Unearned Rent (20 TKkTK) = 80,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q193: What is the Total Current Asset of the firm?
mcqData.explanation: Total Assets = Liability + Equity = (1 TKL + 80kTK) + 5 TKL = 6.8LTK. Current Assets = 6.8 TKL - 3LTK (Fixed) = 3,80,000 TK

mcqData.text: Salary paid for 10 TK months TK 1,20,000 TK. Amount of Accrued Salary?
mcqData.explanation: Per month = 1.2 TKL/10 = 12kTK. Due (2 TK months) = 24,000 TK

mcqData.text: Assets that can be converted to cash very quickly are called—
mcqData.explanation: Liquid assets (তরল সম্পদ) like cash or marketable securities are characterized by quick convertibility.

mcqData.text: Sales 2 TKLTK, Closing. Stock 30 TKkTK, Cost of Goods Sold 1.4 TKLTK, Opening. Expense is 50 TK\%TK of Cost of Goods Sold. Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = Sales - Cost of Goods Sold = 2,00,000 TK - 1,40,000 TK = 60,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q197: Result of Income Statement (NP is Loss of 10 TKkTK)? i) Capital ↓ 10 TKkTK ii) Drawing ↑ 10 TKkTK iii) Internal Liability ↓ 10 TKkTK
mcqData.explanation: NP = 60 TKkTK (Gross Profit) - 70 TKkTK (Opening Expense) = TK(10k)TK loss. Loss reduces Capital (i) and thus Internal Liability (iii).

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 40 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 60 TKkTK, Cost of Goods Sold 2,00,000 TK. Amount of Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase = Cost of Goods Sold + Closing. Stock - Opening. Stock = 2,00,000 TK + 60,000 TK - 40,000 TK = 2,20,000 TK

mcqData.text: Unearned Service Revenue is a/an—
mcqData.explanation: It represents an obligation to perform service in the future, making it a liability.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Non-operating Income?
mcqData.explanation: Interest on investment is a financial income not related to core business operations.

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold + Gross Profit = ?
mcqData.explanation: This is the fundamental equation for the trading part of the Income Statement.

mcqData.text: In which section of the Balance Sheet is 'Creditors' (Account Payable) shown?
mcqData.explanation: Creditors are short-term obligations usually payable within a year.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Indirect Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Audit fees are administrative expenses, while others are direct costs of purchasing/production.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 10 TKkTK, Purchase 1 TKLTK, Pur. Return 7.5 TKkTK, Pur. Discount 2.5 TKkTK, Wages 3 TKkTK, Excise Duty 12 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 25 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Net Pur = 90 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold = 10 TKkTK (Opening) + 90 TKkTK (Net Pur) + 3 TKkTK (Wages) + 12 TKkTK (Duty) - 25 TKkTK (Closing) = 90,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q205: Profit is 20 TK\%TK on Sales. What is the Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: 20 TK\%TK on Sales = 25 TK\%TK on Cost. Gross Profit = 90,000 TK \times 25\% = 22,500 TK

mcqData.text: To determine Net Purchase, which items are deducted from Purchase? i. Discount ii. Carriage Inward iii. Purchase Return
mcqData.explanation: Purchase returns and discounts are deducted. Carriage inward is an additional cost, not a deduction.

mcqData.text: Prepaid Salary in Trial Balance is 15 TKkTK. At year-end, 5 TKkTK is still prepaid. Salary expense in Income Statement?
mcqData.explanation: Expense = Total (15 TKkTK) - Remaining Prepaid (5 TKkTK) = 10,000 TK

mcqData.text: Gross Profit 50 TKkTK, Admin Expense 15 TKkTK, Selling Expense 5 TKkTK, Interest Income 2 TKkTK. Net Profit?
mcqData.explanation: NP = 50 TKkTK (Gross Profit) - 20 TKkTK (Opening Expense) + 2 TKkTK (Non-Opening Income) = 32,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q209: Impact of charging 2 TKkTK Bad Debt Provision? i. NP will be 30 TKkTK ii. Current Assets ↓ 2 TKkTK iii. Total Assets ↓ 2 TKkTK
mcqData.explanation: NP becomes 32 TKk - 2k = 30kTK. Accounts Receivable (Asset) decreases, so total asset also decreases.

mcqData.text: Insurance 30 TKkTK paid on 01-07-2020 for 4 years. Insurance expense for 2020?
mcqData.explanation: Annual = 30 TKk/4 = 7,500 TK. For 6 months (July-Dec) = 3,750 TK

mcqData.text: From Q211: If the full 30 TKkTK is shown as expense, impact? i. NP ↓ 22,500 TK ii. Total Assets ↓ 26,250 TK iii. Equity ↓ 26,250 TK
mcqData.explanation: Extra Expense = 30 TKk - 3.75k = 26,250 TK. This reduces both NP (Equity) and the Prepaid Asset by 26,250 TK

mcqData.text: Objective of preparing Balance Sheet— i. Know Income/Expense ii. Know Assets iii. Know Liabilities
mcqData.explanation: Balance sheet shows financial position (Assets & Liability). Income/Expense is the domain of the Income Statement.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 1 TKLTK, Purchase 1.5 TKLTK, Wages 2 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 97 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
From Q214: If 2 TKkTK salary was accrued— i. Gross Profit will decrease ii. NP will decrease iii. Total salary will be 5 TKkTK
mcqData.explanation: Salary is an indirect expense, so it affects NP, not Gross Profit. Total salary = 3 TKkTK (paid) + 2 TKkTK (due) = 5 TKkTK.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Fictitious/Imaginary Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Discount on shares has no real value and is a fictitious asset. Others are intangible/fixed assets.

mcqData.text: What type of asset is 'Goodwill'?
mcqData.explanation: Goodwill is an asset that lacks physical substance but has long-term value.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Fictitious Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Share discount (শেয়ার বাট্টা) is a classic example of an "Alik" or fictitious asset.

mcqData.text: Net Sales 3 TKLTK. Profit is 20 TK\%TK on Cost of Goods Sold. What is the Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: If Cost of Goods Sold is 100 TK\%TK, Sales is 120 TK\%TK. 3,00,000 TK / 1.2 = 2,50,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q219: Indirect Expense is 30 TK\%TK of Gross Profit. What is the Net Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = 3 TKL - 2.5L = 50kTK. Indirect Expense = 50 TKk \times 30\% = 15kTK. NP = 50 TKk - 15k = 35,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Current Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Unpaid/Accrued salary (অপ্রদত্ত বেতন) is a short-term obligation.

mcqData.text: Purchase including VAT 2,87,000 TK and Purchase Return 7,000 TK. Amount of Purchase VAT?
Required to prepare Balance Sheet— i. Assets ii. Liabilities iii. Capital
mcqData.explanation: The accounting equation TKA = Liability + ETK is the basis of the Balance Sheet.

mcqData.text:
Read the stimulus and answer the question: Business | Sales | Cost of Goods Sold | Operating Expenses | Gross Profit/Loss | Net Profit/Loss 'P' Traders | 80,700 TK | 50,500 TK | 10,000 TK | ? | ? 'Q' Traders | 55,000 TK | ? | 18,500 TK | 15,500 TK | ? Q. What is the amount of Gross Profit and Net Profit for 'P' Traders?

BusinessSalesCost of Goods SoldOperating ExpensesGross Profit/LossNet Profit/Loss
'P' Traders80,700 TK50,500 TK10,000 TK??
'Q' Traders55,000 TK?18,500 TK15,500 TK?

Read the stimulus and answer the question: Business | Sales | Cost of Goods Sold | Operating Expenses | Gross Profit/Loss | Net Profit/Loss 'P' Traders | 80,700 TK | 50,500 TK | 10,000 TK | ? | ? 'Q' Traders | 55,000 TK | ? | 18,500 TK | 15,500 TK | ? Q. What is the amount of Cost of Goods Sold and Net Profit/Loss for 'Q' Traders?

BusinessSalesCost of Goods SoldOperating ExpensesGross Profit/LossNet Profit/Loss
'P' Traders80,700 TK50,500 TK10,000 TK??
'Q' Traders55,000 TK?18,500 TK15,500 TK?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 55,000 TK - 15,500 TK = 39,500 TK. NP = 15,500 TK - 18,500 TK = (3,000 TK)TK Loss.

mcqData.text: Which statement is used to close direct expense accounts?
mcqData.explanation: Direct and indirect expenses are closed through the Income Statement (Trading/Profit and Loss account).

mcqData.text: If the value of Closing Stock is overstated—
mcqData.explanation: High Closing. Stock reduces Cost of Goods Sold (Income ↑) and increases Current Assets (Asset ↑).

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 30 TKkTK, Purchase 1.5 TKLTK, Sales 2 TKLTK, Profit 30 TK\%TK on Sales. Closing Stock?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 2 TKL - 60k = 1.4LTK. Closing. Stock = 30 TKk + 1.5L - 1.4L = 40,000 TK

mcqData.text: At what value is Accounts Receivable shown in the Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: It is shown at Net Realizable Value (after deducting bad debt provisions).

mcqData.text: Trial Balance: Prepaid Insurance 6 TKkTK. Policy taken on 01-01-2019 for 4 years. Insurance expense for 2020?
mcqData.explanation: Original cost = 8 TKkTK. (Since 3 TK years remaining at 2020 TK start = 6 TKkTK). Annual Expense = 2,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q230: If full 6 TKkTK is shown as expense, impact on Assets?
mcqData.explanation: Actual prepaid should be 4 TKkTK (6 TKk - 2kTK Expense). If 6 TKkTK is expensed, asset decreases by 4,000 TK

mcqData.text: On which accounting concept is the Balance Sheet based?
mcqData.explanation: Going concern (চলমান প্রতিষ্ঠান ধারণা) assumes the business will continue indefinitely, justifying Assets/Liabilities.

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold 5.4 TKLTK, Closing. Stock 35 TKkTK, Opening. Stock 25 TKkTK. Current year Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Purchase = Cost of Goods Sold + Closing. Stock - Opening. Stock = 5,40,000 TK + 35,000 TK - 25,000 TK = 5,50,000 TK

mcqData.text: Contingent Liability is placed in the Balance Sheet—
mcqData.explanation: Potential liabilities are not recognized in the table but mentioned in footnotes (দায় পার্শ্বে ফুটনোটে).

mcqData.text: Closing. Stock 65 TKkTK incl. fire loss 7 TKkTK (80 TK\%TK insured) and stationery 4 TKkTK. Actual Closing. Stock?
mcqData.explanation: Closing. Stock = 65,000 TK - 7,000 TK - 4,000 TK = 54,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q235: Impact on Income Statement? (Net Loss from Fire 20 TK\%TK)
mcqData.explanation: Fire loss (20 TK\%TK) = 1,400 TK. It is an expense that increases (ব্যয় বাড়বে ১,৪০০ TK টাকা).

mcqData.text: The reason for the decrease in business capital is—
mcqData.explanation: Increase in expenses reduces net profit, which in turn reduces owner's equity/capital.

mcqData.text: Opening. Provision 3.5 TKkTK. Accounts Receivable 1.75 TKLTK, New Bad Debt 15 TKkTK. Charge 12 TK\%TK New Provision. Total Expense?
mcqData.explanation: New Prov = TK(1.75L - 15k) \times 12\% = 19.2kTK. Expense = 15 TKk + 19.2k - 3.5k = 30,700 TK

mcqData.text: From Q238: Impact of Bad Debt adjustment? i. NP ↓ ii. Accounts Receivable ↓ iii. Equity ↓
mcqData.explanation: It reduces profit (i), book value of receivables (ii), and owner's capital (iii).

mcqData.text: Pur 50 TKkTK, Ship 6 TKkTK, Import 10 TKkTK, Sales 1 TKLTK, Return 3 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 15 TKkTK. Net Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Net Purchase = Purchase (50 TKkTK) - Return (3 TKkTK) = 47,000 TK

mcqData.text: Pur 50 TKkTK, Ship 6 TKkTK, Import 10 TKkTK, Sales 1 TKLTK, Return 3 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 15 TKkTK. Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Net Pur = 47 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold = 47 TKk + 6k + 10k - 15k = 48,000 TK. Gross Profit = 1,00,000 TK - 48,000 TK = 52,000 TK

mcqData.text: What is 'Interest on Capital' for a business?
mcqData.explanation: It is a financial expense paid by the business to the owner for using their capital.

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold 40,000 TK. Profit is 25 TK\%TK on Cost. Sales price?
mcqData.explanation: Sales = Cost + Profit = 40,000 TK + (40,000 TK \times 25\%) = 50,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Current Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Unearned income (অগ্রিম আয়) represents an obligation to serve, making it a current liability.

mcqData.text: Trial Balance Wages 18 TKkTK, Accrued 4 TKkTK. Incl. installation wages 2 TKkTK. Actual wage expense?
mcqData.explanation: Expense = 18 TKkTK (Trial Balance) + 4 TKkTK (Accrued) - 2 TKkTK (Installation) = 20,000 TK

mcqData.text: Examples of Current Assets— i. Accounts Receivable ii. Notes Receivable iii. Accrued Income
mcqData.explanation: All three are expected to be converted to cash or utilized within a year.

mcqData.text: Result of free goods distribution— i. Purchase ↑ ii. Purchase ↓ iii. Ad Expense ↑
mcqData.explanation: It reduces purchases (at cost) and increases advertising expenses.

mcqData.text: Trial Balance Ad 90 TKkTK, Free goods 10 TKkTK. Write off over 4 years. Deferred Ad amount?
mcqData.explanation: Total Ad = 100 TKkTK. Write off (1 TK/4 TK) = 25 TKkTK. Deferred (3 TK/4 TK) = 75,000 TK

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 50 TKkTK, Net Pur 1.5 TKLTK, Closing. Stock 30 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 50,000 TK + 1,50,000 TK - 30,000 TK = 1,70,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q249: If Closing. Stock market value is 20 TKkTK, impact on Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Conservative principle: value at lower (20 TKkTK). Cost of Goods Sold increases by 10 TKkTK, so Gross Profit decreases by 10,000 TK

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold 2.5 TKLTK, Opening. Expense 1 TKLTK, Apprentice Prem 30 TKkTK, Interest. on Capital 20 TKkTK. If Gross Profit is 1.5 TKLTK, Sales?
mcqData.explanation: Sales = Cost of Goods Sold + Gross Profit = 2,50,000 TK + 1,50,000 TK = 4,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q251: If Gross Profit is 2 TKLTK, what is the Net Profit?
mcqData.explanation: NP = 2 TKLTK (Gross Profit) - 1 TKLTK (Opening Expense) + 30 TKkTK (Non-Opening Income) - 20 TKkTK (Non-Opening Expense) = 1,10,000 TK

mcqData.text: Non-operating expenses are— i. Insurance ii. Interest. on Loan iii. Bank Charge
mcqData.explanation: Insurance is an operating expense. Loan interest and bank charges are financial (non-operating) expenses.

mcqData.text: Paid Rent (2 TK/3 TK portion) 8,00,000 TK. Amount of Accrued Rent?
mcqData.explanation: 2 TK/3 = 8kTK. Total = 12 TKkTK. Accrued (1 TK/3 TK) = 4,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Intangible Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Trademarks have value but no physical substance.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is included in Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Ship rent (জাহাজ ভাড়া) is a direct cost related to purchase/import.

mcqData.text: What kind of liability is 'Accounts Payable'?
mcqData.explanation: It is a short-term obligation to suppliers.

mcqData.text: Gross Profit is 7.5 TK\%TK of Sales. If Gross Profit is 15,000 TK, what is the Sales price?
Which of the following is a Fictitious Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Share discount (শেয়ার অবহার) is a fictitious asset with no realizable value.

mcqData.text: From the Balance Sheet, we learn about— i. Fixed & Current Assets ii. Short & Long-term Liability iii. Owner's Capital
mcqData.explanation: The Balance Sheet presents the full financial position of the business at a specific date.

mcqData.text: Net Sales 12 TKLTK, Cost of Goods Sold 7.5 TKLTK, Operating Expense (20% of Sales) + 10 TKkTK, Non-operating Income 20 TKkTK. Net Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = 4.5 TKLTK. Opening Expense = 2.4 TKL + 10k = 2.5LTK. NP = 4.5 TKL - 2.5L + 20kTK (Non-Opening Income) = 2,20,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q261: If Sales increase by 10% and Cost of Goods Sold by 20%, what will be the Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: New Sales = 13.2 TKLTK. New Cost of Goods Sold = 7.5 TKL \times 1.2 = 9LTK. Gross Profit = 13.2 TKL - 9L = 4,20,000 TK

mcqData.text: What type of item is 'Bad Debt'?
mcqData.explanation: Bad debt (অনাদায়ী পাওনা) is considered a part of regular selling and administrative expenses.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Indirect Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Audit fees are administrative (indirect) costs. Others are related to purchase (direct).

mcqData.text: Capital 1.2 TKLTK, Gen. Reserve 30 TKkTK, Loan 20 TKkTK, Creditors 45 TKkTK. What is the External Liability?
mcqData.explanation: External Liability = Loan (20 TKkTK) + Creditors (45 TKkTK) = 65,000 TK (Reserve/Capital are internal).

mcqData.text: Unearned Revenue 50 TKkTK. If 2 TK/5 TK is earned, what is the Current Liability at year-end?
mcqData.explanation: Earned = 20 TKkTK. Remaining liability = 50 TKk - 20k = 30,000 TK

mcqData.text: Prepaid Insurance 2 TKkTK. If it expires, what's the impact on Financial Statements? i. Prepaid ↓ ii. Current Asset ↓ iii. Ins. Expense ↓
mcqData.explanation: Expired insurance becomes an expense. This reduces the prepaid asset and total current assets.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 50 TKkTK, Net Pur 1.25 TKLTK, Closing. Stock 15 TKkTK. Amount of Adjusted Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Adj. Pur = Opening. Stock + Net Pur - Closing. Stock = 50 TKk + 1.25L - 15k = 1,60,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q268: If Closing. Stock market value is 10 TKkTK instead of 15 TKkTK, impact on Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Per conservatism, Closing. stock decreases by 5 TKkTK. This increases Cost of Goods Sold and reduces Gross Profit by 5,000 TK

mcqData.text: Credit purchase 2 TKLTK, Trade Discount 10%, VAT 15%. Payable VAT amount?
mcqData.explanation: Pur after discount = 1.8 TKLTK. VAT = 1.8 TKL \times 15\% = 27,000 TK

mcqData.text: Components of Financial Statements (IAS-01) are— i. IS & OE ii. BS & CF iii. Notes
mcqData.explanation: These are the 5 standard parts of financial reporting under IAS-1.

mcqData.text: Non-operating expenses are— i. Interest. on Invest (Income) ii. Interest. on Loan iii. Bank Charge
mcqData.explanation: Interest on investment is income. Interest on loan and bank charges are expenses.

mcqData.text: Pur 2.8 TKLTK, Opening. Stock 10 TKkTK, Cost of Goods Sold 2.5 TKLTK, Gross Profit 40 TKkTK. Closing Stock?
mcqData.explanation: Closing. Stock = Opening. Stock + Pur - Cost of Goods Sold = 10 TKk + 2.8L - 2.5L = 40,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q273: What is the amount of Sales?
mcqData.explanation: Sales = Cost of Goods Sold + Gross Profit = 2,50,000 TK + 40,000 TK = 2,90,000 TK

mcqData.text: According to which IAS is the Financial Statement prepared?
mcqData.explanation: IAS-1 governs the presentation of financial statements.

mcqData.text: Sales 2.5 TKLTK, Pur 1.32 TKLTK, Carriage In 5 TKkTK, Admin Expense 25 TKkTK, Unsettled claim 10 TKkTK. Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 1.32 TKL + 5k = 1.37LTK. Gross Profit = 2.5 TKL - 1.37L = 1,13,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q276: Impact of the pending 10 TKkTK claim on the Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: Unsettled claims are "Contingent Liabilities" and are only shown in footnotes, not the main table.

mcqData.text: Opening. Capital 1.5 TKLTK, Drawing 10 TKkTK, Cost of Goods Sold 1.5 TKLTK, Sales 2.5 TKLTK, NP 60 TKkTK. Owner's Equity?
mcqData.explanation: OE = Opening. Capital + NP - Drawing = 1.5 TKL + 60k - 10k = 2,00,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q278: If Cost of Goods Sold was 10% less, what would be the impact? i. NP ↑ ii. NP ↓ iii. OE ↑
mcqData.explanation: Lower Cost of Goods Sold increases Net Profit, which directly increases Owner's Equity.

mcqData.text: Machine 60 TKkTK, Carriage 5 TKkTK, Installation 3 TKkTK. Depreciation 10%. Sold for 72 TKkTK. Revenue Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Total Cost = 68 TKkTK. Depreciation (10%) = 6,800 TK (Depreciation is a revenue expense).

mcqData.text: Mr. Dalton purchased a machine for 60,000 TK. Transport and installation costs were 5,000 TK and 3,000 TK respectively. At the end of the year, 10% depreciation was charged. Due to a defect, the machine was sold for 72,000 TK. Q: What is the amount of Revenue Expenditure?
mcqData.explanation: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) ensures consistency and comparability.

mcqData.text: Cost of Goods Sold 1,00,000 TK. Profit is 20 TK\%TK on Sales. What is the Sales amount?
mcqData.explanation: 20 TK\%TK on Sales = 25 TK\%TK on Cost. Sales = 1,00,000 TK \times 1.25 = 1,25,000 TK

mcqData.text: Ad Expense 40 TKkTK, Free goods 10 TKkTK. To be written off over 4 years. Current year's expense?
mcqData.explanation: Total Ad = 50,000 TK. Expense = 50,000 TK / 4 = 12,500 TK

mcqData.text: From Q283: Amount of Deferred Advertisement at the end of the current year?
mcqData.explanation: Deferred = Total (50 TKkTK) - Expense (12.5 TKkTK) = 37,500 TK

mcqData.text: Trial Balance Stationery 7 TKkTK, Opening. Stock incl. 1 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 2 TKkTK. Stationery expense?
mcqData.explanation: Expense = 7,000 TK (Trial Balance) - 1,000 TK (Already in Opening. Stock) - 1,000 TK (Used from Closing. Stock) → actual used = 1 TKk + 7k - 2k = 6,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q285: Unused stationery in the Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: Unused/Closing stationery is an asset. Here it is 2,000 TK

mcqData.text: Salary in Trial Balance is 14,000 TK. Monthly salary is 1,500 TK. How much is Salaries Payable in the Balance Sheet?
mcqData.explanation: Annual Salary should be (1,500 TK \times 12 TK) = 18,000 TK. Paid is 14,000 TK. Payable = 18,000 TK - 14,000 TK = 4,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a purchase-related expense?
mcqData.explanation: Carriage Inward (Purchase Transportation) is a direct expense added to the cost of purchases.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 33.5 TKkTK, Pur 60 TKkTK, Return 3 TKkTK, Disc 2 TKkTK. What is Net Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Net Pur = Purchase (60 TKkTK) - Return (3 TKkTK) - Disc (2 TKkTK) = 55,000 TK

mcqData.text: Not included in Cost of Goods Sold— i. Machine Carriage ii. Office Expense iii. Wage Expense
mcqData.explanation: Machine carriage is capital Expense; Office Expense is indirect. Wages are direct (included).

mcqData.text: If profit is 20 TK\%TK on Sales, what is the rate on Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Formula: TKRate / (100 - Rate) = 20/80 = 25\%TK.

mcqData.text: Asset 5% commission must be paid on Net Profit before charging commission (Net Profit is 45,250 TK). Amount of commission?
mcqData.explanation: Commission = 45,250 TK \times 5\% = 2,262.5 TK \approx 2,263 TK.

mcqData.text: If unrecorded bad debts in 2015 are TK 4,000 TK (from a base of 60,000 TK debtors) and the policy is to maintain a 5% Bad Debt Provision and a 2% Discount Provision, what is the Provision for Discount?
mcqData.explanation: Provision for discount is calculated on "Good Debtors" (Net Debtors after deducting both new bad debts and the new bad debt provision). TK(56,000 TK - 2,800 TK) \times 2\% = 1,064 TK.

mcqData.text: Deferred Advertisement is a/an—
mcqData.explanation: It is a fictitious/imaginary asset (ভুয়া সম্পদ) as it has no realizable value.

mcqData.text: Building 50 TKkTK, Furniture 20 TKkTK, Accounts Receivable 30 TKkTK, Stock 20 TKkTK, Cash 10 TKkTK, Accounts Payable 40 TKkTK. Current Assets?
mcqData.explanation: Current Assets = Accounts Receivable (30 TKkTK) + Stock (20 TKkTK) + Cash (10 TKkTK) = 60,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q291: If 5,000 TK is paid to Accounts Payable, impact on Capital at year-end?
mcqData.explanation: Paying a liability reduces Cash (Asset) and Accounts Payable (Liability); it does not affect Equity/Capital.

mcqData.text: Which is NOT a component of Financial Statements?
mcqData.explanation: Retained Earnings is part of 2nd paper (Company); standard 1st paper uses OE Statement.

mcqData.text: If profit is 20 TK\%TK on Sales, what is the rate on Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: (Same logic as Q289) 20 TK/80 = 1/4 = 25\%TK.

mcqData.text: Which is included in Cost of Goods Sold calculation?
mcqData.explanation: Import duty (আমদানি শুল্ক) is a direct purchase cost.

mcqData.text: Which accounts are affected by Accrued Expenses?
mcqData.explanation: It increases an expense (Income Statement) and creates a liability (Balance Sheet).

mcqData.text: How is Prepaid Expense considered for a business?
mcqData.explanation: Prepaid expenses provide future benefits, hence they are assets.

mcqData.text: Adjusted in the Income Statement— i. Addl. Capital ii. Product Drawing iii. Bad Debt
mcqData.explanation: Capital is a BS item. Product drawing (Purchase ↓) and Bad debt (Expense ↑) are in IS.

mcqData.text: Which is prepared using Capital items of a business?
mcqData.explanation: Balance Sheet (আর্থিক অবস্থার বিবরণী) shows fixed assets and long-term liabilities.

mcqData.text: Product distribution among poor for ad. How do you evaluate this?
mcqData.explanation: It serves both as a promotion (Advertisement) and a social contribution.

mcqData.text: Profitless sales of 2,00,000 TK will be included in the Income Statement as— i. Deducted from Purchase ii. Deducted as Expense iii. Deducted from Sales
mcqData.explanation: Profitless sales reduce both total Sales and total Purchases by the same amount (at cost).

mcqData.text: Which of the following CANNOT be claimed by the owner?
mcqData.explanation: Drawings (উত্তোলন) are what the owner takes from the business, not what they claim as a right.

mcqData.text: Sales 2,80,000 TK. Profit is 25 TK\%TK on Cost of Goods Sold. Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: 25 TK\%TK on Cost = 20 TK\%TK on Sales. Gross Profit = 2,80,000 TK \times 20\% = 56,000 TK

mcqData.text: Reasons for decrease in business capital— i. Increase in Opening. Expense ii. Net loss in current year iii. Increase in sales
mcqData.explanation: Expenses and losses reduce equity. Increased sales generally increase capital through profit.

mcqData.text: Which of the following shows the financial position of a firm?
mcqData.explanation: The Balance Sheet (আর্থিক অবস্থার বিবরণী) reflects assets, liabilities, and equity at a point in time.

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 15 TKkTK, Pur 1 TKLTK, Return 7.5 TKkTK, Disc 2.5 TKkTK, Wages 3 TKkTK, Duty 12 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 25 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Net Pur = 90 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold = 15 TKk + 90k + 3k + 12k - 25k = 95,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q306: Profit is 20 TK\%TK on Sales. Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: 20 TK\%TK on Sales = 25 TK\%TK on Cost. Gross Profit = 95,000 TK \times 25\% = 23,750 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is an Internal Liability?
mcqData.explanation: Capital (মূলধন) is a liability of the business toward the owner.

mcqData.text: Jan 1, 2015: Assets 2.2 TKLTK, Liability 90 TKkTK. Dec 31, 2015: Assets 2.7 TKLTK, Liability 1 TKLTK. Opening. Capital?
mcqData.explanation: Opening. Capital = Opening. Assets - Opening. Liability = 2,20,000 TK - 90,000 TK = 1,30,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q309: Profit for the year 2015?
mcqData.explanation: Closing. Capital = 2.7 TKL - 1L = 1.7LTK. Profit = 1.7 TKL - 1.3L = 40,000 TK

mcqData.text: According to IAS-01, how many components are in a set of financial statements?
mcqData.explanation: The five components are: IS, OE, BS, Cash Flow, and Notes.

mcqData.text: Operating expenses are— i. Purchase ii. Advertisement iii. Salary
mcqData.explanation: Purchase is a part of Cost of Goods Sold (Direct Expense). Ad and Salary are operating/indirect expenses.

mcqData.text: 10 months' Salary 12,000 TK. Amount of Accrued Salary?
mcqData.explanation: Monthly = 1,200 TK. Accrued (2 months) = 2,400 TK

mcqData.text: If machinery purchase is recorded as goods purchase, impact on IS?
mcqData.explanation: Goods purchase is part of Cost of Goods Sold. Increased Cost of Goods Sold reduces Gross Profit.

mcqData.text: Accounts Receivable 60 TKkTK, Bad debt 2 TKkTK. Create 5 TK\%TK Bad Debt Provision. Provision amount?
mcqData.explanation: Provision = TK(60,000 TK - 2,000 TK) \times 5\% = 2,900 TK

mcqData.text: From Q315: If unrecorded bad debt is 4 TKkTK, what is the 2 TK\%TK Discount Provision?
mcqData.explanation: Net Accounts Receivable = TK(60k - 4k) = 56kTK. Prov = 56 TKk \times 5\% = 2.8kTK. Disc Prov = TK(56k - 2.8k) \times 2\% = 1,064 TK (Closest option check logic)

mcqData.text: Fixed assets are— i. Land & Building ii. Machinery & Furniture iii. Investment & Bank
mcqData.explanation: Investments are separate; Bank balance is a current asset.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is NOT a Liquid Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Liquid Assets = Current Assets - (Stock + Prepaid Expense). So, Stock/Inventory is not liquid.

mcqData.text: What type of asset is Accounts Receivable?
mcqData.explanation: Accounts Receivable is a Current Asset as it represents money owed to the business that will be collected shortly.

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Fictitious (Dummy) Asset?
mcqData.explanation: Preliminary expenses are costs that provide no real value but are carried on the balance sheet to be written off over time.

mcqData.text: Sales 25 TKkTK, Opening. Stock 2.5 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 1.5 TKkTK, Pur 12 TKkTK, Carriage In 1 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = Opening. Stock (2.5 TKkTK) + Net Pur (12 TKkTK) + Carriage (1 TKkTK) - Closing. Stock (1.5 TKkTK) = 14,000 TK

mcqData.text: Which of the following is a Direct Expense?
mcqData.explanation: Duty (শুল্ক) is related to goods purchase/import, making it a direct cost.

mcqData.text: Advertisement expense is a—
mcqData.explanation: Advertisement is incurred to promote and sell products (বাজারজাতকরণ খরচ).

mcqData.text: If 3 TK/5 TK of 18,000 TK Ad Expense is written off, what is the Deferred Ad amount?
mcqData.explanation: Written off = 10.8 TKkTK. Deferred (2 TK/5 TK) = 18,000 TK - 10,800 TK = 7,200 TK

mcqData.text: Opening. Stock 15 TKkTK, Pur 45 TKkTK, Wages 12 TKkTK, Factory Rent 3 TKkTK, Closing. Stock 22 TKkTK. Cost of Goods Sold?
mcqData.explanation: Cost of Goods Sold = 15 TKk + 45k + 12k + 3k - 22k = 53,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q325: What is the amount of Adjusted Purchase?
mcqData.explanation: Adjusted Pur = Opening. Stock + Purchase - Closing. Stock = 15 TKk + 45k - 22k = 38,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q325: If sales is 70,000 TK, what is the Gross Profit?
mcqData.explanation: Gross Profit = Sales (70,000 TK) - Cost of Goods Sold (53,000 TK) = 17,000 TK

mcqData.text: Ins. Expense 4,000 TK paid for 4 years (initially as Expense). Prepaid Ins at year-end?
mcqData.explanation: Current yr Expense = 1 TKkTK. Prepaid (remaining 3 yrs) = 3,000 TK

mcqData.text: Rental Income 20 TKkTK, Salary 8 TKkTK (accrued), Ins Expense 1 TKkTK, Unearned Svc Income 2 TKkTK. Net Income?
mcqData.explanation: NP = Income (20 TKk + 1kTK service) - Expense (8 TKkTK salary + 1 TKkTK insurance) = 12,000 TK (Check calc logic).

mcqData.text: Stock: Cost 37 TKkTK, Market 35 TKkTK. Incl fire loss 5 TKkTK. Value per Conservatism?
mcqData.explanation: Lower is 35 TKkTK. Deduct fire loss 5 TKkTK. Actual Stock = 30,000 TK

mcqData.text: From Q330: If Insurance covers 40% of fire loss, Current Assets amount?
mcqData.explanation: Assets = Stock (30 TKkTK) + Ins. Claim (5 TKk \times 40\% = 2kTK) = 32,000 TK

mcqData.text: Free goods distribution 10,000 TK is shown in IS— i. Deduct Pur ii. Deduct Sales iii. Add to Ad
mcqData.explanation: It reduces purchase and increases advertising expense.

mcqData.text: Provision to be reduced by 2,000 TK. Trial Balance Provision 6,000 TK. New Provision?
If profit is 25 TK\%TK on Sales, what is the rate on Cost?
To find Net Purchase, deduct— i. Pur Disc ii. Carriage In iii. Pur Return
mcqData.explanation: Discount and returns are deducted; carriage is added.

mcqData.text: Closing. Stock 45 TKkTK incl. fire loss 5 TKkTK and stationery 6 TKkTK. Actual value?
Impact of fire loss of 5,000 TK on financial position?
